rows.title: Kindergarten and elementary school teachers
rows.old_rationale: AI will significantly automate back-end knowledge work such as lesson planning, grading, and administrative communication, which currently occupy a large portion of a teacher's time. However, the core of the role involves physical supervision, emotional support, and real-time behavioral management of young children, which creates a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While lesson planning and grading have high digital potential, the core value of the occupation is the real-time, physical, and emotional management of children in an inherently unpredictable classroom environment. The necessity for human presence, moral authority, and the legal/ethical accountability for the development and safety of minors makes this role highly resistant to agentic replacement.

rows.title: High school teachers
rows.old_rationale: High school teachers are heavily exposed because core digital tasks like lesson planning, content creation, and grading are highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the physical requirement of classroom management and interpersonal mentorship provides a buffer, AI's ability to provide personalized tutoring and automated feedback significantly reshapes the instructional delivery and administrative workload of the profession.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with lesson planning and grading, the core value of teaching is the real-time, in-person facilitation of human development, which requires navigating the highly unpredictable social and emotional dynamics of a classroom. Because the primary product is the human-to-human bond and the mentorship of students, the occupation remains fundamentally resistant to agentic replacement despite the high cognitive complexity of the role.

rows.title: Middle school teachers
rows.old_rationale: Middle school teachers have high exposure because a significant portion of their workload—lesson planning, grading, and content creation—is digital and highly susceptible to AI automation. However, the core of the job requires physical presence for classroom management, emotional support for adolescents, and real-time interpersonal interaction, which provides a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While lesson planning and grading have high digital potential, the core value of middle school teaching is the real-time, in-person social and emotional development of adolescents, which cannot be delegated to software. The classroom environment is highly unpredictable, requiring constant, high-stakes human judgment and moral authority that remains fundamentally tied to the human-to-human bond.

rows.title: Probation officers and correctional treatment specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of high-exposure digital tasks (writing case reports, risk assessment, and data-driven monitoring) and low-exposure physical/interpersonal tasks (home visits, drug testing, and building trust with hostile clients). While AI can significantly automate documentation and predictive risk modeling, the legal requirement for human testimony and the physical necessity of community supervision provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While reporting and data management are digital, the core value of this role is physical supervision, in-person assessment of volatile environments, and the exercise of human authority in high-stakes legal contexts. The necessity for human presence to build trust, manage physical safety, and accept legal liability for life-altering decisions makes this role highly resistant to full agentic automation.

rows.title: Nuclear engineers
rows.old_rationale: Nuclear engineering is a high-level knowledge profession where core tasks—such as reactor design, dose calculations, and safety simulations—are performed using digital tools and complex mathematical modeling. While the high-stakes regulatory environment and the need for physical inspections at power plants provide a buffer, AI is rapidly advancing in its ability to optimize designs, analyze sensor data for predictive maintenance, and automate technical documentation.
rows.new_rationale: While nuclear engineers utilize digital modeling and simulation, their core output involves physical safety, regulatory compliance, and the management of high-stakes, volatile nuclear environments where AI cannot physically intervene or assume liability. The necessity for human judgment in managing catastrophic risk and the legal requirement for professional accountability ensure that the human role remains central despite the high degree of technical complexity.

rows.title: Emergency management directors
rows.old_rationale: The role involves significant digital knowledge work, such as analyzing hazards, drafting complex response plans, and managing federal funding applications, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core of the job requires high-stakes real-time decision-making, physical presence at command centers during disasters, and intense interpersonal coordination with government officials and the public, providing a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While planning and data analysis can be digitized, the core value of this role lies in high-stakes, real-time leadership during chaotic, unpredictable disasters where human judgment and moral authority are required to make life-or-death decisions. The role is fundamentally anchored in physical-world coordination and legal/ethical accountability that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent, as the director must navigate complex human dynamics and political realities during crises.

rows.title: Lawyers
rows.old_rationale: Lawyers perform high-level knowledge work that is almost entirely digital, involving document drafting, legal research, and complex data analysis—all areas where LLMs excel. While courtroom advocacy and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation provide a protective barrier, the vast majority of billable hours are spent on tasks that AI can now significantly accelerate or automate, leading to major structural changes in the profession.
rows.new_rationale: While a significant portion of legal drafting and research is increasingly digital and automatable, the core value of the profession remains rooted in high-stakes advocacy, strategic negotiation, and the exercise of moral and legal judgment that requires human presence. The necessity for human accountability in courtrooms and the requirement for trust-based, empathetic client counseling create a high barrier to full agentic replacement, despite the high potential for software to handle the underlying document-based workflows.

rows.title: Physicians and surgeons
rows.old_rationale: Physicians and surgeons have moderate exposure because while AI can significantly automate digital tasks like diagnostic image interpretation (radiology), medical history analysis, and treatment planning, the core of the job requires physical presence, manual dexterity for procedures, and complex interpersonal communication. The high-stakes nature of medical liability and the need for human empathy in patient care provide a natural barrier to full automation, though AI will drastically increase productivity in information-heavy specialties.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with diagnostic synthesis, the core output of a physician or surgeon is a physical intervention on a biological system, which cannot be delegated to software. The high scores in environmental unpredictability and ontological necessity reflect the reality that medical practice involves navigating complex, non-standardized biological variables and the fundamental requirement for human trust, empathy, and legal accountability in life-or-death decision-making.

rows.title: Special education teachers
rows.old_rationale: AI will significantly streamline the digital and administrative aspects of the role, such as drafting Individualized Education Programs (IEPs), adapting lesson plans, and tracking student data. However, the core of the job requires intense physical presence, emotional intelligence, and real-time behavioral management for students with complex disabilities, which AI cannot replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with administrative tasks like IEP documentation, the core value of the role is the real-time, physical, and emotional intervention required to support students with diverse disabilities in unstructured classroom environments. The necessity for human presence, moral authority, and legal accountability in managing the development of vulnerable individuals makes this role highly resistant to agentic replacement.

rows.title: Construction managers
rows.old_rationale: Construction management is a hybrid role that combines digital knowledge work—such as cost estimation, scheduling, and contract analysis—with essential physical presence. While AI can significantly automate budgeting and logistical planning, the core of the job requires real-world site inspections, physical problem-solving, and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation with tradesworkers and inspectors that cannot be replicated digitally.
rows.new_rationale: While construction managers use digital tools for planning, their core value is the physical orchestration of complex, high-stakes, and highly unpredictable construction sites where software cannot physically intervene. The role requires deep human judgment to manage safety, legal liability, and the coordination of diverse human teams in volatile environments, making it highly resistant to full agentic replacement.

rows.title: Substance abuse, behavioral disorder, and mental health counselors
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a mix of high-level knowledge work and intense interpersonal interaction. While AI can significantly assist with digital tasks like documentation, treatment planning, and monitoring progress, the core of the job requires human empathy, crisis intervention, and the navigation of complex emotional and physical environments that AI cannot yet replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with documentation and data-driven treatment planning, the core value of this role is the therapeutic alliance and the human-to-human connection, which cannot be delegated to software. The work environment is highly unpredictable, involving crisis intervention and volatile human behavior, requiring a level of moral and legal accountability for life-altering decisions that remains strictly human-centric.

rows.title: Nurse anesthetists, nurse midwives, and nurse practitioners
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of high-level knowledge work, such as diagnosing conditions and analyzing test results, which is highly susceptible to AI assistance. However, the core of the job requires physical presence for exams, procedures (like delivering babies or administering anesthesia), and complex interpersonal communication that provides a strong buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical, in-person clinical intervention and patient care, which cannot be digitized or executed by software agents. While the cognitive synthesis required for diagnosis and treatment planning is high, the work is fundamentally anchored in high-stakes, unpredictable physical environments where human presence, physical touch, and legal/ethical accountability for life-altering decisions are non-delegatable.

rows.title: Civil engineers
rows.old_rationale: Civil engineering involves significant digital knowledge work, including CAD design, data analysis, and cost estimation, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. However, the role is anchored by the necessity for physical site inspections, real-world surveying, and the legal accountability of a Professional Engineer (PE) license, which requires human judgment and liability that AI cannot currently replace.
rows.new_rationale: While civil engineers use digital design tools, their core output is the physical integrity of infrastructure in highly variable, unstructured environments where AI cannot yet navigate or validate real-world physical constraints. The high systemic accountability score reflects the non-delegatable legal and ethical liability for public safety, which requires a human professional to sign off on designs and construction outcomes.

rows.title: Elementary, middle, and high school principals
rows.old_rationale: Principals perform significant knowledge work—such as curriculum development, budget management, and data analysis of student performance—that is highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. However, the core of the role requires physical presence for school safety, real-time crisis management, and high-stakes interpersonal interactions with students, parents, and staff that AI cannot currently replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While principals manage data and reports, their primary value is the physical, real-time leadership of a school community, which cannot be delegated to software. The role requires navigating highly unpredictable human dynamics and carries profound legal and ethical accountability for the safety and development of minors that necessitates a human presence.

rows.title: Mechanical engineers
rows.old_rationale: Mechanical engineering is heavily reliant on digital tools like CAD, simulations, and data analysis, all of which are being rapidly enhanced by AI to automate complex design iterations and predictive modeling. While the role requires physical prototyping, site visits, and hardware testing, the core intellectual work of designing and analyzing mechanical systems is increasingly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.
rows.new_rationale: While mechanical engineers use digital tools for design and simulation, their core output is the physical realization of machines that must function reliably in the real world, which cannot be fully delegated to software. The high complexity of integrating thermal, mechanical, and electronic systems, combined with the severe legal and safety liabilities (PE licensure) associated with physical infrastructure and machinery, ensures that human oversight remains critical for final validation and accountability.

rows.title: Bakers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of a baker are physical and sensory, involving manual dexterity for shaping dough and real-time monitoring of physical products in high-heat environments. While AI can optimize recipes or assist with inventory management and scheduling, the fundamental work of kneading, decorating, and handling equipment remains a physical task resistant to digital automation.
rows.new_rationale: Baking is fundamentally a physical, material-transformation process that requires manual dexterity and real-time sensory feedback, making it highly resistant to digital agentic execution. While recipes and processes can be automated in industrial settings, the core value of the occupation—especially in retail and artisanal contexts—relies on physical manipulation of ingredients and the management of a controlled, yet physically demanding, kitchen environment.

rows.title: Industrial production managers
rows.old_rationale: This role is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical oversight. AI will significantly enhance the analytical aspects of the job, such as production scheduling, budget optimization, and predictive maintenance data analysis, but the core requirements of physical plant presence, personnel management, and real-time problem-solving in a manual environment provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While production data analysis can be automated, the core value of this role lies in managing physical workflows, human labor, and unpredictable supply chain or equipment failures in volatile plant environments. The necessity for human leadership, on-site crisis management, and legal accountability for safety and operational outcomes makes full agentic replacement impossible.

rows.title: Social and community service managers
rows.old_rationale: This role involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as grant writing, data analysis, and budget management, which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core responsibilities of community stakeholder engagement, staff supervision, and navigating complex human crises provide a buffer that requires physical presence and high-level emotional intelligence.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like reporting and budgeting have high digital potential, the core value of this role lies in human-centric coordination, stakeholder negotiation, and crisis management in volatile social environments. The necessity for human empathy, ethical judgment in high-stakes social interventions, and the legal accountability for vulnerable populations makes this role highly resistant to full agentic replacement.

rows.title: Architectural and engineering managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly knowledge-based and digital, involving project planning, budgeting, and technical oversight that can be significantly enhanced by AI tools. While the role requires high-level human judgment, leadership, and physical site visits, the core tasks of analyzing technical data, scheduling, and coordinating complex engineering workflows are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.
rows.new_rationale: While these managers oversee digital design and planning, their core value lies in managing complex, high-stakes physical projects where the 'output' is a real-world structure or product that must survive in unpredictable environments. The role requires high-level human judgment for team leadership, conflict resolution, and accepting ultimate legal and ethical liability for safety-critical infrastructure, which cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Psychologists
rows.old_rationale: Psychology is a knowledge-based profession where core tasks like analyzing data, writing reports, and researching are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. However, the critical requirements for human empathy, ethical judgment, and real-time interpersonal observation in clinical and school settings create a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in data analysis and diagnostic pattern recognition, the core value of psychology is rooted in the therapeutic alliance, empathy, and the ethical responsibility of human-to-human connection, which cannot be delegated to software. The work involves navigating the highly unpredictable and nuanced emotional states of human clients, requiring a level of moral and professional accountability that remains fundamentally human.

rows.title: Producers and directors
rows.old_rationale: Producers and directors are highly exposed because their core work products—scripts, visual aesthetics, and edited video—are increasingly being generated or enhanced by AI tools. While the role requires significant human leadership and physical presence on set, the rapid advancement of generative AI in pre-production (scripting/storyboarding) and post-production (editing/VFX) allows a single director to do the work of a much larger team, potentially consolidating the industry.
rows.new_rationale: While the final product (film/media) is digital, the core value of the role lies in the high-stakes, real-world coordination of human talent, physical sets, and volatile on-location environments that AI cannot yet navigate. The job requires deep creative synthesis and the ability to manage complex human dynamics, making the 'human-in-the-loop' element essential for both the artistic vision and the liability of large-scale production management.

rows.title: Medical scientists
rows.old_rationale: Medical scientists perform high-level knowledge work including data analysis, grant writing, and literature reviews, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. While physical laboratory work and clinical trials provide a buffer, the core of the profession is shifting toward computational biology and AI-driven drug discovery, significantly increasing productivity and reshaping research methodologies.
rows.new_rationale: While medical scientists produce digital data and research papers, the core value is tied to physical laboratory experimentation and clinical trials that require physical manipulation and human oversight. The high complexity and accountability scores reflect the life-altering nature of medical breakthroughs and the ethical, non-delegatable responsibility for patient safety and scientific integrity in high-stakes research environments.

rows.title: Airline and commercial pilots
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of high-level digital information processing and critical physical presence. While AI and advanced automation can handle navigation, flight planning, and system monitoring, the role requires real-time human intervention for physical emergencies, complex sensory awareness in unpredictable weather, and the legal/ethical responsibility of passenger safety in the physical world.
rows.new_rationale: While flight systems are increasingly automated, the core output is the physical transport of passengers and cargo in a high-stakes, volatile 3D environment where AI cannot yet manage unforeseen physical emergencies or unstructured hazards. The requirement for human presence is reinforced by extreme systemic accountability; the pilot remains the final legal and ethical authority responsible for life-critical decisions that cannot be delegated to software.

rows.title: Architects
rows.old_rationale: Architects perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly digital, utilizing CADD and BIM software which is highly susceptible to AI-driven automation in generative design and technical documentation. While the role requires physical site visits and complex client negotiations, a significant portion of the core work—drafting, specification writing, and regulatory compliance—is moving toward high AI integration.
rows.new_rationale: While BIM and AI can generate structural designs, the architect's role is fundamentally tied to physical site constraints, regulatory navigation, and the high-stakes liability of public safety. The necessity for human judgment in reconciling client vision with unpredictable physical site variables and the legal requirement for a licensed professional to 'sign and seal' documents creates a significant barrier to full agentic automation.

rows.title: Drywall installers, ceiling tile installers, and tapers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of this occupation are entirely physical, involving manual labor, dexterity, and movement in unpredictable construction environments. While AI might assist with peripheral administrative tasks or material estimation, it cannot perform the physical installation, cutting, or finishing of drywall and ceiling tiles.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor—the manipulation of heavy materials and the finishing of surfaces in varied, often non-standardized construction environments. Because the output is a physical modification of a building rather than a digital artifact, it has near-zero agentic potential for software-based execution, and the work requires navigating the physical constraints and irregularities of real-world construction sites.

rows.title: Natural sciences managers
rows.old_rationale: This role is predominantly knowledge-based, involving data analysis, budgeting, and technical reporting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. While the job requires significant human-centric leadership and physical oversight of laboratories, AI will drastically increase productivity in reviewing research, drafting operational reports, and optimizing resource allocation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the reporting and data analysis can be digitized, the core value of this role lies in human leadership, team motivation, and the high-stakes ethical oversight of scientific research. The requirement for physical presence in laboratories and field sites, combined with the non-delegatable responsibility for safety and scientific integrity, limits the potential for full agentic automation.

rows.title: Chemists and materials scientists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a high-level blend of digital knowledge work and physical laboratory experimentation. AI is already revolutionizing the digital aspects—such as molecular modeling, predictive simulation, and technical reporting—allowing scientists to screen millions of compounds in silico before ever entering a lab. While the physical requirement of conducting experiments and handling chemicals provides a buffer, the massive productivity gains in research and analysis mean AI will fundamentally restructure how these scientists spend their time.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can increasingly handle the modeling and simulation aspects of chemistry, the core value remains the physical synthesis and validation of materials in unpredictable, non-digital laboratory environments. The occupation carries high systemic accountability, as errors in chemical formulation or safety testing can have catastrophic, life-altering consequences that require human professional liability.

rows.title: Preschool and childcare center directors
rows.old_rationale: This role is a hybrid of administrative knowledge work and high-stakes physical presence. While AI can significantly automate digital tasks like budgeting, scheduling, and curriculum planning, the core of the job involves physical facility oversight, staff supervision, and building trust-based relationships with parents and children that cannot be replicated by AI.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like budgeting and scheduling have some digital output potential, the core of the role involves managing the volatile, high-stakes physical environment of a childcare facility. The necessity for human presence is absolute, as the job centers on the emotional, developmental, and physical safety of children, creating a non-delegatable duty of care that AI cannot fulfill.

rows.title: Marriage and family therapists
rows.old_rationale: While the work is increasingly digital via telehealth and involves significant information processing, the core value of therapy lies in human-to-human empathy, trust, and the navigation of complex interpersonal dynamics. AI will likely automate administrative tasks and provide diagnostic support, but the high-stakes emotional nature of family and marriage counseling provides a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this profession is rooted in the human-to-human therapeutic alliance, which cannot be replicated by digital agents regardless of cognitive sophistication. While administrative tasks are automatable, the primary output—emotional healing and behavioral change within complex family systems—requires a human presence, moral accountability, and the ability to navigate the volatile, unstructured emotional realities of clients.

rows.title: Aerospace engineers
rows.old_rationale: Aerospace engineering is a high-level knowledge occupation where core tasks like aerodynamic modeling, structural analysis, and propulsion design are increasingly performed using digital simulation and AI-driven optimization tools. While the role requires critical human judgment for safety, regulatory compliance, and physical inspection of prototypes, the heavy reliance on computer-aided design (CAD) and complex data analysis makes it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and task automation.
rows.new_rationale: While aerospace engineers produce digital models and simulations, the final output is a physical, high-stakes vehicle that must operate in volatile, unstructured environments where failure is catastrophic. The role requires deep cognitive synthesis to manage complex, multi-variable systems, and the ultimate legal and ethical accountability for human safety remains a non-delegatable human responsibility.

rows.title: Judges and hearing officers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of researching legal issues, evaluating documents, and writing opinions are highly digital and susceptible to AI automation, which can significantly increase productivity and reduce the need for human staff. However, the role requires high-stakes human judgment, presiding over physical courtrooms, and maintaining public trust, which creates a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the analytical and research components of the job are highly susceptible to AI, the core output—a binding legal judgment—requires human moral authority and social legitimacy that cannot be delegated to software. The role is defined by systemic accountability and the necessity of a human arbiter to maintain public trust, rendering the agentic output potential low despite the high cognitive complexity of the underlying legal reasoning.

rows.title: Biochemists and biophysicists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is heavily centered on knowledge work, data analysis, and complex modeling, all of which are being revolutionized by AI (e.g., AlphaFold for protein structure prediction). While physical laboratory work and team management provide a buffer, a significant portion of the role involves literature review, grant writing, and data interpretation—tasks where AI can exponentially increase productivity or automate core analytical functions.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in data analysis and modeling, the core output of this role involves physical experimentation, synthesis of biological materials, and the navigation of unpredictable, non-standardized laboratory environments that require human intervention. Furthermore, the high degree of systemic accountability for safety, ethical research, and the validation of life-altering medical or environmental discoveries necessitates human oversight that cannot be fully delegated to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Physicists and astronomers
rows.old_rationale: Physicists and astronomers perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly digital, including data analysis, mathematical modeling, and software development. While AI significantly accelerates data processing and literature review, the core requirements of original theoretical innovation, experimental design, and complex physical-world problem solving provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with data analysis and modeling, the core value of this profession lies in the physical design, calibration, and operation of experimental apparatuses and the interpretation of novel phenomena, which remain tethered to physical reality. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical nature of their work in fields like medical physics, nuclear energy, and national security, where human oversight is essential for safety and ethical validation.

rows.title: Materials engineers
rows.old_rationale: Materials engineering is heavily reliant on digital simulation, data analysis, and computational modeling, areas where AI and machine learning are already accelerating material discovery and property prediction. While the role requires physical lab work and manufacturing oversight, a significant portion of the core value—designing alloys, analyzing failures, and writing technical reports—is increasingly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.
rows.new_rationale: While materials engineers use digital tools for simulation, their core value lies in the physical validation and deployment of materials in complex, often unpredictable real-world environments where failure can be catastrophic. Because they are responsible for the safety and integrity of physical structures and products, the final decision-making and legal liability remain firmly human, preventing full delegation to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Bioengineers and biomedical engineers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves heavy digital knowledge work, including software design, statistical modeling, and technical writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. While physical tasks like equipment maintenance and clinical collaboration provide a buffer, the core engineering and research functions are increasingly driven by AI-enhanced simulations and data analysis.
rows.new_rationale: While bioengineers produce digital models and software, their core value lies in the physical realization of medical devices and biological interventions, which requires physical testing and integration that AI cannot yet execute autonomously. The high systemic accountability score reflects the life-critical nature of their designs, where errors in medical hardware or genetic engineering carry extreme ethical and legal consequences that cannot be delegated to an agent.

rows.title: Landscape architects
rows.old_rationale: Landscape architecture is a predominantly digital knowledge-based occupation where core tasks like CADD modeling, site analysis, and cost estimation are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While the role requires physical site visits and interpersonal client management, the generative design capabilities of AI can significantly accelerate the creative and technical drafting phases, potentially reducing the total human hours required per project.
rows.new_rationale: While the design and modeling phases are increasingly digital and susceptible to AI, the final output requires physical integration into complex, site-specific environments that are inherently unpredictable and require on-site adaptation. Furthermore, the role carries significant legal and safety liability regarding environmental impact, structural integrity, and public safety, necessitating human professional accountability that cannot be fully delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Arbitrators, mediators, and conciliators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is primarily digital and information-based, involving the analysis of legal documents, evidence, and the drafting of settlement agreements—tasks where AI is rapidly achieving high proficiency. While the role requires significant interpersonal skills and emotional intelligence to manage confrontational parties, the core analytical and administrative functions are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and partial automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the documentation and procedural aspects of this role could be digitized, the core value lies in the human-to-human mediation of emotional, high-stakes conflict, which requires a level of empathy and social intelligence that AI cannot replicate. Furthermore, the role carries significant systemic accountability, as the finality of an arbitrator's decision or the ethical weight of a mediator's influence requires a human agent to hold legal and moral liability for the outcome.

rows.title: Marine engineers and naval architects
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves digital design, complex mathematical modeling, and technical report writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation. While physical site visits to shipyards and offshore platforms provide a buffer, the shift toward AI-integrated CAD, automated performance testing, and generative design for hulls and machinery significantly reshapes the workflow.
rows.new_rationale: While the design and schematic phases are increasingly digital, the core value of this role involves physical integration with complex, high-stakes maritime environments where AI cannot yet navigate the physical unpredictability of ship construction and offshore operations. The high systemic accountability score reflects the life-critical nature of marine engineering, where errors in structural integrity or safety systems carry immense legal and ethical liability that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Mining and geological engineers
rows.old_rationale: Mining and geological engineering involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including mine design, technical reporting, and data analysis, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains. However, the role also requires substantial physical presence for site evaluations, safety inspections, and supervising construction in remote, unpredictable environments, which provides a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While mining engineers produce digital designs and reports, the core value is the physical implementation of complex, high-stakes infrastructure in volatile, unstructured geological environments where AI cannot yet manage the physical risks. The high systemic accountability score reflects the non-delegatable legal and ethical liability for worker safety and environmental integrity, which requires human professional judgment that cannot be offloaded to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Military careers
rows.old_rationale: Military careers are a vast composite of physical combat, manual labor, and high-level digital strategy. While AI will heavily automate back-office administration, logistics, and intelligence analysis (high exposure), the core requirements of physical presence, tactical human leadership, and operation in unpredictable physical environments provide a significant buffer.
rows.new_rationale: Military operations are fundamentally defined by physical, high-stakes, and unpredictable environments where the primary output is the projection of force or physical security, which cannot be delegated to software. The necessity for human moral authority, ethical judgment in lethal scenarios, and the weight of systemic accountability for life-and-death decisions make this occupation highly resistant to AI-driven automation.

rows.title: Hand laborers and material movers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are fundamentally physical, involving the manual movement, cleaning, and packing of heavy materials in unpredictable real-world environments. While AI can optimize logistics, routing, and inventory tracking, the physical labor itself is resistant to AI and requires advanced robotics rather than software-based intelligence to automate.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor—moving, cleaning, and packing tangible goods—which cannot be digitized or executed via software, resulting in a very low agentic output potential. While the environments (warehouses, loading docks, waste collection routes) are often physically demanding and unstructured, the tasks themselves are highly routine and require minimal complex synthesis or human-to-human emotional connection.

rows.title: Top executives
rows.old_rationale: Top executives perform high-level knowledge work including financial analysis, strategic planning, and contract review, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core of the role involves human-centric responsibilities like leadership, high-stakes negotiation, and accountability to boards or the public, which provide a significant buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While top executives produce digital artifacts like strategy documents and budgets, the core value of the role lies in human-centric leadership, negotiation, and the synthesis of complex, often ambiguous organizational dynamics that software cannot replicate. The high systemic accountability score reflects that these roles carry non-delegatable legal and ethical liability for the organization's survival, which cannot be offloaded to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Registered nurses
rows.old_rationale: Registered nursing is a hybrid role that combines significant physical labor, such as administering treatments and moving patients, with complex interpersonal care and emotional support. While AI will heavily impact the knowledge-work components—such as patient monitoring, diagnostic analysis, and care planning—the core requirement for physical presence and human empathy creates a strong barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of nursing is rooted in physical, real-world patient care and the irreplaceable human-to-human bond, making the agentic output potential extremely low despite the high cognitive complexity of clinical decision-making. The environment is highly unpredictable, involving dynamic patient states and physical interventions that require human presence, moral judgment, and non-delegatable legal accountability for life-altering outcomes.

rows.title: Janitors and building cleaners
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical, involving manual labor in unpredictable and varied environments like schools, hospitals, and construction sites. While AI might assist with minor administrative tasks like supply ordering or scheduling, the primary work of cleaning, disinfecting, and making physical repairs remains a significant barrier to AI and robotic automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor in unstructured, real-world environments that require navigating complex, non-standardized spaces, which is highly resistant to current agentic software. While some tasks are routine, the physical variability of building maintenance and the need for on-site, real-time physical intervention keep the agentic output potential extremely low.

rows.title: Heavy and tractor-trailer truck drivers
rows.old_rationale: While the core task of driving is physical and occurs in a complex real-world environment, it is a primary target for autonomous vehicle AI, which could eventually automate long-haul highway segments. However, the job still requires significant physical presence for non-driving tasks like securing cargo, conducting inspections, and navigating 'last-mile' urban environments that remain challenging for current AI.
rows.new_rationale: While the primary task of driving is increasingly susceptible to autonomous vehicle technology, the 'agentic output' remains low because the job is fundamentally about physical movement of goods in highly unstructured, high-stakes environments. The high accountability score reflects the severe legal and ethical liability associated with operating 26,000-pound vehicles, which requires human oversight for safety and regulatory compliance even as automation advances.

rows.title: Teacher assistants
rows.old_rationale: Teacher assistants have moderate exposure because AI can significantly automate their administrative and instructional support tasks, such as grading, lesson preparation, and providing personalized tutoring through AI agents. However, a large portion of the role involves physical supervision, behavioral management, and assisting students with special needs or personal hygiene, which require a physical human presence that AI cannot replace.
rows.new_rationale: While recordkeeping and material preparation have low agentic potential, the core of the role involves physical supervision, behavioral management, and hands-on assistance for students with disabilities, which cannot be digitized. The high ontological necessity score reflects that the primary value is the human-to-human bond, emotional support, and physical safety in an unpredictable, high-stakes classroom environment.

rows.title: Postsecondary teachers
rows.old_rationale: Postsecondary teaching is primarily knowledge work involving research, writing, and curriculum design—all areas where AI is highly capable. While the role requires significant human interaction and mentorship, AI can automate grading, lesson planning, and literature reviews, potentially increasing individual productivity to the point of restructuring faculty needs.
rows.new_rationale: While course materials and administrative tasks have high agentic potential, the core value of postsecondary teaching remains deeply rooted in human mentorship, moral authority, and the facilitation of complex, non-linear intellectual development. The environment is highly structured, but the necessity for human-to-human connection and the ethical responsibility for student growth and academic integrity create significant barriers to full automation.

rows.title: Grounds maintenance workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical and performed in unpredictable outdoor environments, which provides a strong natural barrier against AI. While autonomous mowers or drones may assist with specific tasks, the majority of the work—such as planting, tree climbing, and complex manual maintenance—requires human dexterity and physical presence that AI cannot replicate.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor performed in highly unstructured, volatile outdoor environments, which is inherently resistant to digital agentic execution. While software can assist with scheduling or basic diagnostic monitoring, the final output requires physical manipulation of the real world, making it one of the least susceptible roles to pure software-based automation.

rows.title: Food preparation workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties are highly physical and require manual dexterity in unpredictable kitchen environments, which are difficult for AI to automate directly. While AI might optimize inventory management or scheduling, the primary tasks of washing, cutting, and preparing food remain resistant to digital replacement, though some labor may be displaced by industrial food processing automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of food and sanitation in a dynamic, high-hazard kitchen environment, which is highly resistant to digital agentic execution. While the tasks are routine, the physical variability of the environment (slippery floors, hot equipment, irregular ingredients) requires physical dexterity and real-time sensory-motor adaptation that software cannot currently replicate.

rows.title: Police and detectives
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical presence, emergency response, and manual tasks like making arrests and patrolling, which are resistant to AI. However, a significant portion of the work involves digital tasks such as writing detailed reports, searching databases, and analyzing evidence, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and efficiency gains.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of police work is physical intervention, presence, and the exercise of state-sanctioned authority in highly volatile, unstructured environments, which cannot be delegated to software. While data analysis and report writing are cognitive tasks, the final output—maintaining public order and making life-altering decisions—requires human moral agency, physical presence, and non-delegatable legal accountability.

rows.title: Social workers
rows.old_rationale: Social work involves a high degree of interpersonal interaction, empathy, and crisis management in physical environments, which provides a strong barrier against full automation. However, AI will significantly impact the profession by automating administrative tasks like case documentation, assisting in resource research, and providing diagnostic support tools for clinical practitioners.
rows.new_rationale: While social workers generate documentation, their core value is derived from in-person intervention, physical presence in volatile environments (e.g., home visits, crisis response), and the establishment of a human-to-human therapeutic bond that cannot be digitized. The high systemic accountability and the necessity of human empathy in high-stakes, unpredictable physical settings make this role highly resistant to automation, despite the complexity of the cognitive tasks involved.

rows.title: Licensed practical and licensed vocational nurses
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical tasks such as bathing, dressing, and wound care, which are currently beyond the reach of AI and robotics. However, AI will significantly impact the information-processing aspects of the job, including documenting patient care, monitoring vitals, and assisting with diagnostic reporting, leading to moderate overall exposure.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical, bedside care and the human-to-human bond, which cannot be digitized or executed by software. While the role involves monitoring and documentation, the primary tasks—such as physical patient assistance, wound care, and real-time clinical judgment in unpredictable, high-stakes environments—require a human presence and carry significant legal and ethical liability that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Quality control inspectors
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical inspection and data analysis. While AI-powered computer vision systems are rapidly automating the visual detection of defects, the role still requires significant physical presence to handle materials, operate manual gauges, and perform mechanical tests that robots cannot yet fully replicate in diverse manufacturing environments.
rows.new_rationale: The core function of this role—inspecting, measuring, and reporting on product defects—is increasingly handled by automated vision systems and digital sensors, giving it high agentic potential. While the work occurs in physical manufacturing environments, the task itself is highly procedural and relies on objective, quantifiable data rather than complex human synthesis or interpersonal interaction.

rows.title: Bus drivers
rows.old_rationale: While autonomous driving technology is a direct long-term threat to the core task of vehicle operation, the role involves significant physical and interpersonal responsibilities that AI cannot yet replicate. School bus drivers must manage student behavior and safety, while transit and charter drivers assist passengers with disabilities and handle physical luggage, creating a substantial barrier to full automation in the near term.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical transport of human beings in highly unstructured, high-stakes environments, which cannot be reduced to a digital artifact. While autonomous driving technology exists, the systemic accountability for passenger safety and the human necessity of managing unruly passengers or assisting individuals with disabilities creates a high barrier to full agentic replacement.

rows.title: Real estate brokers and sales agents
rows.old_rationale: Real estate agents perform a mix of digital knowledge work and physical/interpersonal tasks. AI can automate significant portions of their digital workload, such as market analysis, property descriptions, lead generation, and contract preparation, which increases individual productivity and may reduce the total number of agents needed. However, the core of the job still requires physical property showings, high-stakes human negotiation, and local networking, which provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the information retrieval and document preparation can be digitized, the core value of the role relies on high-stakes, human-to-human negotiation, emotional guidance through life-altering financial decisions, and the physical act of property assessment. The necessity for human trust, legal liability in contract mediation, and the nuanced, subjective nature of client relationship management creates a significant barrier to full agentic automation.

rows.title: Pharmacy technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical labor, such as measuring medications, packaging prescriptions, and interacting with patients in person, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, AI and automated dispensing systems are highly capable of handling the information-processing tasks like insurance claims, inventory management, and prescription data entry, which constitute a large portion of the daily workload.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the workflow involves digital data entry and insurance processing—tasks highly susceptible to automation—the physical handling, measuring, and verification of medication in a clinical setting limits full agentic potential. The role carries high systemic accountability due to the life-altering consequences of medication errors, necessitating human oversight even as the physical and administrative tasks become increasingly automated.

rows.title: Property, real estate, and community association managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical site management. While AI can automate significant portions of the job—such as drafting leases, financial reporting, budgeting, and coordinating maintenance requests—the role still requires physical inspections of facilities and high-stakes interpersonal conflict resolution. The exposure is high because the administrative and analytical 'office' tasks that comprise a large part of the day are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like billing and scheduling have high agentic potential, the core of the role involves navigating unpredictable physical environments (maintenance issues, property inspections) and managing complex human conflicts that require empathy and social intelligence. The necessity for human presence to mediate disputes, enforce rules, and provide physical oversight of assets limits the extent to which an AI agent can fully replace the role.

rows.title: Social and human service assistants
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital administrative work and high-touch interpersonal service. AI can significantly automate the 'knowledge work' components, such as researching community resources, assisting with complex benefit applications, and coordinating service schedules. However, the core of the job requires physical presence in homes or clinics and deep emotional intelligence to support clients in crisis, which provides a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the administrative and research components of this role (finding services, filing paperwork) have high digital potential, the core value is rooted in high-stakes, in-person human intervention in volatile environments like shelters or homes. The necessity of human empathy, moral presence, and the ability to navigate unpredictable, emotionally charged crises makes the role fundamentally resistant to full agentic replacement.

rows.title: Financial analysts
rows.old_rationale: Financial analysts perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving the processing of large datasets, trend analysis, and the generation of reports—all areas where AI excels. While high-level strategy and client relationships provide some insulation, the core tasks of data evaluation, risk assessment, and financial modeling are being rapidly automated or significantly enhanced by AI, leading to major restructuring of the occupation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a financial analyst—investment recommendations, portfolio adjustments, and risk reports—is entirely digital and can be executed directly by software agents connected to trading platforms. While the role requires high-level synthesis of complex market variables, the primary barrier to full automation is not the nature of the work itself, but the systemic accountability and legal liability associated with managing large-scale capital, which necessitates a human 'in the loop' for final sign-off.

rows.title: Correctional officers and bailiffs
rows.old_rationale: The core duties are physical and interpersonal, requiring real-time human presence to maintain security, conduct searches, and physically restrain individuals. AI exposure is limited to peripheral administrative tasks like report writing and log entry, or enhancing surveillance systems, but it cannot replace the physical oversight and intervention required in correctional and courtroom environments.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical presence, real-time de-escalation, and the exercise of state-sanctioned force, which cannot be digitized or delegated to software. The environment is highly volatile and unpredictable, requiring human judgment and physical intervention that AI agents are fundamentally unequipped to perform in a high-stakes, legally sensitive context.

rows.title: School and career counselors and advisors
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a mix of high-touch interpersonal work and digital information processing. AI can significantly automate the 'hard' data tasks like analyzing student performance trends, matching aptitudes to career paths, and drafting résumés, but the core functions of emotional support, crisis intervention (reporting abuse), and navigating complex human relationships in a school setting provide a strong buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can synthesize data for career planning or academic tracking, the core value of this role is rooted in human-to-human emotional support, trust-building, and the ethical responsibility of safeguarding student welfare. The agentic potential is low because the final 'product' is a human behavioral or developmental outcome that requires real-time, empathetic, and legally accountable human intervention, which cannot be delegated to software.

rows.title: Industrial engineers
rows.old_rationale: Industrial engineering is heavily focused on data analysis, systems design, and process optimization—tasks where AI and machine learning excel. While the role requires physical site visits and human collaboration to understand workflows, the core work product is digital analysis and modeling, making the occupation highly susceptible to significant productivity gains and restructuring through AI-driven automation.
rows.new_rationale: While industrial engineers produce digital models and process designs that are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization, their work requires navigating the 'messy' physical reality of factory floors and human-machine interfaces, which limits pure agentic execution. The high score in systemic accountability reflects the critical nature of their designs, where errors in safety or production systems carry significant legal and life-safety consequences that require human oversight.

rows.title: Firefighters
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of firefighting are intensely physical and require real-time presence in unpredictable, hazardous environments where AI cannot currently operate. While AI may enhance peripheral tasks like incident reporting, equipment maintenance scheduling, and predictive wildfire modeling, the essential work of rescuing victims and extinguishing fires remains a human-centric manual task.
rows.new_rationale: Firefighting is fundamentally a physical, real-world intervention requiring high-stakes, real-time navigation of chaotic, unstructured environments that cannot be digitized or delegated to software. The core value of the role is rooted in human physical presence, moral authority in life-or-death triage, and absolute legal and ethical accountability for outcomes, making it highly resistant to AI displacement.

rows.title: Painters, construction and maintenance
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are entirely physical, requiring manual dexterity, mobility at heights, and real-time adaptation to unpredictable physical environments. While AI might assist with peripheral business tasks like estimating or scheduling, it cannot perform the labor-intensive tasks of surface preparation and paint application.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor—modifying the material state of structures—which cannot be executed by software or digital agents. The work environment is highly unstructured, involving complex physical navigation, heights, and variable site conditions that remain difficult for autonomous systems to manage reliably.

rows.title: Electrical and electronics engineers
rows.old_rationale: The core work of designing circuits, modeling systems, and writing documentation is digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and optimization. While physical testing, site visits, and manufacturing supervision provide a buffer, the rapid advancement of AI in CAD, simulation, and coding significantly increases the productivity and exposure of the knowledge-work components.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the design and simulation work is digital, the final output often requires physical integration, testing, and validation in complex, real-world environments like power grids or aerospace systems, which limits pure agentic execution. The high score for systemic accountability reflects the critical nature of these systems, where human oversight is legally and ethically required to sign off on safety-critical infrastructure that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Food processing equipment workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, such as loading ingredients, cleaning machinery, and manual quality checks, which are resistant to AI. While AI can optimize recipes, monitor sensor data, and automate some equipment controls, the requirement for human presence in a physical manufacturing environment to handle unpredictable mechanical issues and sanitation limits overall exposure.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this job is physical manipulation of ingredients and machinery in a factory setting, which remains largely outside the reach of digital-only agents. While the work is procedural and repetitive, the requirement for physical interaction with heavy equipment and raw materials limits agentic potential, as the 'final output' is a physical food product rather than a digital artifact.

rows.title: Transportation, storage, and distribution managers
rows.old_rationale: This role involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as logistics planning, budgeting, and supply chain optimization, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven efficiency gains. However, the core job also requires physical presence for facility inspections, safety compliance monitoring, and high-stakes interpersonal leadership in unpredictable environments like warehouses and railyards, providing a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the logistics planning and data analysis can be digitized, the role requires significant real-world intervention, such as managing physical labor, resolving on-site disruptions, and ensuring safety compliance in volatile warehouse or transit environments. The human element remains critical for leadership, conflict resolution, and taking ultimate legal and ethical responsibility for safety and operational failures that software cannot yet fully own.

rows.title: Manicurists and pedicurists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves high-precision physical labor, tactile sensitivity, and real-time human interaction in a physical environment. While AI can assist with peripheral business tasks like scheduling or inventory management, the primary service of cleaning, shaping, and decorating nails remains a manual task that is difficult to automate with current robotics.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is a physical, tactile service performed on a human body, which cannot be digitized or executed by software agents. While the work environment is relatively controlled, the necessity for human touch, interpersonal connection, and the physical dexterity required to safely manipulate sharp tools around a client's skin makes this role highly resistant to automation.

rows.title: Speech-language pathologists
rows.old_rationale: Speech-language pathology involves a significant amount of digital-adjacent work, such as analyzing speech patterns, documenting progress, and developing treatment plans, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. However, the core of the job requires physical presence for swallowing therapy, real-time interpersonal coaching, and high-stakes clinical judgment that limits full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist in diagnostic data analysis and provide supplementary exercises, the core value of the role is the physical, real-time interaction required to treat swallowing disorders and speech mechanics, which cannot be delegated to software. The necessity for human empathy, trust, and the high-stakes accountability for clinical outcomes in a therapeutic setting makes this role highly resistant to full automation.

rows.title: Painting and coating workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and presence in industrial or repair environments, which provides a natural barrier to AI. While AI and computer vision can enhance automated spray systems and quality control sensors, the need for human workers to set up equipment, prepare surfaces, and perform manual touch-ups in unpredictable repair settings remains significant.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of materials in a 3D space, which is inherently resistant to digital agentic execution despite the use of automated machinery. While some tasks involve machine operation, the requirement for manual touch-ups, surface preparation, and handling of irregular objects in physical environments keeps the agentic potential low and the reliance on human physical presence high.

rows.title: Occupational health and safety specialists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital knowledge work. While AI can significantly automate data analysis, report writing, and regulatory cross-referencing, the core duties require physical inspections of worksites, manual testing with specialized equipment, and real-time human interaction to conduct safety trainings and emergency investigations.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can analyze data and generate reports, the core value of this role lies in physical site inspections, real-world hazard mitigation, and the enforcement of safety protocols in volatile, unstructured environments like construction sites or mines. The high systemic accountability score reflects the legal and ethical burden of ensuring human life safety, which requires a human agent to exercise professional judgment and assume liability for site-specific risks that software cannot legally or physically own.

rows.title: Physician assistants
rows.old_rationale: Physician assistants perform a mix of high-level knowledge work and physical clinical tasks. While AI will significantly augment diagnostic accuracy, treatment planning, and administrative documentation, the role requires physical presence for examinations, surgical procedures, and hands-on treatments like stitching wounds, as well as complex interpersonal counseling that is difficult to automate.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with diagnostics and data synthesis, the core output of a PA involves physical interventions—such as suturing, setting bones, and physical examinations—which cannot be executed by software. The role is deeply anchored in human-to-human trust, physical presence in unpredictable clinical environments, and high-stakes legal and ethical accountability for patient outcomes that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Meeting, convention, and event planners
rows.old_rationale: A significant portion of the role involves digital tasks such as logistics planning, vendor research, contract review, and communications, which are highly susceptible to AI automation and productivity gains. However, the core of the job requires physical site inspections, real-time on-site problem solving during events, and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation and relationship management, which provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the administrative and logistical planning (scheduling, budgeting, vendor communication) can be digitized and automated, the core value of the role lies in on-site crisis management and the high-touch, empathetic negotiation required to satisfy human clients. The physical environment of an event is inherently volatile and unstructured, requiring real-time human judgment and social presence that software cannot replicate.

rows.title: Construction and building inspectors
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical site visits and digital knowledge work. AI and computer vision can significantly automate the 'plans examiner' and report-writing functions, while remote inspection technology is already projected to reduce labor demand. However, the core requirement for physical presence to navigate tight spaces, use manual testing equipment, and verify real-world conditions provides a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While plan reviews can be digitized, the core of the job requires physical presence in unstructured, often hazardous environments to verify real-world compliance that sensors cannot yet fully replicate. The high systemic accountability score reflects the fact that these inspectors hold legal and ethical responsibility for public safety, where a failure to identify a structural defect can lead to catastrophic loss of life.

rows.title: Respiratory therapists
rows.old_rationale: Respiratory therapy involves a significant amount of physical, hands-on patient care, such as performing chest physiotherapy, intubating patients, and maintaining equipment in real-time clinical environments. While AI will heavily assist in diagnostic analysis, ventilator setting optimization, and patient monitoring, the core of the job requires physical presence and interpersonal interaction that cannot be digitized.
rows.new_rationale: Respiratory therapy is fundamentally a physical, bedside intervention involving manual procedures like chest physiotherapy and emergency airway management, which cannot be digitized or offloaded to software. The high stakes of life-support management in volatile, unpredictable clinical environments necessitate constant human presence, physical dexterity, and non-delegatable ethical and legal accountability for patient survival.

rows.title: Veterinary technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical tasks that AI cannot perform, such as restraining animals, administering anesthesia, performing surgery prep, and providing emergency first aid. However, AI has significant exposure in the diagnostic and administrative aspects of the job, including analyzing lab results, interpreting X-rays, and managing patient records, which may increase individual productivity.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical, in-person care and the management of unpredictable, living subjects, which cannot be digitized or offloaded to software. While diagnostic data processing may be automated, the physical restraint, administration of anesthesia, and emotional labor required to comfort both animals and owners necessitate a human presence that is fundamentally resistant to agentic replacement.

rows.title: Water and wastewater treatment plant and system operators
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical labor and digital monitoring, with AI and advanced automation already contributing to a projected 7% decline in employment. While AI can optimize chemical dosing, analyze sensor data, and manage complex system flows, the role still requires physical presence for equipment maintenance, manual sampling, and emergency response to mechanical failures.
rows.new_rationale: While monitoring and data reporting are increasingly digital, the core value of this role is the physical maintenance of critical infrastructure and manual intervention during emergencies, which limits agentic potential. The high systemic accountability score reflects the life-critical nature of water safety and the legal liability inherent in managing public utility systems that cannot be fully offloaded to software.

rows.title: Public relations and fundraising managers
rows.old_rationale: The core digital tasks of this role—writing press releases, drafting speeches, analyzing social media trends, and creating fundraising strategies—are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. However, the role's heavy reliance on high-stakes interpersonal relationships, such as face-to-face donor meetings and managing media crises, provides a significant human-centric buffer that prevents a higher score.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the output (press releases, campaign strategy, digital content) is highly amenable to AI generation, the core value of these roles relies on high-stakes human relationship management, trust-building with donors, and the navigation of sensitive, reputation-critical crises. The necessity for human presence in high-value fundraising and the requirement for human accountability in legal and ethical communications limit the extent to which these roles can be fully delegated to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Flooring installers and tile and stone setters
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are entirely physical, involving manual dexterity, heavy lifting, and real-time adaptation to irregular physical surfaces in unpredictable environments. While AI might assist with minor administrative tasks or design visualization, it cannot perform the tactile labor of cutting, laying, and finishing materials on-site.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical manipulation of materials in unstructured, varied environments, which cannot be executed by software or digital agents. While the work requires precision and planning, it is fundamentally a manual trade where the primary output is a physical installation rather than a digital artifact.

rows.title: Rehabilitation counselors
rows.old_rationale: Rehabilitation counseling involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as developing rehabilitation plans, maintaining records, and researching resources, which are highly susceptible to AI assistance. However, the core of the profession relies on high-stakes interpersonal interaction, empathy, and physical advocacy for clients with disabilities, creating a natural barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is the human-to-human therapeutic bond and the navigation of complex, sensitive personal circumstances, which cannot be delegated to an agent. While administrative tasks and resource matching have some digital potential, the primary output is the psychological and social support provided to individuals, requiring high-level human empathy and ethical accountability that AI cannot replicate.

rows.title: Environmental scientists and specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including data analysis, report writing, and regulatory compliance, which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the role is anchored by a physical component involving fieldwork, site inspections, and laboratory sample analysis that AI cannot currently replicate. AI will likely serve as a powerful tool for modeling environmental impacts and drafting technical documents, increasing individual productivity while leaving the physical data collection and stakeholder relationship management to humans.
rows.new_rationale: While the final output (reports, policy recommendations, and compliance plans) is digital, the value is derived from physical site assessments in highly variable, unstructured environments that require human judgment to interpret complex ecological data. The high systemic accountability score reflects the legal and ethical weight of environmental impact assessments, where human professional liability is required to certify that a project meets regulatory standards and public safety requirements.

rows.title: Interior designers
rows.old_rationale: Interior design is heavily reliant on digital tools like CAD and BIM, which are being rapidly transformed by generative AI capable of creating photorealistic renderings and floor plans from text prompts. While the job requires physical site visits, material inspections, and complex interpersonal coordination with contractors, the core creative and technical drafting tasks are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and productivity gains.
rows.new_rationale: While the design and documentation phases are increasingly digital and susceptible to AI-driven generative workflows, the occupation requires significant on-site physical coordination, negotiation with contractors, and the management of unpredictable construction variables. The core value remains deeply tied to human-centric interpretation of client needs and the legal/safety liability inherent in building code compliance and structural modifications.

rows.title: Veterinarians
rows.old_rationale: Veterinary work is a hybrid of physical procedures and complex diagnostic knowledge work. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy through image analysis (X-rays/ultrasounds) and treatment planning, the core of the job requires physical surgery, manual examinations, and high-stakes interpersonal communication with pet owners that cannot be automated.
rows.new_rationale: Veterinary medicine requires complex, real-time physical intervention in highly unpredictable environments, such as performing surgery on animals in remote or field settings. The core value is deeply rooted in the human-animal bond and the ethical, non-delegatable responsibility for the life and welfare of a sentient creature, which cannot be reduced to a digital output.

rows.title: Interpreters and translators
rows.old_rationale: The core work product is purely digital information (text and speech) in a domain where Large Language Models already demonstrate near-human proficiency. While high-stakes interpreting (legal, medical) and sign language still require human presence and nuanced judgment, the vast majority of translation and routine interpreting tasks are being rapidly automated or shifted to 'post-editing' roles, significantly reducing the total human labor required per unit of work.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this profession—translated text or interpreted speech—is inherently digital or auditory, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven automation. While the work requires high-level cognitive synthesis to capture nuance, tone, and cultural context, the primary barrier to full automation is the systemic accountability required in high-stakes environments (legal, medical) and the ontological necessity of human presence for trust and empathy in sensitive interpersonal interactions.

rows.title: Cardiovascular technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role requires significant physical presence for tasks like positioning patients, attaching electrodes, and assisting in invasive surgical procedures.
