While AI will heavily automate the analysis of EKG and pulmonary data, the core requirements of patient care, physical stamina, and real-time technical assistance in a clinical environment provide a strong barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the diagnostic data produced is digital, the core value of the role is tied to physical patient interaction, invasive procedural assistance, and real-time monitoring of unstable biological systems, which cannot be fully offloaded to software. The high scores for accountability and human necessity reflect the life-critical nature of the work, where physical presence and human judgment are required to manage patient safety during volatile medical interventions.

rows.title: Concierges
rows.old_rationale: The role is a hybrid of digital information processing and physical hospitality. While AI can automate core tasks like making reservations, providing local recommendations, and answering emails, the job still requires a physical presence for greeting guests, handling packages, and providing high-touch interpersonal service that is central to the luxury and residential experience.
rows.new_rationale: The core tasks of a concierge—booking, messaging, and information retrieval—are highly digital and already being offloaded to software agents, resulting in high agentic potential. While the work environment is highly controlled, the role retains a high ontological necessity score because the primary value proposition is the 'human touch,' hospitality, and the social comfort provided to guests or residents.

rows.title: Urban and regional planners
rows.old_rationale: Urban planners perform heavy knowledge work including data analysis, GIS mapping, and report writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. However, the role requires significant physical site inspections and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation with government officials and the public, which provides a buffer against total automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the planning output (GIS maps, reports, and data models) is digital and highly susceptible to AI generation, the core value of the role is deeply embedded in political negotiation, public consensus-building, and ethical arbitration between competing community interests. The high systemic accountability score reflects the fact that planners make life-altering decisions regarding land use and infrastructure that require human moral and legal authority, which cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Private detectives and investigators
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of digital research and physical field work. AI significantly enhances the digital aspects—such as background checks, social media analysis, and report writing—making investigators much more productive, but the core requirements of physical surveillance, in-person interviews, and undercover store work provide a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While digital OSINT and data aggregation are highly automatable, the core of the profession relies on physical surveillance, in-person interviews, and navigating unpredictable, real-world environments where human intuition and presence are required. Furthermore, the role carries significant legal and ethical liability regarding privacy and evidence admissibility, which necessitates human accountability that cannot be offloaded to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Adult basic and secondary education and ESL teachers
rows.old_rationale: The core work product—language instruction, literacy training, and test preparation—is fundamentally digital and information-based, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven tutoring and translation tools. While the role requires significant cultural sensitivity and interpersonal motivation, AI can now provide personalized lesson plans, real-time feedback, and adaptive learning paths that significantly increase teacher productivity or replace routine instructional tasks.
rows.new_rationale: While educational content and assessment can be digitized and delivered by AI, the core value of this role lies in the human-to-human pedagogical bond, motivation, and cultural sensitivity required to support adult learners. The teacher's role as a mentor and emotional anchor in a classroom setting remains fundamentally human, even as the delivery of instructional material becomes increasingly automated.

rows.title: Archivists, curators, and museum workers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical preservation. AI will significantly automate archival tasks like cataloging, metadata generation, and electronic record management, while also assisting curators with research and exhibit design. However, the physical requirements of handling artifacts, restoring delicate items, and managing in-person public interactions provide a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While digital cataloging and archival management have high potential for automation, the core value of this profession lies in the physical preservation, restoration, and curation of unique, non-standardized artifacts that require human tactile judgment and ethical stewardship. The work involves complex, high-dimensional synthesis of historical context and physical condition, and the physical environment of museums and archives—often involving fragile, one-of-a-kind items—remains largely resistant to fully autonomous agentic execution.

rows.title: Environmental engineers
rows.old_rationale: Environmental engineering involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including data analysis, system design, and report writing, which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the role is insulated by the necessity for physical site inspections, regulatory compliance monitoring in the field, and the legal accountability associated with Professional Engineer (PE) licensure.
rows.new_rationale: While environmental engineers produce digital reports and designs, the core value lies in physical site remediation and infrastructure implementation, which requires navigating highly unpredictable, unstructured physical environments. The high systemic accountability score reflects the legal and ethical necessity of a licensed human professional to sign off on projects that impact public health and safety, a responsibility that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Agricultural and food scientists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including data analysis, research synthesis, and report writing, which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. However, the role is anchored by physical requirements such as field sampling, laboratory experiments, and on-site inspections at farms or processing plants that AI cannot currently replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While the research and data analysis components are highly cognitive, the core output often involves physical interventions in biological systems (farms, livestock, soil) that cannot be executed by software alone. The work requires navigating highly variable, unstructured physical environments where outcomes are subject to complex biological and environmental factors, necessitating human judgment and physical presence for validation and implementation.

rows.title: Semiconductor processing technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant physical component, requiring presence in cleanrooms to handle wafers, maintain equipment, and perform manual inspections. However, AI and advanced automation are increasingly capable of monitoring process data, optimizing machine parameters, and performing high-precision quality control, which automates the information-processing and monitoring aspects of the job.
rows.new_rationale: While the work environment is highly controlled and the tasks are increasingly automated, the physical handling of delicate wafers and the manual intervention required for equipment maintenance and troubleshooting limit agentic potential. The role is primarily a bridge between automated systems and physical production, requiring human dexterity and on-site physical presence that software cannot yet fully replicate.

rows.title: Set and exhibit designers
rows.old_rationale: The core creative process—including script analysis, conceptual sketching, and CAD modeling—is increasingly susceptible to generative AI and automated design tools that can rapidly produce 3D environments and visual concepts. While the role requires physical-world considerations like material budgets and on-site installation oversight, the shift toward digital production (such as virtual sets and LED volumes) and AI-assisted drafting significantly increases productivity and reduces the total human labor required per project.
rows.new_rationale: While the design phase is increasingly digital and CAD-based, the final output is a physical, site-specific installation that requires real-world material integration and on-site problem solving, limiting pure agentic execution. The role demands high-level creative synthesis to translate abstract narratives into physical spaces, requiring human-to-human collaboration with directors and curators to align on subjective artistic vision.

rows.title: Air traffic controllers
rows.old_rationale: The core work is fundamentally digital and data-driven, involving the monitoring of radar screens and the processing of complex spatial information to ensure aircraft separation. While the high-stakes nature of safety and the need for real-time human communication with pilots provide a buffer, AI and automated systems (like NextGen) are already increasing productivity and handling more traffic per controller, leading to a very low projected growth in human employment.
rows.new_rationale: While the output is highly digital (instructions to pilots), the role requires managing high-stakes, real-time physical variables in an unpredictable environment (weather, mechanical failures, human error). The systemic accountability is absolute, as the controller holds legal and ethical responsibility for the lives of passengers, making the 'human-in-the-loop' a non-delegatable requirement for safety and liability.

rows.title: Health and safety engineers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant mix of digital knowledge work and physical presence. While AI can assist in reviewing equipment plans, analyzing accident data, and interpreting complex safety regulations, the core duties require physical inspections of facilities, interviewing employees for investigations, and directing the hands-on installation of safety devices.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can synthesize safety data and regulatory requirements, the core output involves physical site inspections and the implementation of safety protocols in volatile, unstructured industrial environments where digital models often fail to capture real-world nuance. The high systemic accountability score reflects the non-delegatable legal and ethical burden of certifying human safety, which requires human judgment and professional liability that cannot be offloaded to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Chemical engineers
rows.old_rationale: Chemical engineering involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, including process simulation, CAD design, and data analysis, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization. However, the role is anchored by the need for physical presence in laboratories and industrial plants to monitor equipment, ensure safety compliance, and troubleshoot hardware in the real world.
rows.new_rationale: While chemical engineers utilize digital design tools, their core value lies in the physical implementation and safety optimization of complex, high-stakes industrial processes that cannot be fully abstracted into software. The high score for systemic accountability reflects the life-altering consequences of chemical process failures, which require human professional licensure and ethical oversight that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Forensic science technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role is a hybrid of physical evidence collection and digital analysis. While AI can significantly automate pattern recognition (fingerprints, ballistics) and statistical reporting, the job requires physical presence at crime scenes to collect samples and the human credibility necessary to testify in court as an expert witness.
rows.new_rationale: While laboratory analysis involves digital reporting, the core value of the role is rooted in the physical collection and preservation of evidence in highly unstructured, volatile crime scenes where human judgment is required to maintain the chain of custody. The high systemic accountability score reflects the fact that these technicians provide critical, life-altering testimony in legal proceedings, a responsibility that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent due to the requirement for human liability and courtroom presence.

rows.title: Microbiologists
rows.old_rationale: Microbiologists have a high exposure to AI because a significant portion of their work involves data analysis, pattern recognition in genetic sequences, and technical reporting—all areas where AI is rapidly advancing. However, the physical requirement to conduct laboratory experiments, maintain biological cultures, and collect field samples provides a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can assist with data analysis and reporting, the core value of a microbiologist lies in physical laboratory experimentation, sample handling, and the interpretation of biological phenomena that cannot be fully digitized or automated. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical nature of their work in public health, drug development, and environmental safety, where human oversight is required to validate findings and assume liability for life-altering scientific conclusions.

rows.title: Petroleum engineers
rows.old_rationale: Petroleum engineering involves significant digital knowledge work, such as reservoir modeling, data analysis, and drilling simulation, which are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization and automation. However, the role remains anchored by the necessity of physical site visits to remote or offshore locations to oversee equipment installation and troubleshoot real-world mechanical issues that AI cannot physically address.
rows.new_rationale: While petroleum engineers perform complex data analysis that AI can assist with, their core value lies in high-stakes physical engineering decisions in volatile, unstructured environments like offshore rigs or deep-well sites where AI cannot physically intervene. The role carries immense systemic accountability for safety and environmental impact, requiring human professional judgment and legal liability that cannot be delegated to software.

rows.title: Zoologists and wildlife biologists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital knowledge work. AI significantly impacts the data-heavy aspects of the job, such as analyzing GIS data, processing camera trap imagery, and modeling population dynamics, but it cannot replace the physical requirements of collecting biological specimens, handling wild animals, or navigating remote outdoor environments.
rows.new_rationale: While data analysis and modeling are digital, the core value of this profession is rooted in physical fieldwork, specimen collection, and direct interaction with unpredictable, non-standardized biological environments that software cannot navigate or manipulate. The necessity for human judgment in high-stakes conservation decisions and the physical nature of the work make it highly resistant to full agentic automation.

rows.title: Court reporters and simultaneous captioners
rows.old_rationale: The core product of this occupation is a verbatim digital transcript, a task where AI speech-to-text and natural language processing are already highly proficient. While legal requirements for human oversight and the need to clarify inaudible speech provide a temporary buffer, the rapid advancement of AI in transcription and real-time captioning creates extreme pressure for automation and massive productivity gains that reduce the need for human workers.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation is a digital text transcript, which is highly susceptible to end-to-end automation via advanced speech-to-text and natural language processing agents. While the work requires high accuracy and systemic accountability due to the legal nature of the record, the actual task of transcription is increasingly a digital artifact that AI can generate and format without human intervention.

rows.title: Fire inspectors
rows.old_rationale: The role requires a significant physical presence for onsite inspections, evidence collection in debris, and forest patrolling, which provides a natural barrier to AI. However, AI can substantially assist with digital tasks such as reviewing building blueprints, analyzing fire scene data, and automating the documentation of code violations and legal reports.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical inspection, evidence collection in volatile post-fire environments, and the exercise of legal authority, which cannot be delegated to software. While AI can assist in data analysis and code referencing, the physical, high-stakes nature of the work and the requirement for human legal/moral accountability in criminal proceedings make it highly resistant to agentic automation.

rows.title: Dancers and choreographers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical performance, athleticism, and real-time human interaction in physical spaces, which AI cannot replicate. While AI may assist choreographers with music selection or administrative tasks, the fundamental value of the work lies in the human body's movement and live artistic expression.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical, live performance of human movement, which cannot be replaced by digital agents or software outputs. While choreography involves complex creative synthesis, the execution is fundamentally tied to human embodiment, empathy, and the shared physical experience between performer and audience, rendering it highly resistant to automation.

rows.title: Environmental engineering technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital knowledge work. While AI can significantly automate data analysis, report reviewing, and regulatory compliance checks, it cannot physically collect soil and water samples, maintain field equipment, or perform on-site hazardous material disposal. The BLS already notes that automated testing is increasing productivity, which limits job growth even as the core physical tasks remain human-centric.
rows.new_rationale: While data documentation and reporting are digital, the core value of this role is physical interaction with hazardous environments, sample collection, and equipment maintenance in the field. The high environmental unpredictability and the requirement for physical presence to manage toxic materials or site-specific hazards make it resistant to full agentic automation, despite the regulatory and safety-critical nature of the work.

rows.title: Epidemiologists
rows.old_rationale: Epidemiology is a data-intensive knowledge profession where core tasks like statistical analysis, pattern recognition in large datasets, and literature reviews are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. While the role requires human judgment for policy-making, community outreach, and field investigations, the shift toward digital health records and automated surveillance systems significantly increases the productivity and exposure of the occupation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the data analysis and reporting can be automated, the role requires high-level synthesis of disparate, often incomplete, real-world data to make life-altering public health decisions. The high systemic accountability and the necessity for human trust in communicating risks to the public and policymakers ensure that, despite the digital nature of the output, the final judgment remains a deeply human responsibility.

rows.title: Podiatrists
rows.old_rationale: Podiatry is a medical specialty that requires significant physical presence for exams, manual procedures, and complex surgeries that AI cannot perform. However, AI will heavily impact the knowledge-based aspects of the role, such as analyzing X-rays, diagnosing conditions from medical history, and automating clinical documentation and administrative tasks.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of podiatry is physical, invasive intervention and surgery on human anatomy, which cannot be digitized or executed by software agents. While AI can assist in diagnostic synthesis, the physical unpredictability of patient physiology and the high-stakes legal and ethical accountability for surgical outcomes necessitate a human practitioner at every stage of the process.

rows.title: Aerospace engineering and operations technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical labor, such as building test facilities, installing instruments, and maintaining hardware, which provides a buffer against AI automation. However, the role also includes substantial digital tasks like recording data, running computer simulations, and calibrating systems—areas where AI is rapidly improving and can significantly enhance productivity or automate data analysis.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical manipulation, installation, and maintenance of hardware in high-stakes, non-standardized aerospace environments, which limits agentic output potential despite the digital nature of the data they process. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical safety implications of their work, where human verification of physical integrity remains a non-delegatable requirement for aerospace certification.

rows.title: Hydrologists
rows.old_rationale: Hydrologists have a high exposure to AI because a significant portion of their work involves data analysis, computer modeling, and report writing—tasks where AI is rapidly advancing. However, the physical requirement of field work, such as collecting water samples and measuring streamflow in remote locations, provides a substantial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While hydrologists produce digital models and reports, their work is fundamentally tethered to physical, unstructured field environments where data collection requires human judgment and physical navigation. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical nature of their findings, which dictate public safety and infrastructure viability, necessitating human professional liability that cannot be fully offloaded to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Nuclear technicians
rows.old_rationale: Nuclear technicians perform a mix of digital monitoring and physical labor, such as collecting environmental samples and maintaining hardware. While AI can significantly automate the data analysis, radiation calculation, and system monitoring aspects of the role, the requirement for physical presence in hazardous environments and the manual calibration of equipment provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While data monitoring and analysis are increasingly digital, the core value of this role lies in physical maintenance, onsite sample collection, and emergency response within high-stakes, volatile nuclear environments that require human physical presence. The extreme systemic accountability for safety and the physical nature of the work environment create significant barriers to full agentic automation, as the 'final step' involves physical intervention in hazardous, non-standardized settings.

rows.title: Genetic counselors
rows.old_rationale: Genetic counselors perform high-level knowledge work, including analyzing complex DNA data and medical histories, which is highly susceptible to AI optimization. However, the core of the role involves providing psychological support and navigating sensitive ethical decisions with patients, requiring a level of empathy and human connection that limits full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can synthesize complex genetic data and risk profiles, the core value of the role is the delivery of life-altering, emotionally sensitive information that requires human empathy and moral presence. The high systemic accountability and the necessity for human-to-human trust in high-stakes medical decision-making make this role highly resistant to full agentic automation.

rows.title: Agricultural engineers
rows.old_rationale: Agricultural engineering is a hybrid role that combines digital design and data analysis with physical site visits and hardware testing. While AI will significantly automate core tasks like CAD modeling, environmental simulation, and precision farming data analysis, the requirement for physical oversight of construction, equipment testing in the field, and real-world troubleshooting provides a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While agricultural engineers use digital design tools, their core value lies in physical systems integration within highly volatile, unstructured natural environments (farms, water systems, livestock facilities) where AI cannot yet manage the physical variables. The high accountability score reflects the professional engineering (PE) licensure requirement, where the engineer bears legal and safety liability for infrastructure and machinery that directly impacts human safety and environmental health.

rows.title: Retail sales workers
rows.old_rationale: Retail sales involve a mix of physical tasks (stocking shelves, demonstrating products) and interpersonal interaction that provides a barrier to full automation. However, AI is increasingly handling the information-heavy aspects of the job, such as personalized product recommendations, answering technical questions via kiosks/mobile apps, and automating inventory management and checkout processes, which reduces the total human labor required per store.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the transactional and informational aspect of retail is increasingly handled by digital interfaces and self-checkout systems, the role retains a significant 'human-in-the-loop' requirement for physical assistance, persuasion, and customer service in unstructured store environments. The agentic potential is limited by the necessity of physical interaction with merchandise and the customer's immediate, often subjective, needs, which prevents full end-to-end automation for a large portion of the workforce.

rows.title: Cashiers
rows.old_rationale: While the job has physical components like bagging and stocking, the core function of processing transactions is highly susceptible to automation through computer vision and AI-driven self-checkout systems. The BLS already projects a significant decline in employment due to these technological shifts, as AI can increasingly handle age verification, fraud detection, and customer inquiries.
rows.new_rationale: The core function of a cashier—processing transactions and managing inventory data—is almost entirely digital and already being executed by self-checkout software and automated payment systems. Because the tasks are highly structured and involve minimal complex decision-making, the occupation is exceptionally vulnerable to full automation, requiring little to no human intervention in the primary workflow.

rows.title: Customer service representatives
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this role—answering questions, processing orders, and resolving complaints—are fundamentally digital and information-based, making them highly susceptible to AI-driven automation. Large Language Models and AI agents are already capable of handling complex natural language interactions, and the BLS specifically projects a decline in employment due to automation in this sector.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is information retrieval, account modification, and communication, all of which are natively digital and highly susceptible to end-to-end execution by AI agents. While human interaction is often used for de-escalation or brand differentiation, the functional tasks are largely procedural and occur within highly controlled, predictable digital or retail environments.

rows.title: Cooks
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical dexterity, sensory judgment (taste and smell), and real-time adaptation in a high-heat, unpredictable kitchen environment. While AI-driven automation is already impacting fast food and routine institutional cooking, high-end restaurant and private household cooking remain protected by the need for complex manual skills and human creativity.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a cook is a physical, consumable product, which limits agentic potential to the digital management of recipes or inventory rather than the physical act of cooking. While high-end culinary work requires synthesis, the environment is physically volatile—involving heat, sharp tools, and time-sensitive coordination—which remains difficult for autonomous agents to navigate safely and effectively.

rows.title: General office clerks
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this role—data entry, document formatting, scheduling, and information processing—are almost entirely digital and routine, making them highly susceptible to AI automation. While some physical tasks like handling mail or greeting visitors remain, the vast majority of the workload can be absorbed by AI agents and automated software, as reflected in the BLS's projected decline for the occupation.
rows.new_rationale: The core tasks of a general office clerk—data entry, scheduling, filing, and document processing—are almost entirely digital and can be executed end-to-end by software agents. Because the work occurs in highly structured, predictable office environments and requires minimal complex synthesis or high-stakes human judgment, it is exceptionally vulnerable to full automation.

rows.title: Assemblers and fabricators
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical manipulation, manual dexterity, and real-time adjustments in a physical environment, which provides a significant buffer against AI. While AI and robotics are increasing efficiency and automating some repetitive tasks, a substantial portion of the work—especially in complex assembly, prototyping, and custom fabrication—still requires human presence and tactile skill.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical manipulation and assembly of tangible goods, which cannot be executed by software alone, keeping agentic potential very low. While the environment is generally controlled, the physical nature of the work—requiring dexterity, strength, and real-time adjustment to material variances—remains a significant barrier to full automation despite the increasing use of robotics.

rows.title: Construction laborers and helpers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical, involving manual labor, tool operation, and site preparation in unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like scheduling or specialized robotic equipment control, the vast majority of the work requires human presence, physical strength, and real-time adaptation to physical obstacles.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor in highly unstructured, volatile environments that require real-time sensory-motor adaptation, making it extremely resistant to digital agentic execution. While the cognitive requirements are moderate, the necessity for human physical presence and the high-stakes nature of construction safety and structural integrity create significant barriers to automation.

rows.title: General maintenance and repair workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable physical environments, which are currently beyond the reach of AI and robotics. AI may assist with peripheral tasks like diagnosing equipment issues via computer vision or managing supply orders, but the primary work of repairing buildings and machinery remains a hands-on human requirement.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical intervention in the built environment, which cannot be digitized or executed by software agents. While the work requires troubleshooting, it is constrained by highly unstructured, volatile physical environments (e.g., cramped crawlspaces, aging infrastructure) that necessitate human dexterity and real-time physical adaptation.

rows.title: Wholesale and manufacturing sales representatives
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is heavily focused on information processing, communication, and data analysis, all of which are highly susceptible to AI enhancement and automation. While high-stakes B2B negotiations and physical trade shows provide a buffer, AI can now handle lead generation, personalized outreach, price optimization, and technical product explanations, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of representatives needed.
rows.new_rationale: While the administrative and transactional components of sales are highly susceptible to digital automation (agentic potential), the core value remains deeply rooted in human-to-human relationship building, trust, and persuasion, which are difficult to replicate via software. The environment is generally structured (offices, trade shows), but the necessity for human social navigation and the management of complex, high-stakes B2B relationships keeps the ontological human necessity score high.

rows.title: Bookkeeping, accounting, and auditing clerks
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital and involves routine information processing, data entry, and mathematical verification—tasks that AI and automated software already perform with high accuracy. The BLS already projects a decline in employment due to technological change, and the shift toward 'analytical and advisory' roles is increasingly being encroached upon by advanced LLMs and specialized financial AI agents.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation is entirely digital (financial records, reports, and ledger entries), making it highly susceptible to end-to-end execution by autonomous software agents. While the work requires attention to detail and basic reconciliation, it lacks high-dimensional synthesis or physical unpredictability, as the environment is almost exclusively a controlled digital interface.

rows.title: Accountants and auditors
rows.old_rationale: Accountancy is a fundamentally digital occupation centered on data analysis, regulatory compliance, and reporting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI and robotic process automation. While high-level advisory roles and complex forensic audits require human judgment and relationship management, the core tasks of data organization, tax computation, and anomaly detection are increasingly being automated, significantly increasing worker productivity and restructuring the entry-level labor market.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of accounting—financial statements, tax filings, and audit reports—is inherently digital and increasingly executable by software agents, leading to high agentic potential. However, the profession remains anchored by high systemic accountability, as CPAs must legally sign off on documents and assume liability for accuracy, and by the ontological necessity of human trust and professional judgment in complex, high-stakes advisory and forensic scenarios.

rows.title: Information clerks
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core tasks—processing routine data, answering inquiries, and maintaining records—are fundamentally digital and information-based. While some roles like hotel desk clerks or airport agents require a physical presence, the majority of the work involves routine information processing that is increasingly being automated by AI-driven self-service systems, chatbots, and automated data entry tools.
rows.new_rationale: The vast majority of tasks performed by information clerks—data entry, record maintenance, scheduling, and processing inquiries—are inherently digital and can be executed end-to-end by software agents. While some roles involve physical interaction (like hotel check-ins or handling luggage), the core value proposition is informational and administrative, making it highly susceptible to automation despite the occasional need for human-to-human interface.

rows.title: Security guards and gambling surveillance officers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical presence and digital monitoring. While AI-powered computer vision is rapidly automating the 'surveillance' and 'monitoring' aspects of the job, the core requirements of physical patrolling, detaining suspects, and responding to real-world emergencies provide a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While surveillance and monitoring tasks are increasingly digital and AI-executable, the role retains a high requirement for physical presence, de-escalation, and real-time intervention in unpredictable, unstructured environments. The systemic accountability score reflects the legal and ethical weight of detaining individuals or managing life-safety emergencies, which necessitates human judgment and liability that cannot be fully offloaded to software.

rows.title: Financial clerks
rows.old_rationale: Financial clerks perform routine, digital-first tasks such as data entry, record updating, and basic financial calculations that are highly susceptible to AI automation. While some roles involve customer interaction, the core functions—processing claims, verifying credit, and managing payroll—are increasingly handled by AI-driven software, leading to a projected decline in employment.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of financial clerks is almost entirely digital—records, transactions, and billing entries—making them highly susceptible to end-to-end automation via software agents. The work is performed in highly structured office environments with low cognitive complexity, as tasks are largely procedural and governed by predetermined rules that AI is already optimized to execute.

rows.title: Receptionists
rows.old_rationale: Many core tasks such as scheduling, answering phones, and data entry are highly susceptible to automation via AI voice agents and chatbots. While the role retains a physical component for greeting and security, the BLS already projects zero growth due to automation, and the digital nature of the administrative workload makes it highly exposed to further restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: The core functions of a receptionist—scheduling, data entry, and information routing—are almost entirely digital and can be executed end-to-end by software agents. While there is a minor human element in greeting visitors, the work occurs in highly controlled, predictable office environments that require minimal complex synthesis.

rows.title: Metal and plastic machine workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of the work is physical and requires presence on a factory floor to handle materials and maintain machinery. However, AI and advanced automation are increasingly capable of handling the digital aspects of the job, such as CNC programming, machine monitoring, and quality inspection, which is already contributing to a projected decline in employment.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical transformation of raw materials into tangible goods, which cannot be executed by software alone regardless of how much the process is digitized. While the work environment is generally controlled, it involves high-stakes physical variables like heavy machinery, molten materials, and hazardous conditions that require human presence for safety monitoring and physical intervention.

rows.title: Carpenters
rows.old_rationale: Carpentry is a highly physical occupation requiring manual dexterity, strength, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable physical environments. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis, material estimation, or business management, the core work of cutting, fitting, and installing materials remains resistant to automation.
rows.new_rationale: Carpentry is fundamentally a physical trade involving the manipulation of materials in unstructured, variable environments, making it highly resistant to digital agentic execution. While the cognitive aspects of planning and measurement are complex, the core value lies in the physical installation and structural integrity of the built environment, which carries significant legal and safety liability that cannot be delegated to software.

rows.title: Market research analysts
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this role—data collection, statistical analysis, trend forecasting, and report generation—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with AI's strengths in processing large datasets and generating insights. While human judgment is still needed for high-level strategy and client relationships, AI can now automate the vast majority of the technical and administrative tasks that define the daily work of an analyst.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—data analysis, trend forecasting, and report generation—is entirely digital and increasingly executable by AI agents that can process big data sets autonomously. While the role requires high-level synthesis, the work environment is highly controlled and digital, leaving little room for physical unpredictability or the necessity of a human-to-human bond for the primary product.

rows.title: Material moving machine operators
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job is physical operation of heavy machinery in dynamic environments like warehouses and construction sites, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, AI-driven computer vision and autonomous navigation are increasingly being integrated into forklifts and conveyor systems, leading to moderate exposure as these technologies augment or replace human operators in structured settings.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of heavy objects in real-world environments, which cannot be executed by software alone. While the tasks are procedural, the high-stakes physical environment—such as operating cranes near workers or navigating construction sites—requires human-in-the-loop safety and liability, making full autonomous delegation difficult and legally fraught.

rows.title: Farmers, ranchers, and other agricultural managers
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical labor, equipment maintenance, and real-time management of biological systems that cannot be digitized. However, AI will heavily impact the management side of the job, including crop yield prediction, financial planning, and automated resource allocation, making individual managers significantly more productive and contributing to further farm consolidation.
rows.new_rationale: While agricultural management involves complex data-driven decision-making that AI can assist with, the core value remains the stewardship of biological systems in highly volatile, unstructured physical environments where software cannot execute the final physical outcomes (e.g., animal husbandry, soil management). The high systemic accountability stems from the critical nature of food production and the financial/legal liability inherent in managing large-scale land and livestock operations.

rows.title: Computer and information systems managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, involving strategic planning, analysis, and technical oversight that AI can significantly augment. While high-level leadership, vendor negotiation, and personnel management provide a buffer of human judgment, the core tasks of assessing IT needs, monitoring security, and directing technical workflows are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is digital strategy, system architecture, and resource allocation, all of which are highly susceptible to AI-driven execution and optimization. While the role requires high-level human judgment for organizational politics and accountability, the actual 'work' of managing IT infrastructure is increasingly becoming a software-defined, agentic process.

rows.title: Barbers, hairstylists, and cosmetologists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves manual dexterity and physical interaction in a highly unpredictable environment (human hair and skin). While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like appointment scheduling, marketing, or visualizing hairstyles for clients, the physical act of cutting, coloring, and styling hair remains a significant barrier to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is a physical, in-person service that requires delicate manipulation of a human client, making it highly resistant to digital agentic execution. While the work involves aesthetic judgment, the primary product is the human-to-human interaction and the physical trust required to perform services on a client's body, which cannot be delegated to software.

rows.title: Medical and health services managers
rows.old_rationale: This role involves a high volume of digital knowledge work, such as budgeting, scheduling, and regulatory compliance, which are highly susceptible to AI optimization. However, the core responsibilities of recruiting, supervising staff, and navigating complex interpersonal relationships within a physical healthcare facility provide a significant buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the administrative and analytical output (budgeting, scheduling, reporting) is highly susceptible to digital automation, the role remains anchored in the management of human teams and the navigation of high-stakes, real-world clinical environments. The necessity for human leadership, ethical oversight in patient care, and legal accountability for facility-wide outcomes creates a significant barrier to full agentic replacement.

rows.title: Preschool teachers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical care, emotional support, and real-time social interaction with children under age five, which AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like lesson planning, curriculum design, and progress reporting, the primary job requires physical presence and human empathy to manage safety and developmental needs.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical, emotional, and social development of children, which requires a constant, in-person human presence that cannot be replaced by digital artifacts. While curriculum planning involves cognitive synthesis, the execution is entirely dependent on managing the highly unpredictable, real-time physical environment of a classroom, making the role fundamentally resistant to agentic automation.

rows.title: Construction equipment operators
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical operation of heavy machinery in unpredictable, outdoor environments that require real-time human coordination and manual maintenance. While AI and automation are increasingly integrated into equipment via GPS and computerized controls, the physical nature of the work and the need for on-site safety judgment provide a significant barrier to full automation in the near term.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of the environment, which is highly resistant to digital-only agentic execution. While AI can assist with navigation or precision, the work occurs in highly unstructured, high-stakes physical environments where real-time, site-specific judgment and physical liability for safety-critical operations remain inherently human-centric.

rows.title: Securities, commodities, and financial services sales agents
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital and information-based, involving data analysis, market monitoring, and financial modeling—all areas where AI is rapidly achieving parity with human experts. While high-level relationship management and complex negotiation (like M&A) provide some insulation, the core tasks of identifying investment opportunities, executing trades, and providing routine financial advice are highly susceptible to automation and significant productivity gains that could reduce the need for human agents.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is digital—executing trades, managing portfolios, and structuring financial agreements—which is increasingly handled by software and algorithmic agents. While the work requires high-level synthesis and carries significant legal and fiduciary accountability, the physical environment is highly controlled, and the human necessity is primarily centered on trust, relationship management, and regulatory liability rather than physical presence.

rows.title: Postal service workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, such as delivering parcels and navigating outdoor environments, which provides a natural barrier to AI. However, AI and advanced automation are significantly impacting the sorting and processing components of the job, while the shift toward digital communication reduces overall mail volume and labor demand.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical movement and delivery of tangible goods, which remains largely outside the reach of digital-only agentic execution. While sorting and processing are increasingly automated, the 'last mile' delivery involves navigating unstructured, real-world environments and physical handling that requires human dexterity and adaptability.

rows.title: Welders, cutters, solderers, and brazers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, physical stamina, and real-time adaptation to unpredictable environments like construction sites or repair shops. While AI and robotics can automate routine assembly-line welding, the specialized, non-routine tasks and the need for physical presence in varied locations provide a strong barrier against AI displacement.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation is a physical, structural bond in the real world, which cannot be digitized or executed by software agents. While the cognitive tasks involve blueprint interpretation, the primary challenge is navigating highly unstructured, hazardous, and variable physical environments where the welder bears significant legal and safety accountability for the structural integrity of the work.

rows.title: Taxi drivers, shuttle drivers, and chauffeurs
rows.old_rationale: While the core task of driving is highly susceptible to automation through autonomous vehicle technology (AI), the job currently requires significant physical presence for tasks like loading luggage, assisting passengers with disabilities, and performing vehicle maintenance. The transition to fully driverless fleets faces regulatory and technical hurdles in complex environments, but the long-term trajectory for this digital-adjacent physical task is one of high potential displacement.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation—transporting a passenger from point A to point B—is highly susceptible to automation via autonomous vehicle software, which acts as a direct digital command for physical movement. While the physical environment (traffic, weather, road hazards) remains unstructured and unpredictable, the task itself is increasingly being offloaded to AI systems, leaving only a residual need for human assistance with luggage or specialized care for passengers with disabilities.

rows.title: Administrative services and facilities managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical site management. While AI can significantly automate administrative tasks like recordkeeping, supply procurement, and energy pattern analysis, the role requires a physical presence to inspect facilities, oversee manual labor (janitors, repair workers), and manage real-world safety and security compliance.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative and recordkeeping tasks have high digital potential, the core value of this role—facilities management—requires physical oversight, on-site problem solving, and the coordination of human staff in unpredictable, non-digital environments. The role demands significant human judgment for safety, regulatory compliance, and personnel management, which creates a high barrier to full agentic replacement despite the availability of software-assisted planning tools.

rows.title: Compliance officers
rows.old_rationale: Compliance work is primarily digital and involves analyzing vast amounts of regulatory text, auditing data, and generating reports—all areas where LLMs and automated systems excel. While high-level judgment and interpersonal investigations provide some insulation, the core tasks of monitoring, risk assessment, and documentation are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and productivity gains.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a compliance officer—reports, policy documentation, and risk assessments—is almost entirely digital and can be generated or executed by AI agents. While the role requires high-level synthesis of complex regulations, the primary barrier to full automation is not the physical environment, but the systemic accountability and legal liability inherent in certifying that an organization is compliant with the law.

rows.title: Dental assistants
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical, hands-on tasks such as sterilizing instruments, assisting in surgeries, and providing direct patient care that requires human presence and manual dexterity. While AI can significantly automate the administrative and digital aspects of the role—such as scheduling, billing, and preliminary X-ray analysis—the majority of the workday is spent in the physical dental operatory where AI cannot currently replace human labor.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical, in-person assistance within a highly variable, sensitive environment (the human oral cavity), which cannot be replaced by digital agents. While administrative tasks are digital, the primary function requires real-time physical dexterity, infection control, and the management of patient anxiety, necessitating a human presence for both safety and emotional support.

rows.title: Paralegals and legal assistants
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation—legal research, document drafting, and information organization—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with the strengths of Large Language Models. While some interpersonal and courtroom logistics remain, AI can already automate or significantly accelerate the vast majority of a paralegal's workload, leading to the BLS's projection of 0% job growth despite high demand for legal services.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a paralegal—legal research, document drafting, and case organization—is almost entirely digital and highly susceptible to end-to-end execution by AI agents. While the work requires high-level synthesis of legal information, it occurs in a highly controlled office environment, and the ultimate legal liability and ethical responsibility remain with the attorney, limiting the necessity for human-in-the-loop intervention for the majority of tasks.

rows.title: Fitness trainers and instructors
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation requires physical presence to demonstrate exercises, correct form in real-time to prevent injury, and provide high-touch motivational support. While AI can automate peripheral tasks like workout programming, nutrition planning, and social media marketing, it cannot replace the physical safety monitoring and interpersonal accountability that define the role.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can generate workout plans and track data, the core value of this role is the physical, in-person demonstration of technique, real-time safety monitoring, and the human-to-human motivational bond. The necessity for physical presence to prevent injury and provide emotional encouragement makes the output largely non-agentic, despite the availability of digital fitness content.

rows.title: Claims adjusters, appraisers, examiners, and investigators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is heavily focused on information processing, document review, and data-driven decision-making, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While physical inspections and interpersonal negotiations provide some protection, the BLS already projects a decline in employment due to AI's ability to analyze photos of damage and automate claim calculations.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—the determination of claim validity and settlement amounts—is increasingly digital and data-driven, making it highly susceptible to AI automation. While physical inspections of damaged property introduce some environmental unpredictability, the primary value lies in the synthesis of documentation and policy logic, which can be executed by software agents, leaving only high-stakes, contentious, or fraudulent cases requiring human intervention and accountability.

rows.title: Machinists and tool and die makers
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant physical component, including manual dexterity, machine setup, and material handling in a factory environment. However, AI and advanced automation are increasingly capable of optimizing CNC programming, interpreting CAD designs, and monitoring machine health, which increases worker productivity and reduces the demand for traditional tool and die making skills.
rows.new_rationale: While the design phase (CAD/CAM) is digital, the core value of this occupation is the physical transformation of raw materials into precision parts, which requires physical manipulation and real-world verification that software cannot currently execute end-to-end. The work involves significant systemic accountability, as errors in precision components can lead to catastrophic mechanical failure in high-stakes industries like aerospace or medical manufacturing.

rows.title: Food service managers
rows.old_rationale: While AI can significantly automate administrative tasks like staff scheduling, inventory ordering, and payroll management, the core of the job requires physical presence and real-time human interaction. Managers must physically inspect facilities for safety, handle unpredictable interpersonal conflicts with staff and customers, and perform manual labor during peak hours, which provides a strong buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like scheduling and budgeting have high digital potential, the core of the role is tied to the physical, high-stakes, and unpredictable environment of a commercial kitchen and dining room. The manager's value is heavily derived from real-time, in-person leadership, conflict resolution, and physical oversight of safety and service, which cannot be delegated to software or remote agents.

rows.title: Clinical laboratory technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical laboratory work and digital data analysis. While AI and computer vision are rapidly automating the interpretation of slides and test results (high exposure), the physical requirements of preparing specimens, maintaining complex hardware, and interacting with patients provide a significant barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the diagnostic output is increasingly digital and automated, the role remains tethered to physical specimen handling, equipment calibration, and the high-stakes responsibility of ensuring accuracy in life-altering medical results. The necessity for human oversight is driven primarily by systemic accountability and the need for expert validation of complex, non-routine biological anomalies that automated systems may misinterpret.

rows.title: Tellers
rows.old_rationale: Tellers face high exposure because their core tasks—processing routine financial transactions and verifying information—are digital in nature and highly susceptible to automation through AI and advanced self-service technology. While the physical handling of cash provides a slight buffer, the rapid shift toward mobile banking, AI-driven fraud detection, and interactive video kiosks is significantly reducing the need for human intervention in these roles.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a teller is the digital recording of financial transactions and the verification of data, which is already largely handled by software and automated kiosks. Because the environment is highly controlled and the tasks are procedural rather than novel, the role is exceptionally vulnerable to end-to-end automation.

rows.title: Recreation workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical presence, manual instruction, and real-time interpersonal interaction in unpredictable environments like camps and community centers. While AI can assist with peripheral administrative tasks like scheduling, program planning, and creating newsletters, it cannot replace the physical supervision, safety monitoring, and emotional leadership required to manage group activities.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is the physical, in-person facilitation of human interaction, safety, and motivation, which cannot be digitized or executed by software. While administrative tasks are minor, the primary work involves navigating unpredictable physical environments and providing the human presence necessary for social, emotional, and physical engagement.

rows.title: Public relations specialists
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of this occupation—writing press releases, drafting speeches, creating social media content, and monitoring public sentiment—are digital-first and align perfectly with the capabilities of Large Language Models. While high-level strategy and real-time interpersonal crisis management provide some protection, the vast majority of the daily work product can be generated or significantly optimized by AI, leading to high exposure and potential workforce consolidation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—press releases, social media content, and strategic messaging—is entirely digital and can be generated and disseminated by AI agents. While the job requires high-level synthesis of public sentiment and organizational strategy, the work environment is highly controlled and office-based, making it a prime candidate for automation despite the residual need for human-to-human trust in high-stakes crisis management.

rows.title: Coaches and scouts
rows.old_rationale: While AI is significantly impacting the analytical side of the job—such as processing player data, optimizing game strategy, and analyzing opponent film—the core of the occupation remains deeply physical and interpersonal. Coaches must provide real-time motivation, physical instruction, and human leadership on the field, which cannot be replicated by digital tools.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can synthesize vast amounts of performance data to suggest strategies or identify talent, the core value of coaching is the human-to-human mentorship, motivation, and physical instruction of athletes, which cannot be delegated to software. The environment is highly unpredictable due to the nature of competitive sports and human psychology, requiring real-time, in-person adaptation that remains firmly in the human domain.

rows.title: Masonry workers
rows.old_rationale: Masonry is a highly physical trade performed in unpredictable outdoor environments that require manual dexterity, strength, and real-time sensory feedback. While AI and robotics may assist with blueprint analysis or specialized brick-laying machines in controlled settings, the core tasks of navigating muddy sites, handling heavy materials, and finishing surfaces remain resistant to digital automation.
rows.new_rationale: Masonry is a fundamentally physical trade requiring real-time interaction with unstructured, variable environments (e.g., uneven surfaces, weather, site-specific constraints) that cannot be digitized or executed by software. While the cognitive aspects of planning are complex, the core value is the physical manipulation of materials, and the high systemic accountability stems from the critical safety and structural integrity requirements of the built environment.

rows.title: EMTs and paramedics
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical intervention, manual dexterity, and real-time human presence in unpredictable environments, which are highly resistant to AI. While AI will significantly assist with peripheral tasks like EKG interpretation, diagnostic decision support, and automated documentation, the physical requirements of patient transport and hands-on emergency care remain essential human functions.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical, real-time intervention in highly volatile, unstructured environments where the primary output is the preservation of human life. While AI can assist in diagnostic synthesis, the necessity for physical manipulation, human empathy in crisis, and the non-delegatable legal and ethical liability for life-or-death decisions makes this role highly resistant to agentic automation.

rows.title: Radiologic and MRI technologists
rows.old_rationale: While AI is revolutionizing image analysis and quality control, the core of this role requires physical presence to position patients, administer contrast agents, and operate heavy machinery in a clinical setting. AI will significantly enhance productivity and image evaluation, but the physical and interpersonal requirements of patient care provide a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the final output (the image) is digital, the core value of the role is the physical positioning, safety monitoring, and stabilization of human patients, which cannot be delegated to software. The high scores for human necessity and accountability reflect the critical, high-stakes nature of patient care, where the technologist must manage unpredictable human variables and assume legal liability for patient safety and radiation exposure.

rows.title: Physical therapists
rows.old_rationale: The core of physical therapy involves manual manipulation, physical assistance, and real-time observation of human movement in a physical environment, which AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist with administrative tasks, medical history review, and generating personalized exercise plans, the high level of physical dexterity and interpersonal empathy required limits its overall exposure.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of physical therapy is rooted in physical, hands-on manipulation and the human-to-human therapeutic bond, which cannot be digitized or delegated to software. While the cognitive synthesis required to diagnose and adapt treatment plans is high, the execution remains a physical, high-stakes interaction that requires human accountability for patient safety and recovery outcomes.

rows.title: Graphic designers
rows.old_rationale: Graphic design is a fundamentally digital occupation where core tasks—image generation, layout creation, and photo editing—are being rapidly transformed by generative AI. While high-level creative direction and client management remain human-centric, the automation of production tasks significantly increases individual productivity, likely reducing the total number of designers needed for routine commercial work.
rows.new_rationale: Graphic design output is almost entirely digital, making it highly susceptible to end-to-end execution by generative AI agents. While the role requires creative synthesis and client communication, the core production of visual assets and layouts is increasingly automated, leaving little room for physical or high-stakes accountability constraints.

rows.title: Data scientists
rows.old_rationale: Data science is a fully digital occupation centered on coding, statistical modeling, and data analysis—all areas where AI is rapidly achieving parity or superiority. While human judgment is still needed for business context and ethical oversight, AI can now automate significant portions of the data pipeline, including cleaning raw data, generating complex code, and even suggesting optimal model architectures.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a data scientist is digital—code, models, and analytical reports—which is highly susceptible to automation by AI agents that can perform data cleaning, algorithm selection, and visualization. While the role requires high-level cognitive synthesis to frame business problems, the work occurs in a highly controlled digital environment, and the human necessity is largely limited to stakeholder communication and final ethical oversight of automated decisions.

rows.title: Career and technical education teachers
rows.old_rationale: While AI can significantly automate lesson planning, grading, and the delivery of theoretical knowledge, the core of this occupation involves supervising hands-on activities in physical environments like workshops and labs. Teaching students to safely use physical tools for trades like welding, culinary arts, or auto repair requires real-time human presence and physical intervention that AI cannot currently replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While lesson planning and curriculum design can be digitized, the core value of this role is the physical, in-person supervision of students performing high-stakes manual tasks (e.g., welding, automotive repair), which requires real-time human intervention for safety and skill development. The teacher's role is fundamentally rooted in human mentorship, moral authority, and legal liability for student safety in unpredictable, hands-on workshop environments.

rows.title: Instructional coordinators
rows.old_rationale: Instructional coordinators perform high-level knowledge work including curriculum development, data analysis, and educational material review, all of which are digital-first tasks highly susceptible to AI augmentation. While the role requires significant interpersonal interaction for teacher coaching and stakeholder management, the core 'product'—curricula and instructional strategies—can be generated and optimized much faster using generative AI and advanced analytics.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the curriculum design and data analysis can be digitized and automated, the core value of the role relies on high-stakes human mentorship, consensus-building among staff, and navigating the complex political and social dynamics of school environments. The necessity for human-to-human trust and professional accountability in shaping educational outcomes limits the extent to which an autonomous agent can fully replace the coordinator's role.

rows.title: Postsecondary education administrators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is heavily centered on digital information processing, including reviewing applications, analyzing student data, managing budgets, and maintaining academic records—all tasks where AI can significantly increase productivity or automate routine workflows. While roles like deans and student affairs officers require high-level human judgment and interpersonal relationship management, the underlying administrative and analytical work is highly susceptible to AI integration and restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: Much of the administrative work—such as scheduling, record-keeping, and data analysis—is highly digital and ripe for agentic automation. However, the role remains anchored by the necessity for human-to-human interaction in student advising, conflict resolution, and high-stakes institutional decision-making (e.g., tenure and admissions policy), where human judgment and ethical accountability are non-delegatable.

rows.title: Human resources managers
rows.old_rationale: Human resources management is a digital-heavy knowledge occupation where AI can significantly automate recruitment screening, payroll processing, and regulatory compliance reporting. While the role requires high-level interpersonal skills for dispute mediation and strategic leadership, the vast majority of the administrative and analytical tasks are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the administrative output (payroll, compliance reporting, recruitment screening) is highly digitizable and suitable for agentic execution, the core value of the role relies on high-stakes human mediation, conflict resolution, and ethical judgment. The necessity for human-to-human trust and the legal/moral accountability inherent in managing personnel and labor relations creates a significant barrier to full automation, despite the high potential for software to handle the underlying data and strategic synthesis.

rows.title: Dental hygienists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves high-precision manual dexterity and physical procedures performed on patients in a clinical setting, which AI cannot replicate. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like analyzing X-rays or automating patient documentation, the primary work of cleaning teeth and conducting physical exams remains insulated from automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of dental hygiene is physical, in-person manipulation of oral tissue, which cannot be digitized or executed by software agents. While the work requires clinical judgment, the primary barrier to automation is the necessity for human dexterity in a highly sensitive, variable biological environment and the requirement for a human-to-human bond to manage patient anxiety and provide ethical, accountable care.

rows.title: Woodworkers
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical manipulation of materials, manual dexterity, and real-time sensory monitoring in a workshop environment, which are resistant to AI. While AI can optimize digital design (CAD) and CNC programming, the physical assembly, finishing, and machine maintenance require a human presence that limits overall exposure.
rows.new_rationale: While CNC machines handle the digital-to-physical translation of designs, the core value of the occupation remains the physical manipulation, assembly, and finishing of material, which cannot be executed by software alone. The environment is physically hazardous and requires real-time, tactile adjustments to material inconsistencies that remain difficult for autonomous agents to navigate without significant human intervention.

rows.title: Medical records specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, involving the processing, classification, and entry of data into electronic systems. AI is already highly capable of natural language processing and automated medical coding, which directly automates the core tasks of reviewing records and assigning clinical codes, significantly reducing the need for human intervention.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is digital data entry and classification, which is highly susceptible to automation via AI agents that can interpret clinical notes and assign standardized codes. While the role requires accuracy and adherence to privacy laws, the work occurs in a highly controlled, digital environment, making it a prime candidate for end-to-end software execution.

rows.title: Automotive body and glass repairers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks are highly physical, involving manual dexterity, welding, and the use of pneumatic tools in unpredictable repair environments that are difficult to automate. AI's primary impact is limited to peripheral administrative tasks like cost estimation and damage assessment via computer vision, but the physical restoration of vehicles remains a human-centric trade.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of heavy, damaged, and non-standardized objects, which remains highly resistant to digital agentic execution. While the work requires diagnostic skill, the primary challenge is navigating the unstructured and volatile physical environment of collision damage, where every repair presents unique structural variables that require human dexterity and physical force.

rows.title: Drafters
rows.old_rationale: Drafters perform work that is almost entirely digital, converting conceptual sketches into technical CAD and BIM models. AI is rapidly advancing in generative design and automated technical drawing, allowing for the immediate translation of engineering specifications into precise models, which significantly reduces the human labor required for these tasks.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a drafter is a digital CAD/BIM file, which is inherently compatible with generative AI and automated design agents. While the work requires technical precision and collaboration, the primary value is the creation of digital schematics, making it highly susceptible to end-to-end automation, though human oversight remains necessary for final liability and sign-off on structural integrity.

rows.title: Psychiatric technicians and aides
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical tasks such as bathing, feeding, and physically restraining patients, which AI cannot perform. While AI can assist with monitoring patient behavior and automating documentation or reporting, the high requirement for real-time human presence, physical stamina, and interpersonal compassion creates a significant barrier to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical, in-person care, patient monitoring, and crisis intervention, which cannot be digitized or executed via software. The environment is highly unpredictable due to the potential for patient violence and the need for real-time, empathetic human judgment, making the role fundamentally resistant to AI-driven automation.

rows.title: Information security analysts
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is entirely digital, involving tasks like monitoring networks, analyzing vulnerabilities, and writing reports that are highly susceptible to AI automation. While AI increases the demand for security to counter AI-driven threats, it also significantly automates the core functions of threat detection, log analysis, and incident response, drastically increasing individual worker productivity.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is entirely digital—code, configuration changes, and security policies—making it highly susceptible to agentic execution.
