While the cognitive complexity is high due to the adversarial nature of cybersecurity, the primary barrier to full automation is not physical, but rather the high-stakes systemic accountability and the legal/ethical necessity for human oversight in critical infrastructure protection.

rows.title: Musicians and singers
rows.old_rationale: While AI can generate high-quality digital music and vocals, a significant portion of this occupation involves live performance, physical presence at venues, and human-centric events like religious services and concerts. The 'human element' and the physical act of playing an instrument or singing in real-time provide a natural barrier, though AI will increasingly compete for studio/session work and background music production.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can generate high-quality musical artifacts, the core value of this occupation is the 'live' human performance and the shared physical experience between performer and audience, which cannot be replaced by digital output. The high cognitive complexity stems from the creative synthesis required for artistic expression, but the role lacks systemic accountability as the stakes are primarily cultural and aesthetic rather than life-critical or legal.

rows.title: Massage therapists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring manual manipulation of soft tissues and real-time human empathy in a physical setting. While AI can assist with peripheral administrative tasks like scheduling, marketing, and documentation, it cannot replace the tactile skill and physical presence required for massage therapy.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of massage therapy is the physical, tactile manipulation of human soft tissue, which cannot be digitized or executed by software. While the profession requires diagnostic assessment and personalized treatment planning, the primary product is a human-to-human interaction rooted in physical presence, empathy, and the trust required for intimate, hands-on care.

rows.title: Bill and account collectors
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, consisting of data analysis, skip tracing, and communication via phone or computer. AI and automated systems are already driving a projected 10% decline in employment by handling routine notifications and payment processing, while advanced LLMs are increasingly capable of managing the negotiation and customer service aspects of debt collection.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is digital—updating records, initiating payment plans, and executing communication—all of which are highly amenable to autonomous agents. While the job requires basic negotiation, the environment is a highly controlled, sedentary office or remote setting, and the human element is largely functional rather than deeply relational or irreplaceable.

rows.title: Roofers
rows.old_rationale: Roofing is a highly physical occupation performed in unpredictable outdoor environments that require manual dexterity, balance, and heavy lifting. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like drone-based inspections or material estimation, the core work of installing and repairing shingles and membranes remains entirely dependent on human labor.
rows.new_rationale: Roofing is a fundamentally physical trade requiring real-time navigation of unstructured, high-stakes environments (steep slopes, heights, weather) that cannot be digitized or executed by software. While the work requires precision and planning, the core value is the physical installation and repair of building envelopes, which remains beyond the reach of autonomous agents.

rows.title: Library technicians and assistants
rows.old_rationale: A significant portion of this role involves routine information processing, cataloging, and answering reference questions, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While physical tasks like reshelving books and interpersonal community programming provide some protection, the core digital and clerical functions are increasingly being handled by automated systems and AI-driven search tools.
rows.new_rationale: The core functions of this role—cataloging, database management, and information retrieval—are highly digital and increasingly handled by automated library systems and AI-driven search interfaces, giving it high agentic potential. The work environment is highly structured and predictable, and while human interaction is a component of the service, it does not require the high-stakes moral or professional accountability that would necessitate a human-in-the-loop for every transaction.

rows.title: Occupational therapists
rows.old_rationale: Occupational therapy is a highly physical and interpersonal profession that requires real-time observation, manual manipulation of patients, and the assessment of physical environments like homes and workplaces. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks such as treatment planning, progress documentation, and medical history analysis, the core of the job involves hands-on rehabilitation and emotional support that cannot be replicated digitally.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of occupational therapy is physical, in-person rehabilitation and the development of a therapeutic human bond, which cannot be digitized or executed by software. While the cognitive synthesis required to design treatment plans is high, the execution is inherently tied to the physical, unpredictable, and high-stakes environment of patient care, where human empathy and legal accountability for patient safety are non-delegatable.

rows.title: Physical therapist assistants and aides
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical, hands-on work such as massage, stretching, and assisting patients with mobility, which AI cannot perform. While AI can streamline peripheral tasks like scheduling, documentation, and exercise plan generation, the requirement for real-time human presence and physical dexterity creates a strong barrier to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical, hands-on patient care and manual therapy, which cannot be digitized or executed by software agents. While the work involves following a plan of care, the physical interaction with patients in varying states of health requires high levels of human empathy and real-time physical judgment that remains fundamentally human-centric.

rows.title: Photographers
rows.old_rationale: Photography is a hybrid role where the physical act of capturing real-world events (weddings, news, portraits) provides a buffer, but the digital output is highly vulnerable to AI. Generative AI can now create high-quality commercial and stock imagery from scratch and automate complex post-production tasks, significantly reducing the billable hours and demand for professional photographers in commercial and creative sectors.
rows.new_rationale: While photography involves physical presence and environmental navigation, the core output is increasingly digital, and generative AI is rapidly encroaching on the synthesis of aesthetic composition and post-production editing. The role retains a moderate human necessity for client interaction and directing subjects, but the technical execution of the final image is highly susceptible to digital automation.

rows.title: Dentists
rows.old_rationale: The core of dentistry involves highly precise physical procedures, manual dexterity, and real-time patient interaction in a clinical setting, which are resistant to AI automation. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy through X-ray analysis and streamline administrative practice management, the physical requirement of performing surgery and restorative work creates a strong barrier to high exposure.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of dentistry is the physical, real-time manipulation of human anatomy, which cannot be digitized or offloaded to software agents. While diagnostic and planning tasks involve high cognitive complexity, the execution requires navigating the unpredictable, high-stakes biological environment of a patient's mouth, necessitating human presence, empathy, and absolute professional liability for physical outcomes.

rows.title: Broadcast, sound, and video technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical labor (setting up hardware, rigging lights, running cables) and digital knowledge work (mixing sound, editing video, managing broadcast signals). While the physical installation tasks are resistant to AI, the digital components—such as sound leveling, video editing, and signal monitoring—are being rapidly automated by AI tools, leading to higher productivity and a projected decline in specialized roles like sound and broadcast technicians.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the signal processing and editing can be digitized, the core of this role involves physical installation, rigging, and real-time troubleshooting in varied, often unpredictable physical environments like concert venues or outdoor broadcast sites. The necessity for human intervention remains high due to the physical nature of the equipment setup and the need for immediate, on-site physical adjustments that current robotics cannot reliably perform.

rows.title: Database administrators and architects
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is entirely digital, involving coding (SQL), system architecture, and data management—all areas where AI is exceptionally proficient. AI can already automate routine DBA tasks like performance tuning, query optimization, and security monitoring, while LLMs are highly capable of generating and debugging the complex schemas and scripts used by database architects.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is entirely digital—code, schema, and configuration—making it highly susceptible to autonomous agent execution. While the work requires high-level architectural synthesis and carries significant systemic accountability for data security and integrity, the environment is almost exclusively virtual and controlled, minimizing the need for human physical presence or intervention.

rows.title: Butchers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves manual dexterity, physical strength, and real-time interaction with physical products in a cold, unpredictable environment. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like inventory tracking and sales forecasting, the primary duties of cutting, deboning, and handling meat are resistant to digital automation and require specialized robotics rather than AI software.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of a butcher is the physical manipulation of biological material, which requires high-dexterity, real-time adjustments to irregular carcasses that cannot be digitized or executed via software. While inventory management is digital, the primary labor is physical and environmental, occurring in cold, hazardous, and unstructured settings where human judgment regarding food safety and quality remains critical.

rows.title: Surgical assistants and technologists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, sterile technique, and real-time presence in an operating room. While AI and robotics can assist with surgical precision or inventory management, the role remains centered on hands-on patient care and physical equipment handling that cannot be digitized.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical, real-time intervention in a high-stakes, unpredictable biological environment where the human presence is essential for patient safety and sterile integrity. Because the output is a physical surgical outcome rather than a digital artifact, and the role carries profound legal and ethical liability for life-critical errors, it remains highly resistant to autonomous agent replacement.

rows.title: Phlebotomists
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job is a highly tactile, physical procedure (venipuncture) performed on human patients in unpredictable clinical settings, which is resistant to AI automation. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like data entry, sample tracking, and scheduling, the primary value of the role lies in manual dexterity and providing interpersonal emotional support to anxious patients.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this job is a physical, invasive medical procedure that requires high-precision manual dexterity and real-time adjustment to patient physiology, which cannot be delegated to software. While data entry is digital, the primary output is the successful collection of a biological sample, and the human-to-human interaction is critical for managing patient anxiety and ensuring safety in a clinical environment.

rows.title: Writers and authors
rows.old_rationale: The core work product of writers and authors is entirely digital and text-based, which aligns perfectly with the primary capabilities of Large Language Models. AI can now generate drafts, conduct research, and perform editing tasks across various formats like blogs, copy, and scripts, significantly increasing productivity while simultaneously threatening to automate many entry-level and routine writing roles.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this profession is digital text, which is highly susceptible to end-to-end generation by AI agents. While high-level creative synthesis and narrative architecture remain complex, the physical and environmental constraints are virtually non-existent, and the role lacks the high-stakes, non-delegatable legal or physical accountability found in critical infrastructure or life-safety professions.

rows.title: Art directors
rows.old_rationale: Art directors work in a fundamentally digital domain where AI is rapidly advancing in image generation, layout design, and video synthesis. While the role requires high-level human judgment, leadership, and client communication, the core work product can be heavily augmented or automated by AI, significantly increasing productivity and potentially reducing the number of directors needed to oversee creative projects.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of an art director is a visual concept or design direction, which is increasingly digital and can be generated or iterated upon by AI agents. While the role requires high-level creative synthesis and human-to-human negotiation to align client vision with artistic execution, the physical environment is largely controlled, and the 'accountability' is primarily professional rather than life-critical.

rows.title: Fundraisers
rows.old_rationale: Fundraising involves significant digital work including donor research, data analysis, and the creation of promotional messaging, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While major gift solicitation and event management require high-level interpersonal skills and physical presence, the majority of the administrative, analytical, and communication-based tasks are moving toward AI-driven workflows.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of fundraising—solicitation, donor communication, and campaign management—is increasingly digital and can be executed by AI agents capable of personalized outreach and data-driven strategy. However, the role remains highly dependent on human-to-human trust, empathy, and the cultivation of long-term relationships, which are difficult to fully automate without losing the 'human touch' that drives major philanthropic contributions.

rows.title: Flight attendants
rows.old_rationale: The core responsibilities of flight attendants are physical and interpersonal, involving safety enforcement, emergency medical response, and manual service tasks that AI cannot perform. While AI may assist with peripheral digital tasks like report writing or passenger manifest management, the role is largely defined by a physical presence required by law for passenger safety.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of a flight attendant is physical presence, safety monitoring, and human-to-human crisis management, which cannot be digitized or executed by software. While the job involves procedural tasks, the high-stakes, unpredictable nature of in-flight emergencies and the legal requirement for human authority in cabin security make this role highly resistant to agentic automation.

rows.title: Insurance underwriters
rows.old_rationale: Underwriting is a fundamentally digital occupation centered on routine information processing, risk modeling, and data analysis—all areas where AI excels. The BLS already projects a decline in employment due to automation, and the shift from traditional software to advanced AI will likely automate even complex, non-standard risk assessments that previously required human judgment.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of an underwriter is a digital decision (approval/denial and premium setting), which is already heavily mediated by software and highly susceptible to full automation. While complex cases require human synthesis, the environment is almost entirely controlled and digital, meaning the primary barrier to total automation is systemic accountability—the legal and ethical requirement for a human to sign off on high-stakes financial risk.

rows.title: Editors
rows.old_rationale: Editing is a fully digital occupation centered on language processing, a core strength of Large Language Models. AI can already perform high-level copy editing, fact-checking, and rewriting, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of human editors required for routine content management.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of an editor is digital text and content strategy, which is highly susceptible to end-to-end execution by AI agents. While high-level editorial judgment and the management of human writers require nuance, the primary workflow—reviewing, correcting, and formatting content—is increasingly software-native and delegatable.

rows.title: Operations research analysts
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, involving data collection, mathematical modeling, and report writing—all areas where AI and Large Language Models excel. While analysts still provide high-level human judgment and stakeholder persuasion, AI can now automate the core technical tasks of coding analytical tools, mining data, and generating optimization simulations, significantly increasing individual productivity and restructuring the role.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of an operations research analyst is a digital model, report, or algorithmic recommendation, which is highly susceptible to end-to-end execution by AI agents. While the role requires high-level synthesis of complex variables, the work environment is almost entirely controlled and digital, leaving little room for physical unpredictability that would hinder automation.

rows.title: Public safety telecommunicators
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of this role—receiving digital inputs (voice, text, video), processing information according to protocols, and dispatching resources—are highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the high-stakes nature and need for human empathy provide a temporary buffer, AI's ability to transcribe, translate, and prioritize emergency calls in real-time will significantly restructure the occupation and increase individual worker productivity.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is the digital dispatch of resources and the relaying of information, which is highly amenable to AI execution. However, the role maintains a high score in systemic accountability and ontological human necessity because the dispatcher serves as the critical moral and legal bridge between a panicked caller and life-saving intervention, where human empathy and the assumption of liability for life-altering decisions remain essential.

rows.title: Advertising sales agents
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves digital knowledge work such as analyzing sales statistics, preparing media kits, and drafting contracts, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation. While the role requires interpersonal relationship building, the shift toward programmatic advertising and AI-driven ad placement is already reducing the need for human intermediaries in the sales process, as reflected in the projected employment decline.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—proposals, contracts, and media placement—is almost entirely digital and increasingly handled by programmatic advertising platforms and AI-driven CRM systems. While human rapport is currently used to close deals, the actual synthesis of advertising plans and the execution of sales contracts are highly susceptible to end-to-end automation, leaving little room for non-delegatable human necessity.

rows.title: Dietitians and nutritionists
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of analyzing nutritional data, interpreting research, and creating personalized meal plans are highly digital and data-driven, making them susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. However, the role requires significant interpersonal counseling, empathy, and clinical judgment in medical settings, which provides a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can generate highly accurate, data-driven meal plans and nutritional analysis, the core value of the profession lies in the human-to-human counseling process, which requires empathy, behavioral motivation, and the ability to navigate a client's complex emotional relationship with food. The role carries significant systemic accountability, as dietitians often manage medical nutrition therapy for patients with life-threatening conditions, requiring a human professional to assume liability for clinical outcomes.

rows.title: Diagnostic medical sonographers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical and digital work; while AI is rapidly advancing in image analysis and diagnostic assistance, the role requires significant physical presence to position patients and manipulate transducers with high hand-eye coordination. Furthermore, the interpersonal aspect of patient care and the real-time physical adjustments needed during a scan provide a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the final output (the image) is digital, the core value is the physical, real-time manipulation of a transducer on a patient's body to capture diagnostic-quality data, which requires human dexterity and physical positioning that software cannot currently replicate. The role carries high systemic accountability, as the sonographer's ability to identify subtle abnormalities directly impacts life-altering medical diagnoses, necessitating human oversight and ethical responsibility.

rows.title: Ironworkers
rows.old_rationale: Ironworking is a highly physical occupation performed in unpredictable, outdoor environments and at great heights, which provides a significant natural barrier to AI and robotics. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis or site scheduling, the core duties of manual welding, bolting, and balancing on structural beams require real-time human dexterity and physical presence that cannot be automated with current or near-future technology.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of ironworking is the physical manipulation and assembly of heavy structural materials in highly volatile, unstructured, and dangerous environments, which is fundamentally incompatible with current or near-future digital agentic execution. While the work requires spatial reasoning and safety-critical decision-making, the primary barrier to automation is the physical, high-stakes nature of the work, where the human operator bears direct, non-delegatable legal and physical liability for the structural integrity of the build.

rows.title: Opticians
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical, hands-on work such as measuring faces, adjusting frames with tools, and teaching patients how to insert contact lenses. While AI can automate administrative tasks and assist in frame selection via virtual try-on technology, the core requirements of physical fitting, manual repairs, and real-time interpersonal healthcare guidance provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the ordering and data entry aspects of the job are highly digitizable, the core value of an optician lies in the physical, in-person manipulation of eyewear to fit a specific human face and the interpersonal guidance provided to the customer. The physical nature of fitting, adjusting, and the necessity of human-to-human trust in a healthcare-adjacent retail environment limits the potential for full agentic replacement.

rows.title: Small engine mechanics
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical manipulation of mechanical components, manual dexterity, and real-world troubleshooting in unpredictable environments. While AI can assist with diagnostic software and technical documentation, it cannot perform the physical labor of disassembling engines, replacing parts, or conducting hands-on repairs.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of hardware in unstructured, often cramped or outdoor environments, which remains highly resistant to digital-only agentic execution. While diagnostic software assists in identifying issues, the final repair requires physical dexterity and real-world problem solving that cannot be delegated to software or current-generation autonomous agents.

rows.title: Railroad workers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation involves a significant amount of physical labor, mechanical adjustment, and real-world safety monitoring that AI cannot perform. However, core functions like train operation, scheduling, and signal management are increasingly susceptible to automation and AI-driven efficiency systems, which the BLS notes is already limiting employment growth.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical movement of massive freight and passengers through complex, often unpredictable real-world environments, which limits agentic potential to narrow, controlled tasks. While software can assist with logistics, the high-stakes nature of rail safety and the requirement for human intervention in mechanical failures or emergency situations creates a high barrier for full automation and maintains a heavy burden of systemic accountability.

rows.title: Property appraisers and assessors
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves analyzing data, comparing market trends, and generating reports, all of which are highly susceptible to Automated Valuation Models (AVMs) and AI-driven data analysis. While physical site inspections and the need to defend assessments in public hearings provide a buffer, the shift toward 'desktop appraisals' and AI-assisted mass assessments significantly reduces the human labor required per valuation.
rows.new_rationale: While the physical inspection of property remains a hurdle, the core value of the job—the valuation report—is a digital artifact that is increasingly generated by automated valuation models (AVMs). The high score for systemic accountability reflects the legal and financial weight of these valuations, which serve as the basis for mortgages and tax assessments, requiring a human to sign off on the liability despite the high potential for AI-driven execution.

rows.title: Computer hardware engineers
rows.old_rationale: The core work of designing schematics, analyzing circuits, and testing components is increasingly performed using digital Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tools that are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization and automation. While there is a physical component to testing and manufacturing oversight, the vast majority of the engineering lifecycle is digital and benefits significantly from AI's ability to optimize complex systems and generate code/hardware descriptions.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is digital schematics and design files that are increasingly generated and optimized by AI-driven EDA (Electronic Design Automation) tools, leading to high agentic potential. However, the work requires high-level cognitive synthesis to navigate complex trade-offs in performance, power, and thermal constraints, and carries significant systemic accountability for the safety and reliability of the resulting hardware.

rows.title: Industrial engineering technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of digital work, such as analyzing production costs, using CAD/CAM software, and conducting statistical studies, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation. However, the role also requires physical presence on the manufacturing floor to observe workers, assess machinery performance, and evaluate physical layouts, which provides a partial buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—process diagrams, workflow layouts, and statistical reports—is highly digital and increasingly executable by AI-driven simulation and CAD software. While the work occurs in physical manufacturing environments, the technician's role is primarily observational and analytical rather than manual, making the transition to autonomous data collection and system optimization highly feasible.

rows.title: Insulation workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are highly physical and require manual dexterity in unpredictable, confined environments like attics and crawl spaces. AI has minimal impact on the physical installation of materials, though it may slightly assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis or material estimation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor in highly unstructured, often hazardous environments like crawlspaces, attics, and active industrial sites, which are extremely difficult for current or near-future autonomous agents to navigate. Because the output is a physical installation requiring manual dexterity and real-time adaptation to site-specific constraints, the agentic output potential is near zero.

rows.title: Travel agents
rows.old_rationale: The core functions of a travel agent—researching destinations, comparing prices, building itineraries, and processing bookings—are entirely digital and involve information synthesis that AI can now perform instantly. While high-end human agents provide personalized judgment and crisis management, AI's ability to handle complex natural language queries and real-time logistics makes the vast majority of the occupation's routine tasks highly susceptible to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a travel agent is entirely digital—itineraries, bookings, and reservations—which can be fully executed by autonomous agents interacting with existing travel APIs. While the role involves some advisory synthesis, the work occurs in a highly controlled digital environment with minimal physical variables, making it exceptionally susceptible to full automation.

rows.title: Labor relations specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, involving the drafting of contracts, policy development, and legal compliance—all areas where AI excels. While the high-stakes interpersonal negotiation and dispute resolution provide a buffer, AI will significantly automate the research, document generation, and investigative analysis components of the role.
rows.new_rationale: While the drafting of contracts and policy documents is highly amenable to AI-driven generation and analysis, the core value of the role is rooted in high-stakes human mediation, trust-building, and the navigation of volatile interpersonal dynamics. The necessity for a human to hold moral authority and legal accountability during labor disputes and negotiations creates a significant barrier to full automation, despite the high digital output potential of the documentation tasks.

rows.title: Community health workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is built on trust, cultural sensitivity, and physical presence within specific communities, which AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like data collection, report writing, and resource matching, the primary duties involve home visits, hands-on advocacy, and real-time interpersonal coaching for vulnerable populations.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is rooted in human-to-human connection, trust, and cultural mediation, which cannot be digitized or offloaded to an agent. While data collection and resource referral have some digital potential, the work is defined by navigating unpredictable, high-stakes, real-world human environments where physical presence and empathy are the primary mechanisms of service delivery.

rows.title: Financial examiners
rows.old_rationale: Financial examiners perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving the analysis of balance sheets, loan documentation, and regulatory text. AI is exceptionally well-suited for automating the core tasks of this role, such as identifying anomalies in financial data, summarizing meeting minutes, and checking compliance against complex legal frameworks, though human judgment remains necessary for final risk assessments and high-level management evaluations.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a financial examiner is a digital report or regulatory finding, which is highly susceptible to automation via AI-driven data analysis and audit software. While the work requires significant cognitive synthesis to interpret complex financial risks, the physical environment is highly controlled, and the primary barrier to full automation is the high-stakes systemic accountability and legal liability that currently requires a human to sign off on life-altering regulatory decisions.

rows.title: Civil engineering technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical field work. While AI and advanced CAD software are significantly automating drafting, cost estimation, and data analysis tasks, the role still requires physical site visits, material testing, and real-world inspections that AI cannot currently perform.
rows.new_rationale: While a significant portion of the work involves digital CAD drafting and data reporting—tasks highly susceptible to AI automation—the role also requires physical site inspections, material testing, and navigating unstructured construction environments where real-world variables cannot be fully digitized. The human necessity is moderate, primarily serving as a bridge between high-level engineering designs and the physical reality of construction, but the lack of final legal authority limits their systemic accountability compared to licensed engineers.

rows.title: Glaziers
rows.old_rationale: The core work of a glazier is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, strength, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable construction environments. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis or precision measurements, the actual installation, cutting, and securing of glass in the physical world remain well beyond current AI and robotic capabilities.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical installation and manipulation of materials in unstructured, often hazardous construction environments, which cannot be executed by software. While blueprint reading and precision are required, the physical unpredictability of job sites and the necessity for manual dexterity and safety judgment make this role highly resistant to agentic automation.

rows.title: Surveying and mapping technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital data processing. While AI and automation (via drones and GIS software) significantly enhance data analysis, map creation, and calculation tasks, the role still requires a physical presence to navigate rugged terrain, set markers, and operate equipment in the real world.
rows.new_rationale: While the digital processing and mapping components have high agentic potential, the role remains tethered to unstructured, rugged physical environments that require human navigation and physical equipment management. The core value is technical data collection rather than human-to-human interaction, though the technician bears professional responsibility for the accuracy of the spatial data that informs downstream engineering decisions.

rows.title: Tax examiners and collectors, and revenue agents
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core tasks—reviewing financial documents, identifying discrepancies, and interpreting tax law—are digital information-processing tasks where AI excels. While revenue agents handling complex corporate audits and collectors performing field investigations require human judgment and interpersonal skills, a significant portion of the routine examination and data verification work is already being automated or enhanced by sophisticated algorithms.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is the determination of tax liability and the execution of financial adjustments, which are almost entirely digital and highly amenable to automated software agents. While the work requires analytical rigor and adherence to complex regulations, the primary barrier to full automation is not the nature of the tasks, but the systemic requirement for human legal and ethical accountability in state-sanctioned enforcement actions.

rows.title: Special effects artists and animators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, with core tasks like 3D modeling, rendering, and animation being directly disrupted by generative AI and automated rigging tools. While high-level creative direction and complex team collaboration remain human-centric, AI is rapidly automating the 'routine' aspects of CGI and visual effects, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of workers needed for large-scale productions.
rows.new_rationale: The output of this occupation is almost entirely digital, making it highly susceptible to end-to-end execution by generative AI agents. While the work requires high-level creative synthesis and aesthetic judgment, it occurs in a fully controlled, virtual environment, and the final product does not carry the type of life-altering systemic liability that would necessitate a human-in-the-loop for legal or safety reasons.

rows.title: Sales engineers
rows.old_rationale: Sales engineers perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly digital, including technical presentations, product customization, and troubleshooting. While AI can automate technical documentation, demo generation, and data-driven customer insights, the role's heavy reliance on complex interpersonal relationships, negotiation, and trust-building provides a significant buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While much of the technical documentation and product configuration can be handled by AI, the core value of a sales engineer lies in high-stakes persuasion, trust-building, and navigating complex human organizational dynamics to close deals. The role requires synthesizing deep technical knowledge with nuanced interpersonal strategy, making the human element essential for managing the 'human-to-human' bond and the accountability of high-value B2B commitments.

rows.title: Technical writers
rows.old_rationale: Technical writing is a fully digital occupation centered on synthesizing complex information into clear documentation, a core strength of Large Language Models. AI can already draft manuals, summarize technical specs, and generate code documentation, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the total number of human writers needed for routine documentation tasks.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a technical writer is digital documentation, which is highly susceptible to end-to-end automation via LLMs and integrated software workflows. While the role requires high-level synthesis to translate complex engineering concepts into user-friendly language, the work environment is almost entirely controlled and digital, leaving little room for physical or human-centric barriers to AI replacement.

rows.title: Surveyors
rows.old_rationale: Surveying is a hybrid occupation that combines significant physical fieldwork with digital data processing. While AI and automation (via drones and GIS) are rapidly increasing productivity in the mapping and analysis phases, the core requirements of physical site visits, navigating rugged terrain, and providing legally certified human testimony in court create a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While data processing and mapping are increasingly digital, the core value of surveying remains the physical verification of land boundaries in unstructured, often rugged environments where AI cannot yet navigate or physically interact. The high accountability score reflects the legal necessity for a licensed human to certify property lines, as these decisions carry significant, non-delegatable legal and financial consequences that require human liability.

rows.title: Tour and travel guides
rows.old_rationale: While AI can automate the digital aspects of the job—such as researching history, planning itineraries, and providing multilingual translations—the core of the role requires physical presence and real-time human interaction. Guides must physically escort groups through unpredictable environments, manage group dynamics, and provide a personal, empathetic experience that digital tools cannot fully replicate.
rows.new_rationale: While the informational component of a tour could be digitized, the core value of the role is the in-person, physical facilitation of an experience, which cannot be delegated to software. The job requires real-time, human-centric management of group dynamics and physical navigation of unpredictable environments, making the human presence the primary product rather than a secondary support function.

rows.title: Occupational therapy assistants and aides
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical assistance, manual therapy, and real-time human interaction, such as lifting patients and guiding motor skill exercises, which AI cannot perform. While AI can streamline administrative tasks like scheduling, documentation, and billing, the primary value of the role is rooted in physical presence and empathetic interpersonal support.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical, in-person therapeutic intervention and patient support, which cannot be digitized or executed via software. The work environment is highly unpredictable due to the physical variability of patients and the need for real-time, empathetic human interaction, making the human presence an essential component of the service rather than a replaceable interface.

rows.title: Craft and fine artists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical craftsmanship and digital creation. While craft artists (potters, glassblowers) and sculptors have low exposure due to the physical nature of their work, digital artists and illustrators face very high exposure as AI can now generate high-quality imagery and complex designs from text prompts. The overall score reflects this split, as AI significantly impacts the commercial and digital segments of the field while leaving traditional physical mediums largely intact.
rows.new_rationale: While digital art and illustration have high agentic potential, the core of this occupation involves physical manipulation of materials (glass, clay, textiles) that cannot be executed by software. The value is deeply rooted in human expression, subjective aesthetic judgment, and the 'human touch,' which remains a primary driver of market demand despite the increasing capability of generative AI to mimic artistic styles.

rows.title: Lodging managers
rows.old_rationale: Lodging managers perform a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical site management. While AI can automate back-office tasks like revenue management, dynamic pricing, and staff scheduling, the core role requires physical inspections of facilities and high-stakes interpersonal conflict resolution with guests and staff that cannot be fully digitized.
rows.new_rationale: While revenue management and administrative tasks are increasingly digital, the core of the role involves managing physical facilities and unpredictable human interactions, which limits agentic output potential. The necessity for human presence is high because the job's primary value is providing a hospitable, empathetic, and safe experience for guests in a dynamic, physical environment.

rows.title: News analysts, reporters, and journalists
rows.old_rationale: The core work product is digital (text, scripts, and multimedia), and AI can already automate significant portions of research, drafting, and editing. While physical presence for field reporting and the cultivation of human sources provide a protective barrier, the overall occupation faces high exposure as AI tools drastically increase individual productivity, potentially leading to further industry consolidation and fewer required roles.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of journalism is digital information, which is highly susceptible to AI generation and automated distribution. While the job involves physical presence in unpredictable environments for investigative reporting and relationship building, the final product—the news story or analysis—is increasingly a synthesis task that software can execute end-to-end.

rows.title: Power plant operators, distributors, and dispatchers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of monitoring data, adjusting power flows, and responding to system abnormalities are increasingly digital and data-driven, making them highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and smart-grid optimization. While the high-stakes nature of power infrastructure and the need for physical equipment inspections provide a buffer, the BLS already projects a 10% decline in employment due to technological advances and automated control rooms.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is the digital manipulation of grid and plant controls, which is increasingly being integrated into automated software systems, leading to a high agentic potential. However, the role remains anchored by extreme systemic accountability, particularly in nuclear and grid-critical environments, where human oversight is legally and ethically required to manage high-stakes, life-altering failure scenarios that machines cannot yet fully own.

rows.title: Logging workers
rows.old_rationale: The core work is highly physical, involving the operation of heavy machinery and manual labor in unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI may improve peripheral tasks like log grading through computer vision or optimize harvest planning, the physical requirements of felling and transporting timber provide a strong barrier to AI replacement.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of logging is the physical extraction and processing of timber in highly volatile, unstructured, and dangerous outdoor environments, which remains largely resistant to autonomous digital execution. While some data-entry tasks exist for graders, the primary work involves navigating complex physical terrain and operating heavy machinery, making the agentic potential extremely low.

rows.title: Medical transcriptionists
rows.old_rationale: Medical transcription is a purely digital, routine information-processing task that has already been heavily impacted by speech recognition. Modern AI and Large Language Models (LLMs) can now handle complex medical terminology, formatting, and summarization with high accuracy, transforming the human role into a diminishing oversight function or eliminating it entirely.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is entirely digital and already heavily automated by speech-to-text software, making it a prime candidate for full agentic execution. The work is performed in highly controlled, virtual environments with minimal physical variables, and the human role is primarily that of a quality-control auditor rather than a source of unique human value.

rows.title: Floral designers
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job is a physical craft involving the handling of perishable biological materials and manual assembly, which AI cannot perform. However, AI can significantly assist with peripheral digital tasks such as generating design concepts for customers, managing inventory, and automating customer service for online orders.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical manipulation of perishable biological materials, which cannot be digitized or executed via software. While the design process involves aesthetic synthesis, the final product requires human dexterity and on-site physical setup, making it highly resistant to agentic automation despite the routine nature of some tasks.

rows.title: Entertainment and recreation managers
rows.old_rationale: This role involves a significant amount of digital knowledge work, such as budgeting, scheduling, and strategic planning, which is highly susceptible to AI optimization. However, the core of the job requires physical presence to inspect facilities, manage real-time emergencies, and provide high-touch interpersonal leadership and customer service in physical environments like parks and fitness centers.
rows.new_rationale: While scheduling and budgeting can be digitized, the core value of this role lies in the physical orchestration of events, real-time staff management, and the creation of a 'positive experience' for participants, which requires a human presence. The work environment is often unstructured and physically dynamic, requiring on-site judgment and interpersonal leadership that cannot be fully delegated to software or autonomous agents.

rows.title: Conservation scientists and foresters
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical field work, such as fire suppression, planting trees, and navigating difficult terrain, which provides a natural barrier to AI automation. However, a substantial portion of the role involves data analysis, GIS mapping, and regulatory compliance—tasks where AI can significantly enhance productivity and decision-making. While AI will reshape the information-processing aspects of the job, the requirement for real-world presence and manual intervention keeps exposure moderate.
rows.new_rationale: While data analysis and mapping can be digitized, the core value of this role involves physical intervention in highly volatile, unstructured natural environments (e.g., fire suppression, site preparation, habitat management) that cannot be executed by software. The job requires complex, multi-variable synthesis of ecological data, but the final output is a physical change to the landscape, necessitating human presence and professional accountability for long-term environmental outcomes.

rows.title: Computer and information research scientists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital, involving high-level coding, algorithm design, and data analysis—all areas where AI is rapidly advancing. While these scientists are the ones building AI, the tools they create are increasingly capable of automating their own core tasks, such as writing code, simplifying algorithms, and analyzing experimental results, leading to extreme productivity gains and role restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is digital—algorithms, software architectures, and theoretical models—which are inherently executable by AI agents, leading to a very high agentic potential. While the work involves extreme cognitive complexity and high-stakes accountability for system-critical infrastructure, it occurs in highly controlled, digital environments that lack the physical unpredictability or necessity for human-to-human emotional labor found in other fields.

rows.title: Announcers and DJs
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is split between highly exposed digital work and less exposed physical work. Broadcast and radio roles are under significant threat from AI-generated voices and automated content curation, whereas live event DJs and emcees retain a physical and interpersonal barrier that is harder to automate.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation—audio/visual content and event hosting—is almost entirely digital or performative, making it highly susceptible to AI generation and execution. While live event DJs face some physical environmental variables, the vast majority of broadcast and radio work is performed in controlled, predictable settings where AI agents can already synthesize content and manage broadcast workflows end-to-end.

rows.title: Jewelers and precious stone and metal workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical dexterity, manual repair, and hands-on craftsmanship that AI cannot replicate. However, AI and automation are significantly impacting the design and appraisal phases through generative CAD tools and automated gemstone grading, contributing to a projected decline in total employment.
rows.new_rationale: While CAD/CAM and 3D printing allow for digital design, the core value of the occupation remains the physical manipulation, assembly, and repair of precious materials, which requires high-dexterity manual labor that cannot be fully offloaded to software. The environment is highly controlled (workbenches), and while there is a human element in appraisal and custom design, the physical nature of the craft limits the potential for purely agentic, software-based execution.

rows.title: Mathematicians and statisticians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, involving data analysis, mathematical modeling, and coding—all domains where AI and Large Language Models excel. While high-level theoretical research and complex interdisciplinary communication require human judgment, AI can now automate significant portions of data cleaning, statistical testing, and code generation, drastically increasing individual productivity and restructuring the workflow.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this profession is digital—models, reports, and code—which is highly susceptible to automation by AI agents capable of end-to-end data processing and synthesis. While the work involves high-level cognitive complexity and significant systemic accountability for the validity of models and their real-world consequences, the environment is almost exclusively controlled and digital, requiring minimal physical interaction.

rows.title: Athletic trainers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of athletic trainers—such as applying tape/braces, providing emergency first aid on the field, and performing manual rehabilitation—require physical presence and tactile skills that AI cannot replicate. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like injury analysis, record-keeping, and designing recovery protocols, the job remains fundamentally grounded in real-time human interaction and physical healthcare delivery.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of an athletic trainer is physical, in-person intervention and real-time emergency response in highly unpredictable, unstructured environments like sports fields. While administrative tasks are digital, the primary output—physical injury assessment, manual therapy, and immediate human care—cannot be executed by software, and the high-stakes nature of medical decision-making requires non-delegatable human accountability.

rows.title: Industrial designers
rows.old_rationale: Industrial design is a predominantly digital occupation where core tasks like sketching, 3D modeling, and rendering are being rapidly transformed by generative AI and advanced CAD automation. While the role requires physical prototyping and cross-functional collaboration, the vast majority of the design process is computer-based, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and workflow restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: Industrial design has high agentic potential because the primary output is a digital CAD file or machine-readable instruction that can be directly executed by manufacturing systems. While the work involves complex synthesis of aesthetics, engineering, and market needs, the physical environment is generally controlled, and the human necessity is largely centered on client communication and subjective creative judgment rather than essential physical presence.

rows.title: Solar photovoltaic installers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, involving manual labor, climbing roofs, and using hand tools in unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI might assist with peripheral tasks like system design optimization or site measurements via computer vision, the actual installation and maintenance require human dexterity and physical presence that current and near-future AI/robotics cannot replicate.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this job is physical labor in highly variable, unstructured environments (rooftops, attics, crawlspaces), which is extremely difficult for current agentic software to execute. While planning and testing involve some cognitive effort, the primary output is a physical installation that requires human dexterity, safety judgment, and physical presence to navigate site-specific constraints.

rows.title: Fashion designers
rows.old_rationale: Fashion design is increasingly digital, utilizing CAD and graphics software for sketching, trend analysis, and virtual modeling—all areas where generative AI is rapidly advancing. While the physical aspects of selecting fabrics, creating prototypes, and overseeing production provide a buffer, the core creative and analytical tasks are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and productivity gains.
rows.new_rationale: While the design process is increasingly digital and CAD-based, the final value remains tied to the physical tactile experience of textiles and the fit on human bodies, which limits full agentic automation. The role requires high-level creative synthesis to navigate cultural trends and aesthetic innovation, but it lacks the high-stakes systemic accountability or volatile physical environment found in critical infrastructure or emergency services.

rows.title: Geoscientists
rows.old_rationale: Geoscientists have a balanced mix of physical and digital work. While AI is highly effective at analyzing seismic data, GIS mapping, and predictive modeling, the core of the job requires physical fieldwork, sample collection in remote environments, and laboratory testing that AI cannot perform. Consequently, AI will significantly enhance their analytical productivity but cannot replace the essential physical presence and real-world data gathering required for the profession.
rows.new_rationale: While geoscientists produce digital models and reports, the core value is derived from physical, in-situ data collection in highly unstructured, remote environments that remain difficult for autonomous agents to navigate reliably. Furthermore, the role carries significant professional and legal liability regarding environmental safety and resource extraction, requiring human judgment and accountability that cannot be fully delegated to software.

rows.title: Nuclear medicine technologists
rows.old_rationale: The role requires significant physical presence to prepare and administer radiopharmaceuticals, operate heavy machinery, and physically assist or position patients. While AI will likely automate the digital aspects of the job—such as optimizing image quality, calculating dosages, and streamlining record-keeping—the core clinical and manual tasks remain insulated from full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the diagnostic output is digital, the core value of the role involves the physical, high-stakes administration of radioactive materials to human patients, which cannot be delegated to software. The requirement for real-time patient monitoring, physical assistance, and the severe legal and ethical liability associated with radiation safety and medical intervention necessitates a human presence that AI cannot currently replace.

rows.title: Umpires, referees, and other sports officials
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical presence and objective data processing. While computer vision and AI can already automate 'calls' (like strike zones or out-of-bounds) with higher accuracy than humans, the role still requires physical presence to manage player conduct, ensure safety, and handle real-time interpersonal disputes on the field.
rows.new_rationale: While AI-driven computer vision is increasingly capable of making objective calls (reducing the need for human input in specific rule enforcement), the role remains highly dependent on human presence for conflict resolution, game management, and the social contract of sports. The physical, unpredictable nature of live athletic environments and the requirement for human authority to maintain order and mediate disputes among participants make full automation of the role's core value unlikely.

rows.title: Athletes and sports competitors
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical performance, manual dexterity, and real-time human presence in the physical world, which AI cannot replicate. While AI is used as a peripheral tool for performance analytics, strategy optimization, and diet planning, it does not replace the athlete's physical labor or the entertainment value of human competition.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical performance of a human body in real-time, which cannot be digitized or delegated to software. While athletes engage in complex strategic synthesis, the primary product is the human-to-human connection and the spectacle of biological achievement, rendering the role fundamentally immune to replacement by autonomous agents.

rows.title: Economists
rows.old_rationale: Economists perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving data analysis, mathematical modeling, and technical writing. AI is exceptionally proficient at the core tasks of this role, including processing large datasets, identifying statistical patterns, and generating reports, which significantly increases productivity and reduces the number of human workers needed for routine analysis.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of an economist is digital—reports, forecasts, and models—which are highly susceptible to automation by AI agents capable of processing massive datasets and executing complex simulations. While the work requires high-level cognitive synthesis to interpret economic phenomena, the environment is almost entirely controlled and digital, and the primary human necessity lies in the final layer of policy advocacy and ethical judgment rather than physical interaction.

rows.title: Recreational therapists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical presence, manual assistance, and real-time interpersonal interaction through activities like adaptive sports, dance, and community outings. While AI can significantly assist with digital tasks like patient assessment, treatment planning, and progress documentation, it cannot replace the physical and emotional labor required to lead therapeutic interventions in person.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is rooted in human-to-human connection, empathy, and physical presence, which cannot be digitized or delegated to software. While the planning phase involves complex cognitive synthesis, the execution requires navigating unpredictable, real-world physical environments and managing the high-stakes emotional and physical well-being of vulnerable patients.

rows.title: Wind turbine technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves high-stakes physical labor, including climbing 200-foot towers, rappelling, and performing manual repairs in confined spaces. While AI can assist with remote monitoring and predictive maintenance diagnostics, the physical requirement to replace components and perform manual inspections in unpredictable outdoor environments provides a strong barrier to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical intervention in a highly volatile, unstructured, and dangerous environment (200+ feet in the air, confined spaces), which is extremely difficult for autonomous agents to navigate. While diagnostics can be digitized, the final output—the physical repair and maintenance of massive mechanical components—requires human dexterity and physical presence, keeping agentic potential very low.

rows.title: Geological and hydrologic technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital data analysis. While AI and automation (like drones and remote sensors) are increasingly handling data collection and mapping, the role still requires significant physical presence for equipment maintenance, manual sample collection, and laboratory testing that cannot be fully digitized.
rows.new_rationale: While data analysis and reporting are digital, the core value of this role is tied to physical sample collection and equipment maintenance in remote, unstructured, and often hazardous environments that require human physical presence and dexterity. The high environmental unpredictability and the need for on-site physical judgment limit the agentic potential, as software cannot currently navigate or manipulate the physical world in these specific, non-standardized field conditions.

rows.title: Forest and conservation workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are highly physical and performed in unpredictable outdoor environments, such as planting seedlings, clearing brush, and suppressing forest fires. While AI and remote sensing can assist with peripheral tasks like tree counting and health monitoring, the primary labor-intensive functions cannot be automated by digital AI systems.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor in highly unstructured, volatile natural environments, which is extremely difficult for autonomous agents to navigate or manipulate. While some data-gathering tasks are being digitized, the primary output—maintaining forest health, clearing debris, and fire suppression—requires physical presence and real-time, site-specific physical intervention that cannot be executed via software.

rows.title: Orthotists and prosthetists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical, hands-on work including patient examinations, manual measurements, and the physical fabrication or adjustment of medical devices. While AI and computer-aided design (CAD) can greatly enhance the diagnostic and design phases, the core requirements of real-time human interaction, physical dexterity, and clinical judgment in a healthcare setting provide a strong barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While digital design and 3D printing are increasingly used, the core value remains the physical, high-stakes integration of a device with a unique, living human body, which requires manual dexterity and real-time physical adjustments. The occupation carries significant clinical liability and requires deep empathy and trust, as the practitioner is directly responsible for the patient's mobility, comfort, and long-term physical health.

rows.title: Atmospheric scientists, including meteorologists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves analyzing digital data, running computer models, and writing code, all of which are highly susceptible to AI advancement. Machine learning is already revolutionizing weather forecasting by outperforming traditional numerical models in speed and efficiency, potentially reducing the number of human forecasters needed for routine data processing and report generation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this profession is primarily digital—forecasts, models, and data visualizations—which are increasingly generated and disseminated via automated software systems. While the field requires high-level cognitive synthesis to interpret complex atmospheric variables, the primary constraint on full automation is not the physical environment, but the high-stakes systemic accountability required when issuing life-saving warnings for severe weather events.

rows.title: Anthropologists and archeologists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical fieldwork and digital knowledge work. While AI can significantly automate data analysis, pattern recognition in artifacts, and report writing, it cannot replace the physical requirements of excavation, site surveying, and in-person ethnographic observation.
rows.new_rationale: While the final research reports are digital, the core value of the profession is derived from physical, in-situ data collection and the interpretation of unstructured, often volatile, real-world environments that cannot be fully digitized or automated. The high cognitive complexity stems from the need to synthesize disparate cultural, historical, and biological variables, while the physical nature of fieldwork and the necessity of human observation in social contexts limit the potential for autonomous agents to replace the practitioner.

rows.title: Survey researchers
rows.old_rationale: Survey research is a fundamentally digital knowledge occupation involving background research, survey design, statistical analysis, and report generation—all areas where LLMs and specialized AI tools excel. The BLS already projects a decline in employment due to data mining and big data efficiencies, and the rapid advancement of AI in synthesizing qualitative data and automating complex statistical workflows makes this role highly susceptible to significant restructuring and labor reduction.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—survey design, data analysis, and reporting—is almost entirely digital and can be executed end-to-end by AI agents, as evidenced by the industry's shift toward automated data mining. While the work requires high-level analytical synthesis, it occurs in highly controlled, office-based environments with minimal physical variables, making it highly susceptible to full automation.

rows.title: Medical dosimetrists
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves complex mathematical calculations, image analysis (CT/MRI), and digital treatment planning, all of which are domains where AI and machine learning excel. While there is a minor physical component involving patient positioning and equipment calibration, the vast majority of the work is digital information processing that is highly susceptible to automation and AI-driven optimization.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a dosimetrist is a digital treatment plan (a set of coordinates and dosage parameters) that is directly executable by radiation delivery software, giving it extremely high agentic potential. While the work requires high-level synthesis of complex medical data, the environment is highly controlled and clinical, minimizing physical unpredictability. However, the role remains tethered to human oversight due to the extreme, life-altering consequences of radiation errors, which necessitate non-delegatable professional and ethical accountability.

rows.title: Historians
rows.old_rationale: Historians perform high-level knowledge work—researching, analyzing, and writing—that is increasingly digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven efficiency gains in document processing and synthesis. While the job requires human judgment and physical interaction with artifacts or archives, the core output of reports and articles faces significant restructuring as AI tools become capable of parsing vast historical datasets and drafting complex narratives.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a historian—written reports, analyses, and digital exhibits—is highly susceptible to AI generation, as these are primarily digital artifacts derived from information synthesis. While the work requires high-level cognitive synthesis to interpret complex historical narratives, the physical environment is generally controlled (archives, offices, museums), and the role lacks the high-stakes, non-delegatable liability found in fields like medicine or law.

rows.title: Geographers
rows.old_rationale: Geographers primarily perform digital knowledge work, including data analysis, GIS modeling, and report writing, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While some fieldwork exists, the core of the profession involves processing satellite imagery and census data—tasks where AI and machine learning are rapidly becoming more efficient than humans.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a geographer—maps, spatial models, and analytical reports—is almost entirely digital and increasingly generated through automated GIS and remote sensing pipelines, giving it high agentic potential. While fieldwork introduces some physical unpredictability, the primary value lies in the high-dimensional synthesis of disparate data sets to inform policy, a task where AI is rapidly becoming the primary engine of production.

rows.title: Food and beverage serving and related workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, such as clearing tables, carrying heavy trays, and cleaning, which are difficult to automate with current robotics. However, AI is increasingly handling digital peripheral tasks like order-taking via kiosks or voice-AI drive-thrus and managing reservations, which reduces the need for human staff in front-of-house roles.
rows.new_rationale: While order taking and payment processing are increasingly digital, the core value of this role is the physical delivery of goods and the maintenance of a clean, hospitable environment, which requires physical presence and dexterity. The environment is highly unpredictable due to the constant, fluid interaction with human customers and the physical hazards of a kitchen/dining space, making full automation difficult despite the low cognitive complexity of the individual tasks.

rows.title: Home health and personal care aides
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are fundamentally physical and interpersonal, involving manual tasks like bathing, dressing, and lifting patients in unpredictable home environments. While AI may assist with peripheral administrative tasks like scheduling or monitoring vital signs, it cannot replace the essential human presence and physical labor required for personal care.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical, in-person care and emotional support, which cannot be digitized or executed by software agents. The work environment is highly unstructured, involving the unpredictable physical and emotional needs of vulnerable individuals in their own homes, necessitating a human presence that AI cannot replicate.

rows.title: Secretaries and administrative assistants
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation—scheduling, drafting correspondence, managing databases, and transcribing—are fundamentally digital and align perfectly with the capabilities of LLMs and AI agents. While some roles require physical presence for visitor greeting or office management, the vast majority of tasks are routine information processing that can be automated or significantly streamlined, leading to major restructuring and reduced headcount per organization.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—scheduling, document preparation, data entry, and communication—is almost entirely digital and can be executed end-to-end by autonomous agents. While the role requires interpersonal coordination and judgment, the work environment is highly structured and office-based, leaving little room for physical variables that would impede software-based automation.

rows.title: Waiters and waitresses
rows.old_rationale: While AI and automation are increasingly handling digital tasks like order-taking and payments via kiosks or mobile apps, the core of the job remains physical and interpersonal. The physical requirements of carrying trays, clearing tables, and providing real-time human hospitality in a dynamic environment create a significant barrier to full AI replacement.
rows.new_rationale: While order-taking and payment processing are increasingly digital, the core value of the role remains physical labor—carrying items, cleaning, and navigating a dynamic, unstructured dining room. The high score for ontological human necessity reflects that the role is fundamentally about hospitality and the social experience of service, which remains a human-centric product despite the potential for automation in the transactional components.

rows.title: Software developers, quality assurance analysts, and testers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital, with core tasks like coding, debugging, and test automation being primary use cases for Large Language Models. While high-level system architecture and complex stakeholder communication provide some insulation, AI is drastically increasing individual productivity, which will likely lead to significant restructuring of entry-level roles and QA functions.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation is digital code and system architecture, which is inherently executable by AI agents, leading to a maximum score in agentic potential. While the work requires high-level cognitive synthesis to design complex systems, it occurs in a highly controlled, virtual environment, and the human necessity is primarily limited to stakeholder communication and final ethical oversight rather than physical presence or human-to-human bonding.

rows.title: Delivery truck drivers and driver/sales workers
rows.old_rationale: While AI and autonomous vehicle technology are advancing, the core of this job involves significant physical labor, such as loading/unloading cargo and navigating unpredictable 'last-mile' environments. AI may optimize routing and assist with sales communication, but the physical requirement to move goods from a vehicle to a customer's door remains a major barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the navigation and logistics aspects of delivery are increasingly digitized, the core value of the job remains the physical movement, handling, and final-mile placement of goods in unstructured, real-world environments. Because the job requires physical interaction with unpredictable urban settings and manual labor that cannot be executed by software alone, the agentic output potential remains low.

rows.title: Nursing assistants and orderlies
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of nursing assistants and orderlies—such as bathing, dressing, and physically transferring patients—are highly manual and require real-time human presence in unpredictable physical environments. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like recording vital signs or scheduling, the primary work product is physical care and interpersonal support, which remains largely insulated from AI automation.
