rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical, in-person care and patient interaction, which cannot be digitized or executed via software agents. The work environment is highly unpredictable due to the physical variability of patients and the need for real-time, empathetic human presence, making it fundamentally resistant to automation despite the routine nature of some tasks.

rows.title: Material recording clerks
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is highly exposed because its core functions—tracking data, scheduling, and inventory reporting—are digital information-processing tasks that AI and automated systems excel at. While some physical presence is required to inspect goods or handle packages, the rapid integration of AI-driven logistics software, RFID technology, and computer vision for quality control is already leading to a projected decline in employment.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is information management and inventory tracking, which is increasingly handled by digital systems, RFID, and automated software, giving it high agentic potential. While the work occurs in physical warehouses, the tasks are largely procedural and standardized, requiring minimal complex synthesis or human-to-human emotional labor, making the role highly susceptible to full digital and robotic automation.

rows.title: Management analysts
rows.old_rationale: Management analysts perform high-level knowledge work—including data analysis, report writing, and financial modeling—that is highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. While the role requires significant interpersonal skills for client interviews and stakeholder management, the core 'back-office' tasks of synthesizing information and developing efficiency recommendations are increasingly being streamlined by AI tools.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a management analyst is a digital artifact—reports, strategic recommendations, and process models—which are highly susceptible to AI generation and execution. While the work requires high-level synthesis of organizational dynamics, the physical environment is generally controlled, and the primary 'human' necessity lies in the social capital, trust, and political navigation required to implement changes within a human-led organization.

rows.title: Project management specialists
rows.old_rationale: Project management is a digital-heavy knowledge occupation involving data analysis, scheduling, and documentation, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and optimization. While AI can significantly increase productivity in planning and monitoring, the role's heavy reliance on human-centric skills like stakeholder negotiation, conflict resolution, and team leadership provides a protective barrier against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: Project management is highly susceptible to AI because the core outputs—schedules, budgets, status reports, and resource allocation plans—are digital artifacts that can be generated and executed by autonomous agents. While the role requires high-level synthesis and human-centric negotiation to manage stakeholder expectations and team dynamics, the technical execution of project tracking and documentation is increasingly within the domain of software-driven automation.

rows.title: Childcare workers
rows.old_rationale: The core responsibilities of this role—physical care, safety monitoring, hygiene, and emotional support—require a physical presence and human empathy that AI cannot replicate. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like lesson planning, record-keeping, or parent communication, the fundamental nature of the work is hands-on and occurs in the physical world.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of childcare is the physical, emotional, and developmental presence of a human caregiver, which cannot be digitized or delegated to software. While the work involves routine tasks, the environment is highly unpredictable due to the nature of children, and the legal and ethical accountability for the safety and well-being of minors is non-delegatable.

rows.title: Human resources specialists
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of recruiting, screening, and administrative record-keeping are highly digital and data-driven, making them prime targets for AI automation and augmentation. While the role requires significant interpersonal interaction and human judgment for interviewing and employee relations, AI can now handle complex scheduling, initial candidate vetting, and policy inquiries, significantly increasing individual worker productivity and reducing the need for entry-level administrative staff.
rows.new_rationale: The core outputs of HR specialists—screening, documentation, and communication—are largely digital and increasingly executable by AI agents. However, the role retains high ontological necessity because the 'human-to-human' element is critical for employee relations, conflict resolution, and the trust-based nature of hiring and organizational culture.

rows.title: Computer support specialists
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of diagnosing technical issues, documenting problems, and guiding users through digital workflows are highly susceptible to AI automation via advanced chatbots and LLM-driven troubleshooting. While some physical hardware setup and complex onsite networking remain, the vast majority of the work is digital information processing that AI can increasingly handle, as evidenced by the BLS's projected decline in employment due to automation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is digital—troubleshooting code, configuring networks, and executing software commands—making it highly susceptible to AI agents that can perform these tasks end-to-end. While some physical hardware repair exists, the vast majority of the work occurs in controlled digital environments or via remote access, requiring minimal physical intervention and low levels of human-to-human emotional necessity.

rows.title: Financial managers
rows.old_rationale: Financial management is a predominantly digital knowledge-work occupation focused on data analysis, reporting, and forecasting, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While the role requires significant human judgment, strategic advisory, and leadership, the core technical tasks of monitoring financial details and preparing statements are being rapidly transformed by AI tools.
rows.new_rationale: Financial management is highly digital-native, as the core outputs—financial reports, investment strategies, and risk models—are information-based and can be executed directly by software agents. While the role requires high-level cognitive synthesis to navigate complex market variables, the primary barrier to full automation is not the physical environment, but rather the high-stakes systemic accountability and the human-centric need for trust and ethical judgment in corporate governance.

rows.title: Electricians
rows.old_rationale: The core work of an electrician is highly physical, involving manual installation, repair, and navigation of complex, unpredictable physical environments like construction sites and crawl spaces. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis, diagnostic calculations, or scheduling, it cannot perform the tactile labor or real-time physical problem-solving required for the job.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of an electrician is physical manipulation of infrastructure in highly variable, unstructured environments, which remains largely beyond the reach of current agentic software. While cognitive tasks like blueprint reading and diagnostics are complex, the final execution requires physical dexterity and real-time adaptation to site-specific hazards, coupled with high-stakes legal and safety accountability that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Agricultural workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, animal handling, and operating machinery in unpredictable outdoor environments, which provides a strong natural barrier to AI. While AI and robotics are increasingly used for precision agriculture and automated harvesting, the high cost of hardware and the need for manual dexterity in varied conditions mean human workers remain essential for the majority of tasks.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor in highly unstructured, volatile outdoor environments, which remains largely resistant to digital-only agentic execution. While AI can assist with monitoring or automated machinery, the primary tasks—such as animal husbandry, manual harvesting, and navigating unpredictable terrain—require physical presence and real-world interaction that cannot be replaced by software commands.

rows.title: Medical assistants
rows.old_rationale: Medical assistants perform a hybrid of physical and digital tasks; while AI can significantly automate administrative duties like scheduling, coding, and medical record entry, it cannot perform physical clinical tasks such as drawing blood, administering injections, or taking manual vital signs. The role requires a high degree of real-time human interaction and empathy in a physical clinical setting, which serves as a natural barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like scheduling and data entry have high agentic potential, the core of the role involves physical, in-person clinical interaction—such as drawing blood, taking vitals, and providing compassionate care—which cannot be digitized. The necessity for human presence and the high-stakes nature of patient safety and medical confidentiality create significant barriers to full automation, despite the routine nature of many individual tasks.

rows.title: Automotive service technicians and mechanics
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time mechanical repair in a physical environment, which provides a strong barrier against AI automation. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic capabilities and predictive maintenance software, the actual physical replacement of parts and hands-on troubleshooting remains a human-centric task.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of heavy machinery in unstructured, often cramped, and hazardous environments, which remains largely beyond the reach of current agentic software. While diagnostic software assists in identifying issues, the actual repair requires physical dexterity and real-time adaptation to mechanical wear and tear, and the technician bears significant legal and safety liability for the vehicle's roadworthiness.

rows.title: Bartenders
rows.old_rationale: The core of bartending involves physical manipulation of objects, manual cleaning, and real-time social interaction in a physical environment. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like inventory management or automated ordering, the primary value of the role lies in human presence, physical service, and the social 'hospitality' aspect which remains difficult to automate.
rows.new_rationale: While the physical act of mixing a drink can be automated, the core value of the role is deeply rooted in the social, emotional, and regulatory management of a physical, unpredictable environment. The bartender's necessity as a human arbiter of safety, legal compliance, and social atmosphere creates a high barrier to full agentic replacement, as the 'product' is the human-to-human interaction and the real-time judgment of intoxicated patrons.

rows.title: Sales managers
rows.old_rationale: Sales managers perform high-level knowledge work including data analysis, sales forecasting, and strategic planning, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. While the role requires significant interpersonal leadership and relationship management, much of the core digital workflow—such as monitoring CRM data, generating reports, and developing training materials—is being rapidly transformed by generative and analytical AI.
rows.new_rationale: Sales management is highly susceptible to AI because the core outputs—forecasting, CRM management, performance tracking, and strategy optimization—are digital artifacts that can be executed by autonomous agents. While the role requires complex synthesis of market data and human-centric leadership to motivate staff and build trust, the actual execution of sales operations is increasingly shifting toward software-driven workflows, leaving the human necessity primarily in the realms of high-stakes negotiation, emotional intelligence, and organizational culture.

rows.title: Purchasing managers, buyers, and purchasing agents
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of analyzing price proposals, monitoring inventory, and reviewing financial reports are highly susceptible to AI automation and optimization. While high-stakes negotiations and physical site visits provide a buffer, the majority of the work is digital information processing that can be significantly streamlined by AI, leading to higher productivity per worker and a restructuring of the procurement process.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—contracts, purchase orders, and supply chain logistics—is inherently digital and highly amenable to end-to-end execution by autonomous agents. While the role requires human judgment for complex negotiations and relationship management, the primary value is transactional and informational, making it a prime candidate for AI-driven automation.

rows.title: Insurance sales agents
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of analyzing policies, customizing insurance programs, and maintaining records are digital and data-driven, making them highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the role requires interpersonal trust and persuasive sales skills, AI-powered chatbots and recommendation engines are increasingly capable of handling routine inquiries and policy comparisons, significantly increasing individual agent productivity and reducing the need for human intervention in simple transactions.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of an insurance agent—the policy contract and financial transaction—is entirely digital and already highly automated via software platforms, making it a prime candidate for agentic execution. While the role requires human-to-human trust and empathy to navigate complex client needs and emotional situations, the technical and administrative aspects of the job are increasingly susceptible to AI-driven advisory and fulfillment systems.

rows.title: Industrial machinery mechanics, machinery maintenance workers, and millwrights
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time troubleshooting in unpredictable physical environments, which provides a strong barrier against AI automation. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic capabilities and predictive maintenance scheduling, the actual repair, installation, and physical manipulation of heavy machinery still require human presence and mechanical skill.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this work is physical intervention—disassembling, repairing, and installing heavy machinery—which cannot be executed by software agents. While the diagnostic phase involves complex cognitive synthesis, the execution remains tethered to unstructured, high-stakes physical environments where human dexterity and on-site judgment are essential for safety and operational integrity.

rows.title: Computer systems analysts
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital, involving data modeling, system design, and technical analysis that can be performed entirely on a computer. AI is highly capable of automating core tasks such as writing technical documentation, generating system diagrams, analyzing cost-benefit data, and assisting with the programming and debugging aspects of the role.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a systems analyst is a digital architecture or configuration plan, which is highly amenable to AI-driven generation and execution within software environments. While the role requires high-level synthesis of business needs and technical constraints, the work occurs in a controlled, digital-first environment, minimizing physical unpredictability and reducing the necessity for human presence to the role of stakeholder management and final accountability.

rows.title: Plumbers, pipefitters, and steamfitters
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, strength, and real-time problem-solving in unpredictable physical environments like crawl spaces and construction sites. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like cost estimation, blueprint analysis via BIM, and scheduling, it cannot perform the essential hands-on installation and repair work.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of infrastructure in highly unstructured, often cramped, and volatile environments, which remains largely beyond the reach of current agentic software. While planning and diagnostics involve complex synthesis, the final execution requires real-world dexterity and high-stakes accountability for safety and building code compliance that cannot be delegated to a digital agent.

rows.title: Training and development specialists
rows.old_rationale: Much of the core work, such as designing training manuals, creating online learning modules, and assessing needs through data, is digital and highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the role requires interpersonal skills for live delivery and collaboration, AI can now generate curriculum, simulate training scenarios, and personalize learning paths, significantly increasing individual productivity and potentially reducing the number of specialists needed for content creation.
rows.new_rationale: The core outputs of this role—training modules, manuals, and digital learning content—are highly amenable to AI generation and automated deployment. While the role involves human facilitation and interpersonal coordination, the primary value is increasingly digital and informational, making it highly susceptible to agentic automation despite the need for human-centric delivery in specific high-touch contexts.

rows.title: Animal care and service workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are physical and require real-time presence, such as feeding, grooming, cleaning, and physically training animals. AI may assist with peripheral administrative tasks like scheduling or monitoring animal health data, but it cannot replace the manual labor or the empathetic human-animal interaction required for the job.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical, real-world interaction with living beings, which cannot be digitized or executed by software agents. The work environment is highly unpredictable due to the volatile nature of animals, and the human-to-human bond (with pet owners) and the ethical weight of animal welfare create a high barrier to full automation.

rows.title: Advertising, promotions, and marketing managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is predominantly digital and information-based, involving market research, data analysis, and content strategy—all areas where AI is rapidly advancing. While high-level strategic decision-making and stakeholder management provide some insulation, AI can now automate significant portions of campaign planning, creative drafting, and consumer data processing, leading to major restructuring of the role.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—marketing strategies, ad copy, media buying, and campaign analytics—is almost entirely digital and increasingly executable by autonomous agents. While the role requires high-level strategic synthesis and human-to-human negotiation, the actual execution of the marketing 'product' is moving rapidly toward software-driven automation, leaving little physical or environmental constraint on the work.

rows.title: Heating, air conditioning, and refrigeration mechanics and installers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time problem solving in unpredictable physical environments, which are highly resistant to AI. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like diagnostic software, scheduling, or energy load calculations, it cannot perform the physical installation, repair, or handling of hazardous materials required for the job.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical intervention in complex, unstructured, and often hazardous environments, which cannot be replaced by digital agents. While the work requires significant diagnostic reasoning, the final output is a physical repair or installation that requires human dexterity, physical presence, and legal accountability for safety and building code compliance.

rows.title: Pharmacists
rows.old_rationale: Pharmacists face moderate exposure because their core knowledge-based tasks—checking drug interactions, verifying dosages, and analyzing patient records—are highly susceptible to AI automation and digital optimization. However, the role remains anchored by physical requirements such as administering immunizations, performing clinical rounds, and providing real-time, empathetic patient counseling that requires human trust and physical presence.
rows.new_rationale: While the dispensing of medication is increasingly automated, the core value of the pharmacist lies in high-stakes clinical judgment, drug-interaction synthesis, and the human-centric delivery of care and patient education. The occupation carries profound legal and ethical liability for patient safety that cannot be delegated to an agent, and the necessity for human empathy and trust in a clinical setting remains a critical, non-automatable component of the role.

rows.title: Network and computer systems administrators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is primarily digital and involves tasks like configuration, monitoring, and troubleshooting that are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and 'infrastructure as code' practices. While some physical hardware maintenance remains, the shift toward cloud computing and AI-powered network management tools is already leading to a projected decline in employment as fewer workers can manage larger, more complex systems.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is entirely digital—managing configurations, permissions, and system architecture—which is highly susceptible to autonomous agent execution via Infrastructure-as-Code and automated orchestration. While the work requires complex synthesis to design and troubleshoot systems, the environment is largely controlled and virtualized, leaving little room for human-centric necessity beyond high-level accountability for system-critical failures.

rows.title: Personal financial advisors
rows.old_rationale: The core technical tasks of this role—analyzing market data, optimizing portfolios, and tax planning—are digital and highly susceptible to AI automation, as evidenced by the rise of robo-advisors. However, the occupation is protected by a significant interpersonal component, as clients often require human trust, emotional reassurance during market volatility, and complex ethical judgment that AI cannot yet fully replicate.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is digital financial strategy and transaction execution, which is highly susceptible to agentic automation. While the technical aspects of portfolio management are increasingly algorithmic, the role maintains high scores in human necessity and accountability due to the psychological need for trust, behavioral coaching, and the legal liability associated with managing life-altering assets.

rows.title: Diesel service technicians and mechanics
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity, strength, and real-time problem-solving in a hands-on environment. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic software and technical manuals used by technicians, it cannot perform the physical repairs, parts replacement, or tool manipulation that define the job.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical manipulation and repair of heavy machinery in often unstructured, variable environments, which cannot be digitized or executed by software. While diagnostic software assists the process, the final output requires physical dexterity, strength, and real-world troubleshooting that remains beyond the reach of autonomous agents, coupled with high systemic accountability for the safety of heavy vehicles on public roads.

rows.title: Loan officers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of loan officers—analyzing financial data, verifying documentation, and assessing risk—are fundamentally digital and highly susceptible to AI automation. While complex commercial loans and relationship-based mortgage sales still require human judgment and interpersonal skills, the rapid advancement of automated underwriting and AI-driven customer service significantly reduces the need for human intervention in routine loan processing.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a loan officer is a digital decision (approval/denial) based on data, which is already heavily mediated by underwriting software and could be fully executed by autonomous agents. While complex commercial loans require human synthesis and relationship management, the primary barrier to full automation is systemic accountability and regulatory compliance rather than physical environmental constraints.

rows.title: Telecommunications technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical labor, including climbing towers, installing hardware, and manual troubleshooting in unpredictable environments, which provides a strong barrier against AI automation. While AI can assist with remote diagnostics, network optimization, and predictive maintenance, the physical necessity of splicing cables and mounting equipment ensures the role remains primarily human-driven.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical installation, repair, and maintenance in highly unstructured, often hazardous environments like towers, attics, and underground conduits, which remains largely beyond the reach of current autonomous agents. While diagnostic software can assist with troubleshooting, the final execution requires complex physical dexterity and navigation of unpredictable real-world variables that cannot be digitized or delegated to software.

rows.title: Heavy vehicle and mobile equipment service technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, involving manual dexterity, strength, and real-world troubleshooting in unpredictable environments like farms and construction sites. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic software and predictive maintenance logs, it cannot perform the physical disassembly, repair, and reassembly of heavy mechanical components.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical intervention in complex, often unstructured environments (e.g., field repairs on construction sites), which cannot be digitized or executed by software. While diagnostic software is used, the final repair requires high-stakes physical manipulation of heavy machinery, making the role highly resistant to agentic automation despite the cognitive complexity involved in troubleshooting.

rows.title: Logisticians
rows.old_rationale: Logisticians perform high-level knowledge work that is primarily digital, involving data analysis, forecasting, and supply chain optimization—tasks where AI excels. While the role requires human-to-human negotiation and real-world problem-solving for physical disruptions, the core functions of inventory management and route optimization are increasingly being automated or enhanced by sophisticated AI algorithms.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a logistician is digital—plans, schedules, and procurement commands—which are increasingly executed directly by supply chain management software, giving this role high agentic potential. While the work involves managing physical goods, the logistician operates primarily through data-driven synthesis and system optimization rather than direct physical manipulation, allowing AI to handle the vast majority of the decision-making loop.

rows.title: Cost estimators
rows.old_rationale: Cost estimation is a data-intensive, digital-first occupation centered on analyzing blueprints, technical documents, and historical databases to predict costs. AI and advanced software are already driving a projected decline in employment by automating routine calculations and simulations, though human oversight remains necessary for complex site-specific variables and high-stakes negotiations.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a cost estimator is a digital document—a bid or budget—which is increasingly generated by software that integrates BIM and CAD data, making it highly susceptible to full automation. While the role requires synthesizing complex variables, the work is primarily office-based and analytical, lacking the physical unpredictability or deep human-to-human necessity that would protect it from agentic replacement.

rows.title: Tutors
rows.old_rationale: Tutors primarily engage in knowledge transfer, explanation, and feedback—tasks where generative AI and personalized learning algorithms excel. While the interpersonal and motivational aspects of tutoring provide some protection, the core work product is digital/informational, and AI's ability to provide 24/7, low-cost, personalized instruction for standardized tests and core subjects creates significant displacement risk.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of tutoring—instruction, feedback, and lesson planning—is increasingly digital and can be delivered via AI agents, leading to high agentic potential. However, the role retains a high ontological necessity score because the primary value often lies in the human-to-human bond, motivation, and emotional support required to keep students engaged, which remains difficult for software to replicate.

rows.title: Web developers and digital designers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is entirely digital, with core tasks like writing code (HTML, JavaScript), designing layouts, and creating prototypes being areas where AI has demonstrated high proficiency. AI tools can now generate functional code from natural language and create sophisticated visual mockups, significantly increasing individual productivity and potentially reducing the number of developers needed for standard projects.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation is entirely digital, consisting of code, design assets, and functional interfaces that can be generated, tested, and deployed by autonomous agents. While the work requires high-level synthesis to meet client needs, the environment is strictly controlled and digital, leaving little room for the necessity of human physical presence or high-stakes, non-delegatable liability compared to physical-world professions.

rows.title: Chefs and head cooks
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job is highly physical and sensory, requiring manual dexterity, real-time human leadership in a fast-paced kitchen, and the use of taste and smell. While AI can significantly assist with peripheral digital tasks like menu planning, inventory management, and recipe generation, it cannot replace the physical oversight and hands-on food preparation essential to the role.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like scheduling and inventory are digitizable, the core value of the occupation is the physical transformation of ingredients into a sensory experience, which cannot be executed by software. The environment is highly dynamic and physically demanding, requiring real-time human judgment and sensory feedback that remains difficult to replicate with autonomous agents.

rows.title: Computer network architects
rows.old_rationale: The core work of designing, documenting, and configuring networks is fundamentally digital and data-driven, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and optimization tools. While physical hardware deployment and complex stakeholder management provide a slight buffer, AI is rapidly advancing in automated network topology design, security configuration, and predictive troubleshooting, which will significantly increase individual productivity and restructure the role.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a network architect is digital configuration and architectural design, which is highly amenable to execution by autonomous agents via Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and software-defined networking. While the role requires high-level synthesis to design complex systems, the physical environment is largely controlled, and the human necessity is primarily functional rather than relational, leaving systemic accountability as the main barrier to full automation.

rows.title: Aircraft and avionics equipment mechanics and technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and real-time troubleshooting in a physical environment, which provides a strong barrier against AI automation. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic capabilities, predictive maintenance, and the interpretation of flight data, the actual repair, assembly, and FAA-regulated physical inspections must still be performed by human technicians.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical maintenance and repair of complex, safety-critical hardware in unstructured, often cramped, or volatile environments, which remains largely beyond the reach of current agentic software. While diagnostic software can assist, the final execution requires high-stakes physical manipulation and legal sign-off, making the role highly resistant to automation despite the cognitive complexity of the diagnostic tasks.

rows.title: Gambling services workers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of physical hospitality and digital-adjacent information processing. While AI and automation are significantly impacting sports book writers and slot operations through online platforms and automated betting, the core of the job for dealers and managers involves physical presence, manual dexterity, and real-time human interaction to ensure a 'pleasant experience' and monitor for physical rule violations.
rows.new_rationale: While the core mechanics of gambling are increasingly digitized, the physical presence of a dealer or supervisor is essential for maintaining the social atmosphere, enforcing house rules in real-time, and managing human conflict. The agentic potential is limited because the value proposition relies on the physical, in-person experience and the human-to-human trust required to mitigate disputes in a high-stakes, volatile environment.

rows.title: Librarians and library media specialists
rows.old_rationale: Librarianship is fundamentally about information retrieval, organization, and research—domains where LLMs and AI search engines excel. While the physical presence in schools and public libraries provides a buffer through community programming and interpersonal support, the core technical tasks of cataloging, database management, and reference assistance are highly exposed to AI automation and displacement.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a librarian—information retrieval, curation, and research synthesis—is increasingly digital and can be executed by AI agents, leading to high agentic potential. While the role involves human-centric tasks like community programming and pedagogical support, the primary functional value of information management is highly susceptible to automation in structured, low-volatility library environments.

rows.title: Electrical power-line installers and repairers
rows.old_rationale: The core work is highly physical, involving climbing towers, operating heavy machinery, and manual repair in unpredictable outdoor environments. While AI can assist with remote monitoring and diagnostic data analysis, it cannot perform the high-risk physical labor required to install or repair high-voltage infrastructure.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this job is physical manipulation of high-voltage infrastructure in volatile, unstructured outdoor environments, which is fundamentally incompatible with current or near-future agentic software execution. While diagnostic tasks involve cognitive complexity, the primary requirement is high-stakes, non-delegatable physical intervention where human judgment and liability are essential for safety and grid stability.

rows.title: Sheet metal workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor, manual dexterity, and on-site installation in unpredictable construction environments, which are highly resistant to AI. While AI and computer-controlled machinery (CADD, lasers, and automated presses) are increasingly used in the fabrication phase, the majority of the work—including installation, maintenance, and complex physical maneuvering—cannot be performed by digital tools.
rows.new_rationale: While fabrication can be increasingly automated, the core value of the occupation lies in the physical installation, maneuvering, and custom fitting of materials within highly unstructured, variable construction environments. The necessity for human intervention is driven by the physical unpredictability of job sites and the requirement for manual dexterity in tight, non-standardized spaces, rather than complex cognitive synthesis or human-to-human interaction.

rows.title: Computer programmers
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of writing, testing, and debugging code are entirely digital and align perfectly with the strengths of Large Language Models, which are already highly proficient in these domains. The BLS specifically projects a decline in this occupation due to automation and AI, as these tools allow fewer workers to produce the same volume of code and shift higher-level logic to software developers.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a computer programmer is digital code, which is inherently executable by AI agents, making this occupation highly susceptible to full automation. While the work requires significant cognitive synthesis, it occurs in a highly controlled, virtual environment with minimal physical variables, and the human role is increasingly limited to oversight rather than essential human-to-human interaction.

rows.title: Electrical and electronics installers and repairers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical manipulation of hardware, manual troubleshooting in unpredictable environments, and the use of hand tools, which are highly resistant to AI automation. While AI can significantly enhance diagnostic software and predictive maintenance schedules, the physical acts of disassembling, repairing, and reassembling complex electrical components require human dexterity and presence.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical intervention—disassembling, repairing, and reassembling hardware—which cannot be executed by software alone, keeping agentic potential very low. The work frequently occurs in unstructured, high-stakes environments (powerhouses, transportation systems, or field sites) where physical dexterity and the ability to navigate unpredictable mechanical failures are essential, necessitating high systemic accountability for safety-critical infrastructure.

rows.title: Veterinary assistants and laboratory animal caretakers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties of this occupation are almost entirely physical and manual, involving the handling, feeding, and restraining of live animals in unpredictable environments. While AI might assist with peripheral administrative tasks or diagnostic data entry, it cannot replace the physical presence and manual dexterity required for animal care, sterilization, and surgical assistance.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical, in-person interaction with living creatures, which cannot be digitized or executed by software agents. The high environmental unpredictability stems from the volatile nature of handling scared or aggressive animals, requiring real-time physical judgment and human presence that remains essential for safety and animal welfare.

rows.title: Oil and gas workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves heavy physical labor, equipment maintenance, and manual operation in unpredictable outdoor environments, which provides a strong buffer against AI. However, the industry is increasingly adopting automated drilling technologies and remote monitoring systems that improve productivity, meaning fewer human workers may be required to achieve the same output.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor, equipment maintenance, and extraction in highly volatile, unstructured, and dangerous environments, which cannot be replaced by digital agents. While cognitive tasks like monitoring and troubleshooting exist, the primary output is the physical manipulation of heavy machinery and raw materials, necessitating human presence for safety and real-time physical intervention.

rows.title: Compensation, benefits, and job analysis specialists
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital and data-driven, involving core tasks like market research, cost analysis, and report generation that align perfectly with AI's strengths. AI can automate the drafting of job descriptions, perform complex compensation benchmarking, and monitor regulatory changes, significantly increasing individual productivity and reducing the need for manual data processing.
rows.new_rationale: The core outputs of this role—salary structures, benefits plans, and job classifications—are entirely digital artifacts that can be generated, modeled, and implemented via software agents. While the work requires high-level analytical synthesis of labor trends and legal compliance, it occurs in a highly controlled office environment with minimal physical variables, making it a prime candidate for end-to-end automation.

rows.title: Pest control workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is highly physical, requiring workers to crawl into tight spaces, set manual traps, and apply chemicals in unpredictable real-world environments. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like route optimization, pest identification from photos, or automated billing, the primary labor cannot be performed digitally or remotely.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this job is physical intervention in highly variable, unstructured environments (crawlspaces, attics, and diverse building structures) that require human dexterity and real-time physical judgment. While the planning aspect is cognitive, the execution is inherently physical and high-stakes due to the use of toxic chemicals, necessitating human accountability for safety and regulatory compliance.

rows.title: Skincare specialists
rows.old_rationale: The core of the job involves physical, hands-on treatments like facials, massages, and hair removal that require real-time human presence and manual dexterity. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like skin analysis, product recommendations, and business management, it cannot replace the tactile and interpersonal nature of the primary service.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical, in-person service involving tactile manipulation of human skin, which cannot be digitized or executed by software. While the diagnostic and advisory aspects could be augmented by AI, the necessity for human touch, physical presence, and the liability associated with performing chemical or mechanical procedures on a client's body makes the role highly resistant to automation.

rows.title: Electrical and electronic engineering technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical labor, such as assembling prototypes, soldering circuitry, and repairing hardware, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, a substantial portion of the role involves digital tasks like drafting schematics, analyzing test data, and writing technical reports—all areas where AI is becoming highly proficient. AI will likely augment their troubleshooting capabilities and automate the documentation aspects of the job, increasing individual productivity but potentially reducing overall demand.
rows.new_rationale: While these technicians perform significant digital documentation and design support, the core of their value lies in physical assembly, calibration, and troubleshooting of hardware in varied environments, which remains difficult for autonomous agents to execute end-to-end. The high accountability score reflects the critical nature of electrical systems, where errors in physical implementation or safety protocols carry significant liability and risk of system failure.

rows.title: Water transportation workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of the work is physical and requires real-time presence in unpredictable maritime environments, involving manual tasks like equipment maintenance, cargo handling, and emergency response. While AI can assist with navigation, fuel optimization, and predictive maintenance for engineers, the high-stakes physical safety requirements and the need for human intervention in complex docking or mechanical failures limit overall exposure.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation is the physical movement of vessels and cargo through highly volatile, unstructured maritime environments, which cannot be reduced to a digital artifact. While navigation and engine monitoring involve complex synthesis, the physical maintenance and emergency response requirements, combined with the high-stakes legal and safety accountability of a ship's captain, necessitate a human presence that software cannot currently replace or legally assume.

rows.title: Biological technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role is a hybrid of physical laboratory work and digital data analysis. While AI can significantly automate data interpretation, report writing, and experimental modeling, the core duties involve manual tasks like setting up equipment, preparing biological samples, and conducting physical experiments that require a human presence in a lab or field environment.
rows.new_rationale: While data analysis and reporting are digital, the core value of this role is physical manipulation of biological samples and laboratory equipment, which remains difficult to fully automate in diverse, non-standardized research settings. The environmental score reflects the mix of highly controlled labs and unpredictable field work, while the accountability score reflects the high stakes of scientific integrity and safety protocols where human oversight is required to prevent catastrophic experimental or environmental contamination.

rows.title: Film and video editors and camera operators
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical camera operation and digital video editing. While camera operators have a physical barrier to AI due to the need for on-site equipment management and real-world positioning, editors work entirely in digital environments where AI is rapidly automating tasks like color grading, sound syncing, and even rough-cut generation. The rise of generative video and AI-driven post-production tools significantly increases the productivity of each worker, potentially reducing the total number of editors needed for complex projects.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this profession is digital media, which is increasingly susceptible to end-to-end generation and manipulation by AI agents, particularly in the editing phase. While camera operation involves physical interaction with unstructured environments, the increasing use of robotic cameras and drones demonstrates that the physical constraints are rapidly becoming manageable through software-driven automation.

rows.title: Health education specialists
rows.old_rationale: A significant portion of the role involves digital knowledge work such as analyzing community health data, writing grant proposals, and creating educational materials, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. However, the core requirements of community advocacy, empathetic interpersonal interaction, and physical presence for local events and training provide a buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: A significant portion of the output—developing educational materials, data analysis, and program strategy—is digital and highly amenable to AI agent execution. However, the core value of the role relies heavily on human-to-human trust, empathy, and the ability to navigate complex, sensitive community dynamics that require a human presence to be effective and credible.

rows.title: Medical equipment repairers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation is physical and manual, requiring dexterity to handle tools, disassemble machinery, and work in tight physical spaces. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic software, troubleshooting, and predictive maintenance scheduling, the physical act of repairing, installing, and calibrating hardware remains a human-centric task that cannot be automated by digital AI alone.
rows.new_rationale: While software diagnostics and calibration are increasingly automated, the core value of this role lies in physical, on-site manipulation of complex, life-critical hardware in unpredictable clinical environments. The high systemic accountability score reflects the life-or-death consequences of equipment failure, which necessitates human liability and physical intervention that cannot be delegated to autonomous agents.

rows.title: Dental and ophthalmic laboratory technicians and medical appliance technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical craftsmanship and digital design, with AI and automation already driving a projected decline in dental technician roles. While the physical assembly, polishing, and repair of medical devices require manual dexterity, the 'knowledge' phase of the job—interpreting prescriptions and designing appliances—is rapidly shifting toward AI-integrated CAD/CAM software and 3D printing.
rows.new_rationale: While the design phase of these appliances is increasingly digital, the core value of the job remains the physical fabrication, fitting, and material manipulation of medical devices, which requires physical presence and manual dexterity. The work occurs in highly controlled laboratory or manufacturing environments, and while the output is critical for patient health, the primary task is technical execution rather than high-level strategic synthesis or direct human-to-human emotional labor.

rows.title: Funeral service workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical tasks like preparing remains and transporting the deceased, alongside high-stakes interpersonal work providing grief counseling and comfort. While AI can streamline administrative duties like filing death certificates, scheduling, and writing obituaries, it cannot replace the physical presence and emotional intelligence required for funeral services.
rows.new_rationale: While administrative tasks like filing paperwork have some digital potential, the core value of the occupation is rooted in physical care of the deceased and the provision of emotional support to grieving families, which cannot be digitized or automated. The work requires high-stakes, real-time human interaction and ethical responsibility that is fundamentally tied to the human experience of loss, making it highly resistant to agentic replacement.

rows.title: Chiropractors
rows.old_rationale: The core of chiropractic work is highly physical, requiring manual dexterity for spinal adjustments and real-time physical assessment of a patient's musculoskeletal system. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like medical imaging analysis, diagnostic suggestions, and administrative business management, it cannot replace the essential hands-on therapeutic interventions that define the profession.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of chiropractic care is physical, manual manipulation of the human body, which cannot be digitized or executed by software agents. While the diagnostic phase involves complex synthesis of patient history and imaging, the delivery of care requires high-stakes, real-time physical interaction and professional liability that necessitates a human practitioner.

rows.title: Actors
rows.old_rationale: While live theater and physical presence on set remain human-centric, the occupation is highly exposed due to AI's ability to generate digital likenesses, synthesize voices, and replace background extras or voiceover artists. The core work product—audio and video—is increasingly susceptible to generative AI, leading to significant industry restructuring and potential displacement in digital media productions.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of acting—performance, voice, and likeness—is increasingly digital and replicable by AI, leading to high agentic potential in film and media. However, the profession remains deeply rooted in the 'ontological human necessity' of authentic human connection and the shared physical experience of live performance, which AI cannot replicate in a way that satisfies the audience's desire for human-to-human resonance.

rows.title: Chemical technicians
rows.old_rationale: The role is a hybrid of physical laboratory work and digital data analysis. While AI can significantly automate the compilation of results, report generation, and predictive modeling of chemical properties, the core duties of setting up equipment, preparing physical solutions, and maintaining laboratory instruments require a human presence in a physical environment.
rows.new_rationale: While data reporting and analysis are digital, the core value of this role is physical manipulation of chemicals and equipment in lab or manufacturing environments, which limits agentic output potential. The work requires high precision and adherence to safety protocols, creating significant systemic accountability for errors in chemical processing or experimental integrity, even if the physical environment is relatively controlled.

rows.title: Hazardous materials removal workers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical labor in unpredictable environments, such as scrubbing surfaces, constructing containment areas, and operating heavy machinery. While AI may assist with peripheral tasks like record-keeping, regulatory compliance checks, or analyzing sensor data for radiation levels, the primary work requires a physical human presence to navigate complex, hazardous sites.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this job is physical remediation in highly volatile, unstructured, and dangerous environments, which is fundamentally incompatible with current or near-future autonomous agent capabilities. While the work requires adherence to strict protocols, the primary barrier to automation is the physical manipulation of hazardous materials in unpredictable, real-world settings where the 'buck stops' with the human worker for safety and legal compliance.

rows.title: Budget analysts
rows.old_rationale: Budget analysts perform work that is almost entirely digital, involving data analysis, financial modeling, and report writing—all areas where AI is highly capable. While the role requires human negotiation and communication with stakeholders, the core tasks of consolidating budgets, identifying discrepancies, and performing cost-benefit analyses are highly susceptible to automation and significant productivity gains through AI.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a budget analyst is a digital report, financial model, or funding recommendation, all of which are highly amenable to AI execution and integration into existing financial software. While the role requires high-level synthesis of organizational data and carries significant accountability for fiscal compliance and public policy, the work environment is almost entirely controlled and digital, minimizing the need for physical human intervention.

rows.title: Optometrists
rows.old_rationale: Optometry is a hybrid of physical clinical procedures and high-level data analysis. AI is highly effective at analyzing retinal scans and diagnostic data to identify diseases, which will significantly augment the diagnostic process; however, the core role requires physical presence for eye exams, minor surgical procedures, and the fitting of corrective lenses, which provides a strong buffer against full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While diagnostic data and prescription generation are increasingly digital, the core value of optometry remains the physical examination, minor surgical intervention, and the human-centric delivery of care. The high systemic accountability and the necessity for human trust in clinical decision-making and patient counseling act as significant barriers to full agentic automation.

rows.title: Music directors and composers
rows.old_rationale: The occupation is a hybrid of high-exposure digital work (composing) and lower-exposure physical work (conducting). Generative AI is rapidly automating music composition, arrangement, and scoring for commercial media, while the music director role remains more resilient due to the need for live leadership, physical rehearsals, and human-to-human artistic interpretation.
rows.new_rationale: While the composition of music is increasingly digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven generation, the role of a music director remains deeply rooted in the human-to-human interaction required to lead, inspire, and synchronize live performers. The high cognitive complexity reflects the nuanced, non-routine nature of artistic interpretation, which, while potentially replicable in output, requires a human presence to maintain the social and emotional contract of a live performance.

rows.title: Training and development managers
rows.old_rationale: This role is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, involving the creation of instructional content, data-driven needs assessments, and budget management—all areas where AI is highly capable. While the role requires significant human-centric leadership and stakeholder collaboration, AI can automate the generation of training materials, personalize learning paths, and analyze program effectiveness, substantially increasing the productivity of each manager.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—curriculum design, training materials, and performance assessment—is largely digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven generation and execution. While the role requires human-centric skills like leadership and organizational alignment, the actual delivery and management of training programs are increasingly shifting toward automated, software-based systems that require minimal physical presence.

rows.title: Health information technologists and medical registrars
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, involving the analysis, organization, and validation of clinical data within computerized systems. AI is exceptionally well-suited for core tasks like data abstraction, classification coding, and generating reports from large datasets, which significantly increases the risk of automation and restructuring.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is digital data, database entries, and system configurations, making it highly susceptible to end-to-end execution by AI agents. While the work requires analytical rigor and adherence to privacy regulations, it occurs in a highly controlled, predictable office environment with minimal physical interaction, leaving systemic accountability as the primary barrier to full automation.

rows.title: Environmental science and protection technicians
rows.old_rationale: The core of this role involves physical fieldwork, such as collecting soil and water samples and inspecting facilities, which AI cannot perform. However, AI will significantly impact the digital aspects of the job, including data analysis, the preparation of reports and charts, and the modeling of environmental impacts.
rows.new_rationale: While data analysis and reporting are digital, the core value of this role is the physical collection of samples and on-site inspection of unstructured, hazardous environments, which requires human physical presence and dexterity. The high systemic accountability score reflects the legal and public health consequences of their findings, which require human verification and professional liability that cannot be fully offloaded to an autonomous agent.

rows.title: Agricultural and food science technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical labor, such as collecting biological samples, maintaining farm equipment, and operating laboratory hardware, which provides a buffer against full automation. However, AI will heavily impact the data-heavy portions of the job, including compiling test results, analyzing chemical properties, and generating reports, making technicians more productive but potentially reducing the number of workers needed for data processing.
rows.new_rationale: While data analysis and reporting are digital, the core value of this role is tied to physical sample collection, laboratory manipulation, and on-site inspection of crops or livestock, which limits agentic output potential. The work environment is frequently unstructured and volatile, involving biological variables in farms or processing plants that require physical presence and real-time, non-routine physical intervention that software cannot execute.

rows.title: Mechanical engineering technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of digital knowledge work and physical labor. While AI can significantly automate digital tasks like 3D modeling, data analysis, and cost estimation, the role still requires a substantial physical presence for assembling parts, setting up vehicle crash tests, and operating machinery like lathes and grinders.
rows.new_rationale: While the design and documentation aspects of this role are increasingly digital and susceptible to AI, the core value lies in the physical assembly, installation, and troubleshooting of mechanical systems in varied, often unstructured industrial environments. Because the work requires physical interaction with hardware and real-world testing where variables are not perfectly predictable, it remains resistant to full agentic automation despite the high cognitive requirements for technical problem-solving.

rows.title: Actuaries
rows.old_rationale: Actuarial work is fundamentally digital, involving the analysis of large datasets, statistical modeling, and financial forecasting—all areas where AI and machine learning excel. While the profession requires high-level human judgment and the ability to explain complex risks to stakeholders, the core technical tasks of data compilation and probability estimation are highly susceptible to automation and significant productivity gains through AI.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of an actuary is a digital artifact—a risk model, premium calculation, or financial report—which is highly susceptible to automation by advanced AI agents. While the work requires high-level cognitive synthesis to navigate complex regulatory and business environments, the physical environment is entirely controlled, and the primary barrier to full automation is not the task itself, but the high-stakes systemic accountability and legal liability that currently require a human 'enrolled actuary' to sign off on critical financial projections.

rows.title: Stationary engineers and boiler operators
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves physical maintenance, manual repairs, and hands-on troubleshooting of heavy mechanical equipment in unpredictable physical environments. While AI and automated control systems can enhance monitoring and data logging, they cannot perform the physical tasks of overhauling valves, lubricating parts, or crawling inside boilers for inspections.
rows.new_rationale: While monitoring and control systems are increasingly digitized, the core value of this role lies in physical maintenance, repair, and the navigation of complex, often hazardous, industrial environments that require human dexterity and on-site physical intervention. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical nature of boiler and pressure vessel safety, where human oversight is legally and ethically mandated to prevent catastrophic physical failure.

rows.title: Credit counselors
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of credit counseling—analyzing financial data, creating budgets, and explaining debt management options—are fundamentally digital and information-based, making them highly susceptible to AI automation. While the role requires interpersonal skills and emotional support for clients in financial distress, AI can already perform the complex calculations and personalized recommendation generation that constitute the bulk of the technical work.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a credit counselor—financial plans, debt management strategies, and communication with creditors—is entirely digital and can be generated and executed by software agents. While the work requires human empathy to navigate the emotional stress of debt and build client trust, the technical and analytical components are highly susceptible to automation, leaving the human element primarily as a supportive, rather than functional, necessity.

rows.title: Elevator and escalator installers and repairers
rows.old_rationale: The core of this occupation involves highly physical, manual labor in unpredictable environments, such as elevator shafts and machine rooms, which provides a strong barrier against AI automation. While AI can significantly enhance diagnostic troubleshooting and predictive maintenance through computerized control systems, the actual installation, repair, and physical manipulation of heavy mechanical components still require a human presence. AI acts as a sophisticated tool for the technician rather than a replacement for the physical work.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of heavy, complex machinery in hazardous, unstructured environments like elevator shafts and crawlspaces, which cannot be executed by software. While the work involves complex troubleshooting of computerized systems, the physical nature of the repairs and the extreme safety-critical liability associated with vertical transportation ensure that human intervention remains non-delegatable.

rows.title: Exercise physiologists
rows.old_rationale: The role involves a significant amount of physical presence, including conducting stress tests, measuring vital signs with medical equipment, and providing hands-on rehabilitative care. While AI can highly automate the digital aspects of the job—such as analyzing medical history, processing biometric data, and generating personalized exercise regimens—the core requirement for real-time human interaction, physical assessment, and clinical supervision in a medical setting limits its total exposure.
rows.new_rationale: While AI can generate exercise plans and analyze biometric data, the core value of the role is the physical, real-time monitoring and manual guidance of patients with chronic conditions, which requires human presence for safety and motivation. The high ontological necessity and systemic accountability stem from the clinical risk involved in managing patients with pulmonary or cardiovascular issues, where human judgment and physical intervention are critical to preventing adverse medical events.

rows.title: Fishing and hunting workers
rows.old_rationale: The core duties are highly physical and take place in unpredictable outdoor environments, requiring manual labor, mechanical repair, and real-time physical coordination. AI's impact is limited to peripheral enhancements in fish-finding sonar, GPS navigation, and regulatory data processing, but it cannot replace the physical act of hauling nets, trapping animals, or maintaining vessels.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this occupation is the physical extraction of biological resources from highly volatile, unstructured, and hazardous environments, which cannot be digitized or executed via software. While navigation and fish-finding are increasingly assisted by technology, the physical labor of handling gear, managing vessels in storms, and navigating unpredictable terrain remains fundamentally tied to physical presence and real-world interaction.

rows.title: Compensation and benefits managers
rows.old_rationale: This occupation is primarily digital and data-driven, involving the analysis of wage trends, benefits structures, and regulatory compliance—tasks where AI excels. While the role requires high-level human judgment for vendor negotiations and strategic decision-making, AI can automate significant portions of the data analysis, reporting, and routine employee communication, leading to higher productivity and potential workforce consolidation.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role—compensation structures, policy design, and benefit administration—is almost entirely digital and data-driven, making it highly susceptible to agentic execution. While the role requires high-level synthesis of market trends and organizational strategy, the environment is a stable, controlled office setting, and the human necessity is largely limited to internal stakeholder management and high-level accountability for legal and financial compliance.

rows.title: Radiation therapists
rows.old_rationale: The role requires a significant physical presence to position patients, operate heavy machinery, and provide empathetic interpersonal care during serious illness. While AI will heavily automate the digital aspects of the job—such as tumor localization, treatment planning, and dosage calculations—the physical and clinical safety requirements create a substantial barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the operation of the linear accelerator is technical, the core value is the physical positioning of a patient and the real-time monitoring of their physiological and emotional state, which cannot be delegated to software. The high systemic accountability score reflects the life-critical nature of radiation delivery, where human oversight is legally and ethically required to prevent catastrophic medical errors.

rows.title: Audiologists
rows.old_rationale: Audiology is a clinical healthcare profession that requires physical presence for examinations, wax removal, and fitting hardware like hearing aids or cochlear implants. While AI will significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy, signal processing in hearing devices, and automated testing, the core of the job involves interpersonal counseling, physical patient interaction, and complex clinical judgment that cannot be fully digitized.
rows.new_rationale: While diagnostic testing and data analysis can be increasingly automated, the core of the role involves physical interventions like fitting devices, performing physical balance maneuvers, and managing complex patient-provider relationships. The necessity for human empathy, physical presence for clinical procedures, and the high-stakes legal liability for medical diagnosis and treatment outcomes make this role highly resistant to full agentic replacement.

rows.title: Calibration technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant physical component, requiring manual dexterity to adjust hardware and presence in industrial or laboratory environments. While AI can automate data recording, scheduling, and the analysis of calibration deviations, the core tasks of physical inspection, maintenance, and hands-on equipment manipulation provide a natural barrier to full automation.
rows.new_rationale: While the diagnostic reporting is digital, the core value of this role is the physical manipulation, adjustment, and verification of hardware in diverse, often hazardous industrial environments that require human dexterity and physical presence. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical nature of these calibrations, where errors in measurement standards can lead to catastrophic failures in manufacturing or healthcare, necessitating human oversight that cannot be fully delegated to software.

rows.title: Electro-mechanical and mechatronics technologists and technicians
rows.old_rationale: This occupation involves a significant amount of physical labor, including soldering, operating metalworking machinery, and repairing hydraulic systems, which provides a strong barrier against full AI automation. However, AI will increasingly assist with the knowledge-based aspects of the job, such as analyzing test results, interpreting complex schematics, and diagnosing faults in the very robotic systems these technicians maintain.
rows.new_rationale: The core value of this role is physical intervention—repairing, calibrating, and maintaining hardware in often unpredictable or hazardous environments, which cannot be replaced by digital agents. While they utilize complex technical knowledge, the physical nature of the work and the requirement for manual dexterity and onsite troubleshooting keep agentic output potential low.

rows.title: Cartographers and photogrammetrists
rows.old_rationale: The core tasks of collecting, analyzing, and visualizing geospatial data are fundamentally digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven automation. Computer vision and machine learning are already significantly faster than humans at interpreting satellite imagery, LiDAR, and survey data, while generative AI can increasingly automate the design and production of both static and interactive maps.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this profession is digital geospatial data and visualizations, which are increasingly generated and processed through automated software pipelines, giving it high agentic potential. While fieldwork introduces minor environmental variables, the primary value lies in synthesizing complex data sets into actionable digital products, a task where human necessity is limited to high-level oversight and legal certification of accuracy.

rows.title: Boilermakers
rows.old_rationale: The core work is highly physical, involving manual assembly, welding, and repair in unpredictable and cramped environments like boilers and tanks. While AI can assist with peripheral tasks like blueprint analysis or maintenance scheduling, the primary duties require human dexterity and physical presence that current AI and robotics cannot replicate in non-factory settings.
rows.new_rationale: The work of a boilermaker is fundamentally physical, involving the manipulation of heavy, industrial-scale hardware in highly unstructured, hazardous, and confined environments that are currently beyond the reach of autonomous agents. While the job requires technical knowledge, the primary value is the physical installation and repair of critical infrastructure, which carries immense systemic accountability for safety and structural integrity that cannot be delegated to software.

rows.title: Models
rows.old_rationale: While runway and live event modeling require physical presence, the majority of modeling work results in digital or print imagery which is highly vulnerable to AI-generated synthetic media. AI can now create hyper-realistic virtual models or 'digital twins' of existing models, allowing brands to showcase clothing and products without physical photo shoots, significantly reducing the demand for human labor in commercial and catalog sectors.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of modeling—a visual representation of a product or aesthetic—is increasingly digital and can be synthesized by generative AI, leading to high agentic potential. While live runway or in-person retail work requires physical presence, the vast majority of the industry's value is derived from the final image or video, which is highly susceptible to automation.

rows.title: Political scientists
rows.old_rationale: Political science is a purely digital knowledge-work occupation centered on data analysis, research, and writing, all of which are core strengths of generative AI and LLMs. While high-level strategic judgment and interpersonal networking remain human-centric, AI can now automate the collection of public opinion data, the drafting of policy briefs, and the forecasting of political trends, leading to significant productivity gains and a projected decline in total employment.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a political scientist—reports, policy forecasts, and data-driven strategic insights—is entirely digital and highly susceptible to automation by advanced AI agents capable of synthesizing vast datasets. While the work requires high-level cognitive synthesis to navigate complex political systems, the environment is largely controlled and information-based, minimizing the need for physical presence or human-to-human emotional labor as a primary product.

rows.title: Desktop publishers
rows.old_rationale: The work product is entirely digital and involves routine information processing, layout design, and basic editing—all areas where AI is highly proficient. Generative AI and automated layout tools can now handle text integration, image adjustment, and formatting with minimal human intervention, leading to a projected decline in the occupation as these tasks are automated or absorbed by other roles using AI tools.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of this role is entirely digital and consists of file formatting and layout execution, which is already being subsumed by automated software and AI-driven design tools. Because the work occurs entirely within a controlled digital environment and lacks high-stakes physical or legal liability, it is highly susceptible to full automation.

rows.title: Sociologists
rows.old_rationale: Sociology is a knowledge-intensive field where core tasks like data analysis, literature reviews, and report writing are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. While qualitative field research and interpersonal interviews provide a buffer, the shift toward digital data collection and AI-driven statistical modeling significantly increases worker productivity and reshapes the research process.
rows.new_rationale: The core output of a sociologist—research reports, policy recommendations, and statistical insights—is almost entirely digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven synthesis and generation. While the work involves human interaction for qualitative data collection, the primary value lies in the intellectual synthesis of complex social data, which is increasingly within the domain of autonomous agents, though the final ethical and policy-level accountability remains a human responsibility.
